title: Although Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated extraordinary capabilities in many domains, they still have...
abstract: The abstract discusses the issue of hallucination in Large Language Models (LLMs) and proposes a solution by integrating LLMs with information retrieval (IR) systems, referred to as retrieval-augmented LLMs. This approach allows LLMs to generate more factual responses based on content from external corpora. By incorporating external knowledge, these models can answer in-domain questions that cannot be addressed solely with world knowledge stored in parameters. A toolkit called RETA-LLM is developed to facilitate research and the creation of customized in-domain LLM-based systems. It offers a complete pipeline for building such systems, with various plug-and-play modules like request rewriting, document retrieval, passage extraction, answer generation, and fact checking.
categories: Integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) with information retrieval (IR) systems to generate more factual responses., Developing a toolkit named RETA-LLM for building customized in-domain LLM-based systems, including request rewriting, document retrieval, passage extraction, answer generation, and fact checking modules.

title: This work presents a Fully BInarized Large Language Model (FBI-LLM), demonstrating for the first time...
abstract: The abstract introduces a Fully BInarized Large Language Model (FBI-LLM), which for the first time trains a large-scale binary language model from scratch to match the performance of its full-precision counterparts in transformer-based LLMs. The FBI-LLM achieves this by employing an autoregressive distillation (AD) loss, maintaining equivalent model dimensions and training data volume as regular LLM pretraining. The research finds that the pretrained weight is not necessary for training binarized LLMs from scratch.
categories: Training a large-scale binary language model from scratch, Employing an autoregressive distillation (AD) loss, Maintaining equivalent model dimensions and training data volume as regular LLM pretraining

In LLM inference, the same prompt may yield different outputs across different runs. At the...
abstract: The abstract introduces LLM-42, a scheduling-based approach to enable determinism in Language Model (LLM) inference. The non-determinism issue arises from floating-point non-associativity, dynamic batching, and GPU kernels with varying reduction orders. Disabling dynamic batching degrades throughput, while making kernels batch-invariant tightly couples determinism to kernel design. LLM-42 leverages the observation that consistent states produce consistent next tokens, even with dynamic batching, and most GPU kernels use shape-consistent reductions. It decodes tokens using a non-deterministic fast path and enforces determinism via a lightweight verify-rollback loop. The verifier replays candidate tokens under a fixed-shape reduction schedule, commits those that are consistent across runs, and rolls back those violating determinism.
categories: Scheduling-based approach (LLM-42), Non-deterministic fast path, Lightweight verify-rollback loop

title: Multiple-choice questions (MCQ) are frequently used to assess large language models (LLMs). Typically, an LLM...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of multiple-choice questions (MCQ) for assessing large language models (LLMs). It mentions that LLMs may have inherent biases towards certain answer choice IDs, leading to selection bias. The issue of random guessing is also highlighted, where an LLM may not learn particular knowledge but still choose the correct option by chance. To address these problems, a shift towards open-style questions is proposed as it could eliminate selection bias and random guessing issues. However, this transition poses challenges such as identifying suitable open-style questions and validating LLM responses against human-annotated ground-truths. The work aims to overcome these difficulties by establishing a new LLM evaluation benchmark using entirely open-style questions. The Open-LLM-Leaderboard is introduced to track the performance of various LLMs, including GPT-4o/4/3.5, Claude 3, and Gemini. The code and dataset for this project can be found at https://github.com/VILA-Lab/Open-LLM-Leaderboard.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) agents significantly extend the capabilities of standalone LLMs, empowering them to...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel approach to extend the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by introducing a modular framework that decomposes tasks into a planner, caller, and summarizer. Each component is implemented by a single LLM, focusing on a specific capability and collaborating with others to accomplish tasks. The approach overcomes performance limitations of traditional methods, particularly with smaller models. A two-stage training paradigm is proposed for effective training of this framework. The proposed multi-LLM framework surpasses the traditional single-LLM approach in tool learning, as demonstrated through various evaluations across tool-use benchmarks.
categories: Decomposing the capabilities into a planner, caller, and summarizer, each implemented by a single LLM, Two-stage training paradigm: fine-tuning a backbone LLM on the entire dataset without discriminating sub-tasks, followed by fine-tuning the LLMs for respective sub-tasks, Evaluation across various tool-use benchmarks

title: The integration of large language model (LLM) and data management (DATA) is rapidly redefining both...
abstract: This survey analyzes the integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Data Management (DATA), discussing bidirectional relationships in both domains. The paper covers topics like data processing, storage, serving for LLMs, and data manipulation, analysis, system optimization using LLMs. The study highlights how large-scale data processing, storage, and serving feeds high-quality, diverse, and timely data to LLMs for various stages such as pre-training, post-training, retrieval-augmented generation, and agentic workflows.
categories: Data processing methods include scalable acquisition, deduplication, filtering, selection, domain mixing, and synthetic augmentation., Data Storage focuses on efficient data and model formats, distributed and heterogeneous storage hierarchies, KV-cache management, and fault-tolerant checkpointing., Data serving addresses challenges in RAG (e.g., knowledge post-processing), LLM inference (e.g., prompt compression, data provenance), and training strategies (e.g., data packing and shuffling)., In LLM4DATA, data manipulation includes automatic data cleaning, integration, discovery., Data analysis covers reasoning over structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data., System optimization techniques include configuration tuning, query rewriting, anomaly diagnosis, powered by LLM techniques like retrieval-augmented prompting, task-specialized fine-tuning, and multi-agent collaboration.

title: Traditional reinforcement learning and planning typically requires vast amounts of data and training to develop...
abstract: The abstract introduces STRATEGIST, a novel approach that combines the strengths of traditional reinforcement learning and planning with large language models (LLMs) to optimize high-level strategies for competitive, multi-turn games with partial information. STRATEGIST utilizes LLMs to search and update strategies as text, which are then refined and executed by low-level Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS). The framework is generalizable and optimized through population-based self-play simulations without the need for any training data. The effectiveness of STRATEGIST is demonstrated in learning optimal strategies for games like Game of Pure Strategy (GOPS) and multi-agent, hidden-identity discussion games like The Resistance: Avalon. Results show that agents equipped with STRATEGIST outperform those trained with traditional RL methods, other LLM-based skill acquisition techniques, pre-existing LLM agents across both game environments, and achieve comparable performance against human players.
categories: Large Language Models (LLMs) for strategy search and update, Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) for refining and executing strategies, Population-based self-play simulations for optimization

title: High Level Synthesis (HLS) tools offer rapid hardware design from C code, but their compatibility...
abstract: The paper presents a novel approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) for rewriting C code into HLS-synthesizable c, which is usually limited by code constructs in traditional HLS tools. The LLM is shown to implement functions like streaming data and hardware-specific signals, improving the compatibility of the refactored code with HLS. Case studies include NIST 800-22 randomness tests, QuickSort algorithm, and AES-128. The evaluation demonstrates the potential of LLMs in assisting hardware design refactoring by converting regular C code into HLS synthesizable C code.

abstract: This work investigates integrating Large Language Models (LLM) into the Coverage Directed Test Generation (CDG) process for hardware design verification. The LLM functions as a Verilog Reader, accurately grasping the code logic to generate stimuli that reach unexplored code branches. Experiments show this framework outperforms random testing on designs within the LLM's comprehension scope. The work also proposes optimizations for prompt engineering to expand the LLM's understanding scope and accuracy.

abstract: The abstract introduces ARB-LLM, a novel 1-bit post-training quantization technique designed for Large Language Models. The key feature is an alternating refined binarization (ARB) algorithm that reduces the quantization error by progressively updating binarization parameters. Additionally, the method considers the calibration data's pivotal role and column deviation in LLM weights, extending ARB to ARB-X and ARB-RC. The performance is further enhanced with a refined weight partition strategy using column-group bitmap (CGB). The models equipped with CGB are named ARB-LLM_X and ARB-LLM_RC, demonstrating superior performance compared to existing binarization methods for LLMs.

title: Utilizing large language models (LLMs) for tool planning has emerged as a promising avenue for...
abstract: The paper presents the Cost-Aware Tool Planning with LLMs (CATP-LLM) framework, designed to enable large language models (LLMs) for cost-aware tool planning. The CATP-LLM is proposed to address the issue of prior studies overlooking tool execution costs in plan generation, leading to expensive plans. To achieve this, a tool planning language is designed to create multi-branch non-sequential plans efficiently and concurrently, reducing costs. Additionally, a cost-aware offline reinforcement learning algorithm is proposed to fine-tune the LLM for optimizing the performance-cost trade-off in tool planning. The paper also introduces OpenCATP, the first dataset for cost-aware planning, with 11,100 evaluation samples from various tasks. Experiments show that CATP-LLM outperforms GPT-4 using Llama2-7B as its backbone, achieving an average improvement of 1.5%-93.9% in plan quality.

abstract: The abstract discusses SVD-LLM, a novel post-training compression method for Large Language Models (LLMs) that aims to address the limitations of existing SVD-based methods. The proposed approach incorporates a truncation-aware data whitening technique and a parameter update with sequential low-rank approximation to minimize compression loss and mitigate accuracy degradation after SVD compression. Results from evaluations on 10 datasets and seven models across three different LLM families demonstrate the superiority of SVD-LLM, particularly at high model compression ratios.
categories: SVD-based post-training LLM compression, Truncation-aware data whitening technique, Parameter update with sequential low-rank approximation

title: Integrating large language models (LLMs) into personal assistants, like Xiao Ai and Blue Heart V,...
abstract: The study focuses on integrating large language models into personal assistants, enhancing their ability to interact with humans, solve complex tasks, and manage IoT devices. It identifies a significant degradation in user experience due to the latency in generating plans with an LLM. To address this issue, the researchers developed AgentReuse, a plan reuse mechanism that leverages semantic similarities among requests and uses intent classification to evaluate request similarities. This leads to a high effective plan reuse rate of 93% and a significant reduction in latency by 93.12%.

abstract: Reliability and failure detection of large language models (LLMs) is critical for their deployment in high-stakes, multi-step reasoning tasks. This work extends self-evaluation techniques to multi-step tasks by testing two intuitive approaches: holistic scoring and step-by-step scoring.

abstract: [AutoDroid] is a mobile task automation system that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to handle arbitrary tasks on any Android application without manual efforts. The system combines commonsense knowledge of LLMs and domain-specific knowledge of apps through automated dynamic analysis. Key components include a functionality-aware UI representation method, exploration-based memory injection techniques, and a multi-granularity query optimization module to reduce model inference cost.

abstract: This study investigates how the source preferences of large language models (LLMs) affect their resolution of inter-context knowledge conflicts in English. The research is motivated by interdisciplinary studies on credibility. They find that LLMs tend to prefer institutionally-corroborated information (e.g., government or newspaper sources) over information from people and social media. However, these preferences can be reversed by simply repeating information from less credible sources. To mitigate repetition effects, a novel method is proposed that reduces repetition bias significantly while maintaining most original preferences. The researchers evaluate 13 open-weight LLMs comprehensively in a tightly-controlled environment.
categories: Investigation of LLM source preferences using a novel framework., Evaluation of 13 open-weight LLMs, Tightly controlled evaluation process

abstract: SelfControl is a proposed inference-time model control method that utilizes gradients to control the behavior of large language models without explicit human annotations. It offers precise and transparent control, eliminates human supervision, achieves on-the-fly adaptability, and can be used for data synthesis and improving reasoning abilities.

abstract: The abstract discusses the development of FinCon, a multi-agent framework based on large language models (LLMs) designed for various financial tasks. FinCon is modeled after organizational structures of investment firms and utilizes a manager-analyst communication hierarchy to enable synchronized cross-functional agent collaboration towards unified goals.

abstract: The paper introduces RTL++, an LLM-assisted method for RTL code generation that utilizes graph representations of code structures to enhance the quality of generated code. The approach encodes RTL code into textualized control flow graphs (CFG) and data flow graphs (DFG), capturing the inherent hierarchy, dependencies, and relationships within the code.

abstract: The abstract introduces DINGO, a new method for constrained decoding in diffusion LLMs. DINGO aims to solve the issue of unreliability in existing diffusion models for tasks requiring structured outputs, like fixed-schema JSON generation. On standard symbolic math and JSON generation benchmarks, DINGO achieves up to a 68 percentage point improvement over unconstrained inference.

title: With the rapid advancements of large language models (LLMs), information retrieval (IR) systems, such as...
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive survey of emerging bias and unfairness issues in information retrieval (IR) systems due to the integration of large language models (LLMs). The authors unify these issues as distribution mismatch problems, categorizing various mitigation strategies through distribution alignment. They delve into specific issues arising from data collection, model development, and result evaluation stages during LLMs integration into IR systems. The paper reviews recent literature on definitions, characteristics, and corresponding mitigation strategies associated with these issues. Open problems and challenges for future work are identified, aiming to inspire researchers and stakeholders in the IR field. A GitHub repository for relevant papers and resources is maintained at https://github.com/KID-22/LLM-IR-Bias-Fairness-Survey.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have shown high agreement with human raters across a variety of...
categories: Confidence-Driven Inference: a method that combines Large Language Model (LLM) annotations and LLM confidence indicators to strategically select which human annotations should be collected. The goal is to produce accurate statistical estimates and provably valid confidence intervals while reducing the number of human annotations needed. Safeguards are in place against LLM annotations of poor quality, ensuring the conclusions will be both valid and no less accurate than if only relying on human annotations.

title: Visual grounding is an essential tool that links user-provided text queries with query-specific regions within...
abstract: The abstract introduces LLM-Optic, an innovative method that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance existing visual grounding models in comprehending complex text queries. The approach involves using an LLM as a Text Grounder to interpret complex queries and identify objects of interest. A pre-trained visual grounding model is then employed to generate candidate bounding boxes for the refined query. Subsequently, the candidate bounding boxes are annotated with numerical marks to establish a connection between text and image regions. Lastly, a Large Multimodal Model (LMM) is used as a Visual Grounder to select the marked objects that best correspond to the original text query. The method aims to achieve universal visual grounding, allowing for the detection of arbitrary objects specified by arbitrary human language input without requiring additional training or fine-tuning. Experiments demonstrate that LLM-Optic achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot visual grounding capabilities.

abstract: The research proposes a new paradigm for detecting LLM-generated content, introducing two novel tasks: LLM Role Recognition and LLM Influence Measurement. A benchmark, LLMDetect, is proposed, including the Hybrid News Detection Corpus for training detectors and DetectEval for comprehensive evaluation. The study validates these methods and offers insights for developing more effective detection models for LLM-generated content, contributing to a better understanding of this topic.

abstract: The prediction of crystal properties is crucial for the crystal design process. Current methods focus on modeling crystal structures using graph neural networks (GNNs), but accurately modeling complex atom-molecule interactions within a crystal remains challenging. A new benchmark dataset, TextEdge, containing text descriptions of crystal structures and their properties has been developed and made public. A new method, LLM-Prop, is proposed that leverages the general-purpose learning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to predict the physical and electronic properties of crystals from their text descriptions.
categories: Development and release of TextEdge dataset for crystal structure property prediction, Proposal of LLM-Prop method that utilizes LLMs for crystal property prediction, Comparison with the current state-of-the-art GNN-based crystal property predictor

title: Understanding emotional nuances in everyday language is crucial for computational linguistics and emotion research. While...
abstract: The study found that despite the advancements in Large Language Models, they underperformed compared to traditional methods like LIWC and Pattern in predicting emotional valence in spontaneous, real-world narratives in Flemish. Particularly, Pattern showed superior performance. The results challenge assumptions about LLM superiority in sentiment analysis tasks and suggest the need for developing culturally and linguistically tailored evaluation frameworks for low-resource language variants and question whether current LLM fine-tuning approaches adequately address nuanced emotional expressions found in everyday language use.

abstract: The paper introduces RestTSLLM, an approach using Test Specification Language (TSL) and Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the generation of test cases for REST APIs. The approach aims to tackle the creation of test scenarios and input data definition challenges. Evaluation results show that top-performing LLMs such as Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Deepseek R1, Qwen 2.5 32b, and Sabia 3 consistently produced robust and coherent REST API tests, with Claude 3.5 Sonnet outperforming all others across every metric.

abstract: This paper explores the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) as automated judges for software engineering tasks like code translation, generation, and summarization. It compares these methods with human judgments and finds that output-based methods reach high Pearson correlations of 81.32 and 68.51 in code translation and generation respectively, outperforming ChrF++ significantly.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) adapters enable low-cost model specialization, but introduce complex caching and scheduling...
abstract: This paper introduces a data-driven pipeline for optimizing the placement of Large Language Model (LLM) adapters in distributed serving systems. The goal is to serve workloads with the minimum number of GPUs, avoiding request starvation and GPU memory errors. The pipeline employs three components: Digital Twin, machine learning model, and a greedy placement algorithm. The Digital Twin emulates system dynamics with high fidelity, while the learned ML models accelerate performance estimation. The pipeline demonstrates significant improvements in GPU efficiency by reducing the number of GPUs required to sustain target workloads. The approach leverages accurate performance predictions learned from real serving behavior to compute an adapter placement that maximizes GPU efficiency.
categories: Digital Twin (DT): A system emulator tailored to LLM-adapter serving that achieves below 5% throughput estimation error and executes up to 90 times faster than full LLM benchmarking., Machine Learning (ML) Model: Trained on DT-generated data for accurate performance estimation, Greedy Placement Algorithm: Leverages ML-based performance estimates to maximize GPU efficiency

Collection of non-expert human responses for comparison, limited to the Key Factors section due to their brevity. Evaluation of multiple frontier LLMs (GPT-4o-mini, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Deepseek-V3, Gemini-1.5-Flash) using a composite metric framework based on BLEU, Damerau-Levenshtein distance, TF-IDF cosine similarity, and Universal Sentence Encoder similarity. Metric weights are computed through an inversion-based ranking alignment and pairwise AHP analysis, enabling fine-grained comparison of model outputs to expert responses.

abstract: This paper presents a scalable pipeline to train specialized conversational AI models for the finance domain using a general-purpose retrieval embedding model as a foundation. The method yields significant improvements in information retrieval metrics such as MRR@5, mean DCG@5, and NDCG across various financial filing types and document classes. It adapts retrieval embeddings for RAG, not LLM generators, using LLM-judged relevance to distill domain knowledge into a compact retriever.
categories: The pipeline introduces interaction between student and teacher models that interleaves retrieval-based mining of hard positive/negative examples from the unlabeled corpus with iterative retraining of the student model's weights using these examples., Each retrieval iteration uses the refined student model to mine the corpus for progressively harder training examples for the subsequent training iteration., The methodology provides a cost-effective solution to bridging the gap between general-purpose models and specialized domains without requiring labor-intensive human annotation.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have transformed numerous AI applications. On-device LLM is becoming increasingly important:...
categories: Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ) is a hardware-friendly approach for LLM low-bit weight-only quantization., The method finds that not all weights in an LLM are equally important. Protecting only 1% salient weights can greatly reduce quantization error., To identify salient weight channels, the method should refer to the activation distribution, not weights., To avoid the hardware-inefficient mix-precision quantization, a mathematical derivation is made to scale up the salient channels to reduce the quantization error., An equivalent transformation is employed to scale the salient weight channels to protect them. The scale is determined by collecting the activation statistics offline., The method does not rely on any backpropagation or reconstruction, so it generalizes to different domains and modalities without overfitting the calibration set.

title: This paper presents an evaluation framework for assessing Large Language Models' (LLMs) capabilities in combinatorial...
abstract: This paper evaluates Large Language Models' (LLMs) performance in the 2D bin-packing problem using combinatorial optimization. A methodology that integrates LLMs with evolutionary algorithms to generate and refine heuristic solutions is introduced. The study shows that LLMs can produce more efficient solutions with fewer computational resources, as demonstrated by GPT-4o achieving optimal solutions in two iterations, reducing average bin usage from 16 to 15 bins, and improving space utilization from 0.76-0.78 to 0.83. The research contributes to understanding LLM evaluation in specialized domains and sets benchmarks for assessing LLM performance in combinatorial optimization tasks.

title: This position paper argues that large language models (LLMs) constitute promising yet underutilized academic reading...
abstract: Initial findings demonstrate tangible improvements in reading comprehension and engagement among participants using the AI agent (Claude) versus unsupported independent study.

title: Generative AI, including large language models (LLMs) have the potential -- and already are being...
abstract: The abstract discusses the potential for Generative AI, particularly large language models (LLMs), to facilitate unsafe online conversations. It notes that LLMs can lower the barrier for bad actors due to their ability to generate persuasive and human-like text. The current work aims to promote online safety by educating people about such dangerous interactions, both with and without the use of LLMs. The study builds upon research showing LLMs can simulate scam conversations effectively. It also utilizes findings from the learning sciences suggesting that providing feedback on hypothetical actions can encourage learning. The focus is on simulating scam conversations using LLMs, where two models converse to create realistic, unsafe interactions. Users are asked to provide feedback to a target model within the system.

categories: Benchmarking a distilled 1.5B parameter reasoning model (DeepSeek-R1) against several state-of-the-art non-reasoning LLMs within a generator-discriminator LLM planning framework for the text-to-SQL task., Introducing a novel method for extracting soft scores from the chain-of-thought (CoT) outputs from reasoning that enables fine-grained ranking of candidates.

title: This paper addresses the challenge of aligning large language models (LLMs) with diverse human preferences...
abstract: The paper discusses the challenge of aligning large language models (LLMs) with diverse human preferences within federated learning (FL) environments. It presents a comprehensive evaluation framework to assess the trade-off between alignment quality and fairness using different aggregation strategies for human preferences.
categories: The paper uses a federated setting where each group locally evaluates rollouts and produces reward signals, with the server aggregating these group-level rewards without accessing raw data., Standard reward aggregation techniques (min, max, and average) are evaluated, along with a novel adaptive scheme that dynamically adjusts preference weights based on a group's historical alignment performance., The study utilizes a PPO-based RLHF pipeline for question-answering tasks.

title: Text-conditioned diffusion models have emerged as a promising tool for neural video generation. However, current...
abstract: The abstract introduces LLM-grounded Video Diffusion (LVD), a novel approach for neural video generation that addresses the limitations of current models in handling intricate spatiotemporal prompts and generating restricted or incorrect motion. LVD leverages a large language model (LLM) to generate dynamic scene layouts based on text inputs, which are then used to guide a diffusion model for video generation. The study demonstrates that LLMs can understand complex spatiotemporal dynamics from text alone and generate layouts that align closely with both the prompts and the object motion patterns typically observed in the real world.
categories: LVD first uses a large language model (LLM) to generate dynamic scene layouts based on text inputs., The generated layouts are then used to guide a diffusion model for video generation., To adjust the attention maps, the approach guides video diffusion models with these layouts., This method is training-free and can be integrated into any video diffusion model that admits classifier guidance.

We propose Love First, Know Later: a paradigm shift in computational matching that simulates interactions...
abstract: The proposed paradigm, 'Love First, Know Later', shifts from comparing static user profiles in computational matching to simulating interactions first and assessing compatibility later. The framework leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) as text world engines that function both as persona-driven agents following behavioral policies and as the environment modeling interaction dynamics. Compatibility assessment is formalized as a reward-modeling problem where signals are extracted from simulations to predict human preferences. The model hinges on responses to critical moments in relationships, translating observations from relationship psychology into mathematical hypotheses for effective simulation. Theoretically, it's proven that as LLM policies better approximate human behavior, the induced matching converges to optimal stable matching. Empirical validation is done on speed dating data for initial chemistry prediction and divorce prediction for long-term stability. This paradigm enables interactive, personalized matching systems where users iteratively refine their agents, opening up future possibilities for transparent and interactive compatibility assessment.

title: As large language models (LLMs) become increasingly prevalent, ensuring their robustness against adversarial misuse is...
Large language models (LLMs) can often be made to behave in undesirable ways that they...
abstract: The abstract discusses the ability of large language models (LLMs) to behave in undesirable ways despite being fine-tuned not to. It highlights the 'jailbreaking' techniques used to elicit harmful text from models, and suggests that this is due to adversarial fine-tuning suppressing rather than removing undesirable capabilities. The paper introduces Latent Adversarial Training (LAT) as a method to improve robustness to various failures. Untargeted LAT provides generic robustness but doesn't leverage specific failure modes information. In this study, they experiment with targeted LAT that aims to minimize loss on a specific competing task. Results show it improves robustness to jailbreaks, removes backdoors without trigger knowledge, and unlearns specific undesirable tasks more effectively and robustly. The conclusion is that targeted LAT can be an effective tool for defending against harmful behaviors from LLMs.

title: Is an LLM telling you different facts than it's telling me? This paper introduces ConsistencyAI,...
categories: The researchers introduced ConsistencyAI, an independent benchmark for measuring factual consistency of LLMs. This benchmark involved querying 19 LLMs with prompts that requested 5 facts for each of 15 topics, repeated 100 times for each LLM. Each query was accompanied by prompt context from a different persona selected from a subset of personas modeling the general population. The responses were processed into sentence embeddings, and cross-persona cosine similarity was computed. The weighted average of cross-persona cosine similarity was then calculated to determine factual consistency scores. A score range of 0.9065 to 0.7896 was observed, with a mean of 0.8656 adopted as a benchmark threshold. The most consistent LLM, according to these experiments, was xAI's Grok-3, while several lightweight models ranked lowest.

title: Training new engineers in digital design is a challenge, particularly when it comes to teaching...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of teaching digital design to new engineers, focusing on the complex Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tooling such as Quartus Prime and Vivado for deploying designs in Verilog and VHDL languages. It explores the potential use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in generating novice-friendly explanations of compile-time synthesis error messages from these tools, aiming to simplify the learning process. The study involves creating 936 error message explanations using three OpenAI LLMs over 21 different buggy code samples.
categories: Generating explanations for compile-time synthesis error messages using Large Language Models (LLMs), Evaluating the generated explanations for relevance and correctness, Investigating the suitability of the explanations for novice learners

abstract: This work introduces Macaw-LLM, a multi-modal large language model that integrates visual, audio, and textual information. It proposes a novel alignment module to bridge multi-modal features to textual features, simplifying the adaptation process.
categories: Macaw-LLM consists of three main components: a modality module for encoding multi-modal data, a cognitive module for harnessing pretrained LLMs, and an alignment module for harmonizing diverse representations.

title: Evaluating the creative capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in complex tasks often requires human...
abstract: The abstract introduces a scalable methodology for evaluating the creative story generation capabilities of large language models (LLMs) by analyzing underlying social structures in narratives as signed character networks. A large-scale comparative analysis was conducted using networks from over 1,200 stories generated by four leading LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and Gemini 1.5 Flash) and a human-written corpus. The findings reveal that LLM-generated stories consistently exhibit a strong bias towards tightly-knit, positive relationships.
categories: Development of a novel methodology for evaluating LLM story generation by analyzing underlying social structures in narratives as signed character networks., Creation of networks from over 1,200 stories generated by four leading LLMs and human-written stories., Analysis of network properties like density, clustering, and signed edge weights to evaluate the creative storytelling capabilities of LLMs.

abstract: The paper presents CCSBench, a new benchmark for compositional controllable summarization in the scientific domain. It aims to broaden the dissemination of scientific knowledge by controlling multiple attributes such as length and empirical focus simultaneously. The study conducts experiments using various large language models under different settings to balance control over these attributes but finds that LLMs struggle to manage trade-offs, particularly with implicit attributes requiring deeper understanding and abstract reasoning.

abstract: The abstract discusses the transformation brought by Large Language Models (LLMs) in Natural Language Processing, focusing on post-training techniques as the new focus for breakthroughs. Post-training methods refine LLMs' knowledge, improve reasoning, enhance factual accuracy, and align with user intents and ethical considerations. Three critical strategies for optimizing LLMs performance are mentioned: fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and test-time scaling.

The design is borrowed from human cultural evolution literature and involves LLM agents receiving, producing, and transmitting texts. The evolution of text toxicity, positivity, difficulty, and length across transmission chains was tracked to uncover biases and attractors.

title: Jailbreaking is an emerging adversarial attack that bypasses the safety alignment deployed in off-the-shelf large...
abstract: The paper presents SELFDEFEND, a lightweight defense mechanism against jailbreaking attacks on off-the-shelf large language models (LLMs). The defense works by detecting harmful prompts in user inputs and triggering a checkpoint to ensure safety. The key insight is that jailbreak strategies ultimately involve a harmful prompt, which existing LLMs can identify as they violate safety policies. The proposed method involves a shadow stack that concurrently checks for harmful prompts in the user input and triggers a checkpoint in the normal stack when a 'No' token or harmful prompt is output. The system can also generate explainable responses to adversarial prompts.
categories: Design of a shadow stack that checks for harmful prompts in user inputs, Triggering of a checkpoint in the normal stack when a 'No' token or harmful prompt is output, Generation of explainable responses to adversarial prompts

title: Padding tokens are widely used in large language models (LLMs) to equalize sequence lengths during...
abstract: Padding tokens in large language models (LLMs) can influence computation due to implementation errors, despite being intended to be fully masked. The study investigates this effect across three open-source model families (Llama, Gemma, Qwen), examining the impact of controlled amounts of padding on activations, generation quality, bias, and safety.
categories: Inserting controlled amounts of padding in various models, Evaluating outcomes along four axes: activations, generation quality, bias, and safety

abstract: All four LLMs could generate syntactically and semantically correct, mostly non-ambiguous artifacts. They were found to be inconsistent at times and differ significantly in the completeness of the generated specifications. Claude was observed to generate more complete specification artifacts overall, while Gemini was more precise in the specifications it generated. The study indicates that there is room for improvement, particularly in generating relevant Business Rules.

title: Large vision-language models (VLMs) can jointly interpret images and text, but they are also prone...
abstract: The abstract introduces a new benchmark for evaluating large vision-language models (VLMs). The benchmark consists of 1,343 image-question pairs with ground-truth answers and demographic attributes such as age, gender, race, occupation, and sports. The study examines how visual context influences model outputs in open-ended settings and finds that bias prevalence varies across attributes and models, with a high risk for gender and occupation. Contrary to expectation, higher faithfulness does not necessarily lead to lower bias. The researchers employ a large language model as judge and conduct human verification. They release the benchmark prompts, evaluation rubric, and code to support reproducible and fairness-aware multimodal assessment.

abstract: The abstract discusses a novel context engineering workflow for improving the accuracy and reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs) in automating code generation and software engineering tasks, particularly in complex, multi-file projects.

title: In healthcare intelligence, the ability to fuse heterogeneous, multi-intent information from diverse clinical sources is...
categories: MedAide proposes an LLM-based medical multi-agent collaboration framework for intent-aware information fusion and coordinated reasoning. The framework includes a regularization-guided module that combines syntactic constraints with retrieval augmented generation to decompose complex queries into structured representations. This facilitates fine-grained clinical information fusion and intent resolution., A dynamic intent prototype matching module is proposed for adaptive recognition and updating of the agent's intent in multi-round healthcare dialogues, utilizing dynamic prototype representation with a semantic similarity matching mechanism., Lastly, MedAide introduces a rotation agent collaboration mechanism that includes dynamic role rotation and decision-level information fusion across specialized medical agents.

title: Device-cloud collaboration holds promise for deploying large language models (LLMs), leveraging lightweight on-device models for...
abstract: This research focuses on device-cloud collaboration for deploying large language models (LLMs). The main challenge lies in determining whether an incoming query should be processed locally or offloaded to the cloud. Traditional methods use external routers that struggle with complex reasoning tasks. To address this, the authors propose a reinforcement learning based approach where on-device LLMs can decide internally whether to invoke cloud assistance during inference time. This is achieved by casting the on-device LLM post-training as a reward maximization problem, and designing hierarchical rewards to encourage local problem solving and judicious cloud offloading. The authors develop an algorithm featuring a group-level policy gradient that stabilizes optimization, together with adaptive prompt filtering that provides complementary learning signals to mitigate policy collapse. Experiments on various reasoning benchmarks show improvements over baselines and a reduced gap compared to full cloud LLMs.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has proven effective for training large language models (LLMs)...
abstract: The study introduces a Self-aware Weakness-driven problem Synthesis framework (SwS) for large language models (LLMs). SwS identifies model deficiencies through repeated sampling during RL training and synthesizes new problems to strengthen the model's weak areas. This approach enables robust generalization, as the model self-identifies and addresses its weaknesses in Reinforcement Learning, improving average performance by 10.0% and 7.7% on 7B and 32B models across eight mainstream reasoning benchmarks.

abstract: The research proposed in this abstract introduces a novel attack technique, the 'embedding space attack', for open-source language models (LLMs). This method targets the continuous embedding representation of input tokens and is said to be more efficient at causing harmful behavior than other approaches. The findings also suggest that these attacks can extract supposedly deleted information from unlearned LLMs across multiple datasets and models, emphasizing their importance in the context of open-source LLMs.

abstract: The abstract discusses the vulnerability of safety-critical applications using analog and mixed-signal integrated circuits (ICs) to security threats due to outsourcing to untrusted third-party foundries, specifically focusing on stealthy analog Trojans. Analog Trojans are less studied compared to digital ones, with limited research on their diversity and stealth in analog designs.
categories: The authors propose LATENT, a framework that utilizes a large language model (LLM) as an autonomous agent to craft circuit-specific, stealthy analog Trojans., LATENT intelligently inserts and refines Trojan components within analog designs based on iterative feedback from a detection model., This feedback loop ensures that the inserted Trojans remain stealthy while successfully evading detection.

title: Recently, people have suffered from LLM hallucination and have become increasingly aware of the reliability...
abstract: The abstract discusses the increasing concerns about LLMs (Large Language Models) reliability in open and knowledge-intensive tasks due to hallucination issues. To address this, people have resorted to search-augmented LLMs. However, these systems can also be misused, leading to harmful content retrieval that is difficult to retract. The authors propose 'SearchAttack' for red-teaming to investigate LLM's unsafe search behavior. They curate a domain-specific illicit activity benchmark and introduce a fact-checking framework. Extensive experiments are conducted to evaluate the vulnerabilities of these systems. The study finds that even without web search, LLMs can be steered towards harmful content output due to their stereotypical information-seeking behaviors.
categories: Proposal of 'SearchAttack' for red-teaming: rephrases harmful semantics via dense and benign knowledge to evade direct in-context decoding, eliciting unsafe information retrieval., Stress-testing LLMs' reward-chasing bias by steering them to synthesize unsafe retrieved content., Curation of an emergent, domain-specific illicit activity benchmark for search-based threat assessment., Introduction of a fact-checking framework to ground and quantify harm in both offline and online attack settings.

title: Synthetic personae experiments have become a prominent method in Large Language Model alignment research, yet...
abstract: The analysis of 63 peer-reviewed studies between 2023 and 2025 reveals a critical gap: task and population of interest are often underspecified in persona-based experiments. The study finds substantial differences in user representation, with most focusing on limited sociodemographic attributes and only 35% discussing the representativeness of their LLM personae. To address this issue, the authors introduce a persona transparency checklist that emphasizes representative sampling, explicit grounding in empirical data, and enhanced ecological validity. The work offers a comprehensive assessment of current practices and practical guidelines to improve the rigor and ecological validity of persona-based evaluations in language model alignment research.

title: In this paper, we present a new framework, named GPTAid, for automatic APSRs generation by...
abstract: The paper introduces GPTAid, a framework that automatically generates API Security Recommendations (APSRs) by analyzing API source code using LLM and detecting API misuse caused by incorrect parameter use. GPTAid achieves a precision of 92.3% on a dataset containing 200 randomly selected APIs from eight popular libraries. The framework has been shown to be effective in API misuse detection, with 210 unknown security bugs found potentially resulting in severe security issues across 47 applications. 150 of these have been confirmed by developers after reports.

title: We present LinkQ, a system that leverages a large language model (LLM) to facilitate knowledge...
categories: LinkQ is a system that uses a large language model (LLM) to facilitate knowledge graph (KG) query construction., The system interprets a user's question and converts it into a well-formed query through a multistep protocol., LinkQ helps users iteratively refine any open-ended questions into precise ones, supporting both targeted and exploratory analysis., To guard against the LLM hallucinating outputs, LinkQ ensures that answers are only ever derived from ground truth KG data.

abstract: The abstract introduces a novel problem setting that integrates a Multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) into cooperative autonomous driving through Vehicle-to-Vehicle Question-Answering (V2V-QA) dataset and benchmark.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are widely believed to possess self-correction capabilities, yet recent studies suggest...
abstract: The abstract discusses the study on self-correction capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). It reveals an 'Accuracy-Correction Paradox', where weaker models (GPT-3.5) have higher intrinsic correction rates than stronger models (DeepSeek), despite having lower accuracy. The paper systematically decomposes self-correction into three sub-capabilities: error detection, error localization, and error correction. The study suggests that stronger models make fewer but deeper errors that resist self-correction, a phenomenon known as the 'Error Depth Hypothesis'.
categories: Cross-model experiments on GSM8K-Complex dataset with three major LLMs: GPT-3.5, DeepSeek, and Claude., Evaluation of models' accuracy, intrinsic correction rates, error detection rates, and the impact of providing error location hints.

title: Large language models (LLMs) offer an inexpensive yet powerful way to annotate text, but are...
abstract: The abstract discusses the inconsistency between large language models (LLMs) and experts in text annotation, which can lead to bias in downstream estimates. To combat this, debiasing methods like Design-based Supervised Learning (DSL) and Prediction-Powered Inference (PPI) are introduced, combining LLM annotations with limited expert annotations for valid estimation. The study investigates the performance of these methods as the number of expert annotations varies, highlighting scenarios where LLM bias or limited expert labels impact results. Additionally, DSL and PPI are compared across various tasks, revealing that both have low bias with large datasets but DSL often outperforms PPI on bias reduction and efficiency. However, DSL's performance is less consistent across datasets. The research suggests a bias-variance tradeoff among debiasing methods and calls for more research into metrics for quantifying their efficiency in finite samples.
categories: Design-based Supervised Learning (DSL), Prediction-Powered Inference (PPI)

title: We propose reinforcement learning (RL) strategies tailored for reasoning in large language models (LLMs) under...
We present the LLM Economist, a novel framework that uses agent-based modeling to design and...
abstract: The LLM Economist framework utilizes agent-based modeling to design and evaluate economic policies in complex strategic environments with hierarchical decision-making. Lower level worker agents, modeled after U.S. Census data, make labor supply decisions based on text-based utility functions learned through context. An upper level planner agent employs reinforcement learning to propose piecewise-linear marginal tax schedules, mimicking the current U.S. federal brackets. The simulated economic systems possess three crucial capabilities for credible fiscal experimentation: optimization of heterogeneous utilities, generation of large demographically realistic agent populations, and mechanism design expressed in natural language. Experiments reveal that the planner converges near Stackelberg equilibria which boost aggregate social welfare compared to Saez solutions. A periodic voting procedure enhances these gains under decentralized governance. The research suggests that large language model-based agents can collaborate in modeling, simulating, and governing complex economic systems, offering a scalable platform for policy evaluation and societal improvement.
categories: Agent-based modeling, Bounded rational worker agents with persona-conditioned prompts calibrated using U.S. Census data, Text-based utility functions learned in-context, Upper level planner agent using reinforcement learning for proposing piecewise-linear marginal tax schedules, Natural language mechanism design, Experimentation with up to 100 interacting agents

abstract: The paper introduces DeFiScope, an LLM-based approach for detecting DeFi price manipulation attacks in both standard and custom price models. This system is designed to be more effective than existing systems like DeFiRanger and DeFort on non-standard price models found in custom DeFi protocols. DeFiScope achieves high recall, precision, and zero false alarms on benign transactions, helping an industry partner confirm 147 real-world price manipulation attacks and discover 81 previously unknown historical incidents.

title: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) for domain-specific question-answering (QA) tasks by leveraging...
abstract: Vendi-RAG is a new framework that improves large language models for domain-specific question-answering tasks by optimizing retrieval diversity and answer quality in an iterative process. It uses the Vendi Score, a flexible similarity-based diversity metric, to promote semantic diversity in document retrieval. An LLM judge evaluates candidate answers after a reasoning step and outputs a score for balancing relevance and diversity among retrieved documents during each iteration. Experiments on three challenging datasets show Vendi-RAG's effectiveness in multi-hop reasoning tasks, with accuracy improvements over traditional RAG approaches. Significant accuracy increases were observed: +4.2% on HotpotQA, +4.1% on 2WikiMultiHopQA, and +1.3% on MuSiQue compared to Adaptive-RAG, the current best baseline. The benefits of Vendi-RAG are more pronounced as the number of retrieved documents increases, and it has been shown to be model-agnostic across different LLM backbones like GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and GPT-4o-mini.

title: As the demand for emotional intelligence in large language models (LLMs) grows, a key challenge...
abstract: This study investigates the internal mechanisms that give rise to emotional expression in large language models (LLMs). The researchers address three core questions: 1) whether LLMs have context-agnostic mechanisms shaping emotional expression, 2) what form these mechanisms take, and 3) if they can be utilized for universal emotion control. To do this, they create a controlled dataset called SEV, which elicits comparable internal states across emotions. They then extract emotion directions that consistently encode emotion across contexts (Q1). Neurons and attention heads are identified as local components implementing emotional computation through analytical decomposition and causal analysis (Q2). Directly modulating these circuits achieves high accuracy in controlling emotional expression, surpassing prompting- and steering-based methods (Q3). The study is said to be the first systematic one to uncover and validate emotion circuits in LLMs.

abstract: This study investigates the potential of large language models (LLMs) in addressing personalization challenges in mobile health, specifically for physical activity promotion. The research is inspired by health coaching and involves formative interviews with health professionals and potential coaching recipients to develop design principles for an LLM-based health coach. The resulting chatbot, named GPTCoach, incorporates elements from an evidence-based coaching program, motivational interviewing strategies, and wearable data to create personalized physical activity plans.

abstract: The paper discusses the potential risk of injecting harmful information into Large Language Models (LLMs) by means of knowledge editing, termed as Editing Attack. The study focuses on two safety risks: Misinformation Injection and Bias Injection. It is revealed that editing attacks can introduce both commonsense and long-tail misinformation into LLMs, with a high effectiveness for the former. Also, biased sentences can be injected into LLMs with high efficiency, causing a degradation of overall fairness. The paper highlights the stealthiness of editing attacks, indicating the rising risks of compromising the safety alignment of LLMs and disseminating misinformation or bias through these new channels.

abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework that combines stochastic environmental perturbations (God Mode) and Large Language Model (LLM)-based behavioral profiling to transform evolved genotypes into interpretable character archetypes in the context of the Iterated Prisoners Dilemma (IPD). The study aims to fill two gaps in evolutionary game theory research: the absence of realistic environmental stressors during strategy evolution and the Interpretability Gap, where evolved genetic strategies remain opaque binary sequences devoid of semantic meaning. Evolved strategies under chaotic conditions exhibit superior resilience and present distinct behavioral phenotypes, such as Ruthless Capitalists and Diplomatic Enforcers, which are readily classified by LLMs but difficult to interpret through manual genome inspection alone.

abstract: The study applies the QuaLLM framework to analyze over one million comments from two rideshare worker communities on Reddit, marking the largest study of its kind. The results reveal significant concerns among workers about AI and algorithmic platform decisions, aligning with regulatory calls for worker insights. The work sets a precedent for using AI-assisted quantitative data analysis to identify concerns in online forums.

categories: LLaVul is proposed as a multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) tailored to provide fine-grained reasoning about code through question-answering (QA)., The model is trained to integrate paired code and natural queries into a unified space., The authors evaluate the model's performance using a curated dataset of real-world vulnerabilities paired with security-focused questions and answers.

title: Integration of Large Language Models with search/retrieval engines has become ubiquitous, yet these systems harbor...
abstract: The abstract discusses a study on the 'chameleon behavior' of large language models (LLMs) in multi-turn conversations, particularly in search-enabled systems. The research reveals that these models exhibit an alarming tendency to shift stances when faced with contradictory questions, leading to instability and reliability issues. The authors present a novel Chameleon Benchmark Dataset containing 17,770 question-answer pairs across 12 controversial domains. They introduce two metrics: the Chameleon Score (0-1) for quantifying stance instability and Source Re-use Rate (0-1) for measuring knowledge diversity. The evaluation of several models, including Llama-4-Maverick, GPT-4o-mini, and Gemini-2.5-Flash, shows consistent failures with high chameleon behavior scores. The study uncovers the mechanism: a strong correlation between source re-use rate, confidence, and stance changes, suggesting that limited knowledge diversity makes models overly deferential to query framing. The authors emphasize the importance of comprehensive consistency evaluation before deploying LLMs in critical domains like healthcare, law, and finance.

abstract: LLM-Blender is an ensembling framework that utilizes multiple open-source large language models (LLMs) to achieve superior performance by leveraging their diverse strengths. It consists of two modules: PairRanker and GenFuser. PairRanker employs a specialized pairwise comparison method to identify the best candidate output for a given input text. GenFuser aims to merge the top-ranked candidates, generating an improved output by capitalizing on their strengths and mitigating their weaknesses. A new benchmark dataset, MixInstruct, was introduced to facilitate large-scale evaluation. The researchers found that LLM-Blender significantly outperforms individual LLMs and baseline methods across various metrics, establishing a substantial performance gap.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being implemented as joint decision-makers and explanation generators for...
abstract: This paper evaluates Large Language Models (LLMs) used as decision-makers and explanation generators in Group Recommender Systems (GRS). The study compares these recommendations and explanations to social choice-based aggregation strategies, such as Additive Utilitarian (ADD). The findings indicate that LLM-generated recommendations resemble those produced by ADD aggregation. However, the explanations often refer to averaging ratings but are not identical to ADD. The group structure, whether uniform or divergent, does not impact the recommendations. LLMs regularly use additional criteria such as user or item similarity, diversity, or undefined popularity metrics or thresholds.

title: Refusal training is widely used to prevent LLMs from generating harmful, undesirable, or illegal outputs....
abstract: The study investigates a vulnerability in current refusal training approaches for LLMs (Language Learning Models). The researchers found that reformulating harmful requests in the past tense can 'jailbreak' many state-of-the-art models, with significantly higher success rates compared to direct requests. They evaluated this method on various models including Llama-3 8B, Claude-3.5 Sonnet, GPT-3.5 Turbo, Gemma-2 9B, Phi-3-Mini, GPT-4o mini, GPT-4o, o1-mini, o1-preview, and R2D2, using GPT-3.5 Turbo as a reformulation model. For instance, the success rate of this attack on GPT-4o increased from 1% using direct requests to 88% using 20 past tense reformulation attempts on harmful requests from JailbreakBench with GPT-4 as a judge.

abstract: The paper introduces Raconteur, a shell command explainer powered by large language models (LLMs). Raconteur aims to provide comprehensive and understandable explanations for shell commands, including both behavior and purpose. It is equipped with professional knowledge to explain not only what the command does but also why it does so. To clarify the high-level intent of the command, Raconteur translates natural-language-based explanations into standard techniques & tactics defined by MITRE ATT&CK.
categories: Raconteur uses LLMs for explanation generation., It incorporates professional knowledge to provide detailed explanations., To shed light on the command's high-level intent, Raconteur translates explanations into MITRE ATT&CK techniques & tactics., A documentation retriever is developed to assist in explaining unseen private commands.

abstract: The abstract discusses Contextual Memory Virtualisation (CMV), a system designed to manage the accumulated state and understanding within large language models during extended reasoning tasks. It describes how CMV models session history as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) with snapshot, branch, and trim primitives for context reuse across independent parallel sessions. The abstract introduces a three-pass structurally lossless trimming algorithm to reduce token counts by an average of 20% and up to 86%, primarily by removing mechanical bloat.

title: Large language models are increasingly adopted as semantic backbones for neural text-to-speech systems. However, frozen...
abstract: The abstract discusses the fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) in neural text-to-speech systems. This approach improves voice consistency and Signal to Noise Ratio (SNR) in voice cloning tasks. Across multiple speakers, LoRA finetuning outperforms the base Qwen-0.5B model, improving perceptual quality, speaker fidelity, and signal level quality. Improvements are strongly linked to the characteristics of the training data, particularly for speakers with high variability in acoustic energy and perceptual quality. The study concludes that LoRA finetuning is not just a parameter efficient optimization technique but an effective mechanism for better speaker-level adaptation in compact LLM-based TTS systems. When supported by sufficiently diverse training data, the LoRA-adapted Qwen-0.5B model consistently surpasses its frozen base model in terms of perceptual quality and speaker similarity, with low latency using GGUF model hosted in quantized form.

abstract: The paper investigates the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) to integrate Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) functionalities into ML training code bases. Two models, OpenAI's gpt-3.5-turbo and WizardCoder, are evaluated for their performance in automating various MLOps tasks such as experiment tracking, model optimization, hyperparameter optimization, and model registration. A benchmarking study is conducted to assess the models' ability to adapt existing code samples with MLOps functionality, and translate from one component of an MLOps functionality to another.
categories: Evaluation of gpt-3.5-turbo and WizardCoder models in performing various MLOps tasks, Assessment of the models' ability to adapt existing code samples with MLOps functionalities (Inlining), Benchmarking study to evaluate the models' capacity for translating from one component of an MLOps functionality to another, Proposal of three approaches teaching LLMs to comprehend API documentation while accomplishing Translation tasks

title: The current study describes a cost-effective method for adapting large language models (LLMs) for academic...
abstract: The study presents a cost-effective method for adapting a large language model (LLM) for academic advising in study-abroad contexts using low-resource methods for acculturation. After training the Mistral-7B-Instruct model in two stages, the researchers demonstrate a reduction in training loss by 52.7%, 92% accuracy in domain-specific recommendations, 95% markdown-based formatting support, and a median run-rate of 100 samples per second on off-the-shelf GPU equipment. The findings support the effective application of instruction-tuned LLMs within educational advisers, particularly in low-resource institutional scenarios.

abstract: The abstract presents PhishDebate, a multi-agent framework based on large language models (LLMs) for improved phishing website detection. It addresses the limitations of single-agent classification approaches by using modular debate to achieve more accurate and interpretable decisions. PhishDebate reduces uncertain predictions and provides transparent reasoning, functioning as an analyst-augmentation system for early detection of phishing threats.

abstract: The abstract introduces an open emergency triage benchmark for deterioration prediction focused on ICU transfer and in-hospital mortality. This benchmark is constructed using large language models (LLMs) to harmonize noisy fields, prioritize clinically relevant vitals and labs, and guide schema alignment and merging of disparate tables. The benchmark defines two regimes: a hospital-rich setting with multiple data sources and an MCI-like field simulation limited to vital signs, observations, and notes.

title: Traditional approaches for designing analog circuits are time-consuming and require significant human expertise. Existing automation...
abstract: The abstract introduces LEDRO, a novel approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) in conjunction with optimization techniques to iteratively refine the design space for analog circuit sizing. LEDRO is highly generalizable compared to other Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Bayesian Optimization (BO) baselines, as it eliminates the need for design annotation or model training for different topologies or technology nodes. A comprehensive evaluation was conducted on 22 different Op-Amp topologies across four FinFET technology nodes, demonstrating LEDRO's superior performance and efficiency.

abstract: The abstract introduces LLM Chemistry, a framework that measures synergistic or antagonistic behaviors in combinations of large language models (LLMs). The framework analyzes interaction dependencies between collaborating models to recommend optimal model ensembles based on heterogeneous model profiles, task type, group size, and complexity. Initial evaluation provides evidence for task-dependent effects, reinforcing the theoretical analysis.

abstract: The paper argues that a single general-purpose Language Model (LLM) may not be sufficient for reliable outputs, particularly in complex scenarios. It advocates for multi-LLM collaboration as a solution to better represent diverse data, skills, and people. The paper organizes existing multi-LLM collaborations into a hierarchy based on access and information exchange levels. Multi-LLM collaboration is claimed to address challenges that a single LLM struggles with, such as reliability, democratization, and pluralism. However, the paper also identifies limitations of current methods and motivates future work towards compositional intelligence and collaborative AI development.

abstract: The paper presents LLM-Pilot, a system designed to characterize and predict the performance of Large Language Model (LLM) inference services. The system performs benchmarking under realistic workloads across various GPUs, optimizes service configurations for each GPU, learns a predictive model from the characterization data, and recommends cost-effective hardware for unseen LLMs. Comparative analysis shows that LLM-Pilot delivers on performance requirements 33% more frequently than existing methods and reduces costs by 60% on average.

title: The exceptional potential of large language models (LLMs) in handling text information has garnered significant...
abstract: The abstract discusses a proposal for a position-aware trading agent system, named FinPos, to improve upon existing trading agents that operate in intraday, independent tasks. FinPos utilizes large language models (LLMs) to handle text information and aims to simulate a more realistic market by explicitly modeling and managing continuous positions. It achieves this through three key mechanisms: professional-level interpretation of diverse market data, a dual-agent decision structure separating directional reasoning from risk-aware position adjustment, and multi-timescale reward signals for experiential feedback. Experiments demonstrate that FinPos outperforms state-of-the-art trading agents in the proposed position-aware trading task, reflecting real-world market conditions. The study suggests a significant, largely untapped potential for LLM-centered agent systems in long-term market decision-making.

title: Given the challenge of automatically evaluating free-form outputs from large language models (LLMs), an increasingly...
abstract: The study investigates the use of large language models (LLMs) as their own judging mechanism for evaluating other LLM outputs. This approach is common but only accounts for sampling variability and ignores judge quality uncertainty. The research proposes a novel geometric perspective, representing LLM judges and candidates as points on an $(M-1)$-dimensional probability simplex. This representation allows for intuitive theoretical conditions and visual proofs to determine when rankings are identifiable, such as the 'folk wisdom' that LLM judges are more effective for two-level scoring than multi-level scoring. The study also introduces geometric Bayesian priors to model epistemic uncertainty about judge quality and conduct sensitivity analyses. The experiments on LLM benchmarks show that rankings based solely on LLM judges are robust in many but not all datasets, highlighting both their widespread success and the need for caution.
categories: Representation of LLM judges and candidates as points on an $(M-1)$-dimensional probability simplex., Use of geometric concepts to correspond to key ranking concepts., Design of geometric Bayesian priors to encode epistemic uncertainty about judge quality., Varying the priors for sensitivity analyses.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are popular for high-quality text generation but can produce harmful content,...
categories: LLM Self Defense: A simple approach to defend against harmful content produced by large language models (LLMs). The method involves having an LLM screen the induced responses. It analyzes the generated content, incorporates it into a pre-defined prompt, and employs another instance of an LLM to analyze the text and predict whether it is harmful., The system does not require any fine-tuning, input preprocessing, or iterative output generation.

title: Conversational question-answering (CQA) systems aim to create interactive search systems that effectively retrieve information by...
categories: Proposed simulation framework for conversational question-answering (CQA) systems that employs zero-shot learner Large Language Models (LLMs). The framework involves two LLMs interacting on a specific topic, with the first LLM acting as a student, generating questions to explore a given search topic. The second LLM plays the role of a teacher by answering questions and is equipped with additional information, including a text on the given topic. Both the student and teacher are implemented by zero-shot prompting the GPT-4 model.

title: Existing large language models (LLMs) show exceptional problem-solving capabilities but might struggle with complex reasoning...
abstract: The paper introduces RAG-Star, a novel approach that integrates retrieved information to guide the tree-based deliberative reasoning process of large language models (LLMs). RAG-Star utilizes Monte Carlo Tree Search to iteratively plan intermediate sub-queries and answers for reasoning based on the LLM itself. It also proposes a retrieval-augmented verification that uses query- and answer-aware reward modeling to consolidate internal and external knowledge, thereby improving the inherent reasoning of LLMs.
categories: RAG-Star approach, Tree-based deliberative reasoning process, Monte Carlo Tree Search for planning intermediate sub-queries, Retrieval-augmented verification with query- and answer-aware reward modeling

title: With Large Language Models (LLMs) being widely used across various tasks, detecting errors in their...
abstract: The abstract introduces ReaLMistake, a new error detection benchmark for Large Language Models (LLMs). It aims to address the lack of research on error detection in LLM responses and focuses on realistic, objective, and diverse errors. The benchmark consists of three tasks with four categories of errors: reasoning correctness, instruction-following, context-faithfulness, and parameterized knowledge. GPT-4 and Llama 2 70B were used for testing the benchmark, and experts provided annotations. The study evaluates error detectors based on 12 LLMs, revealing that top models like GPT-4 and Claude 3 have low recall in detecting errors made by other LLMs, with LLM-based error detectors performing worse than humans. The findings also highlight the unreliability of explanations by LLM-based error detectors, sensitivity to prompt changes, and challenges in improving LLM-based error detection. Popular approaches for improving LLMs do not improve error detection performance.
categories: The study uses a new error detection benchmark called ReaLMistake, which includes three tasks with four categories of errors: reasoning correctness, instruction-following, context-faithfulness, and parameterized knowledge. GPT-4 and Llama 2 70B were used for testing the benchmark, and experts provided annotations.

This is achieved by building upon the concept of LLM-as-a-judge., The evaluation process leverages McDonald's omega, a statistical method used to measure inter-rater reliability., The reliability of LLMs is evaluated when judging the outputs of other LLMs on standard single-turn and multi-turn benchmarks., The impact of temperature on reliability is simultaneously investigated.

title: We introduce LLM-as-an-Interviewer, a novel paradigm for evaluating large language models (LLMs). This approach leverages...
abstract: The study presents a novel evaluation framework for large language models called 'LLM-as-an-Interviewer'. It uses multi-turn interactions where the LLM interviewer provides feedback on responses and poses follow-up questions. The research applies this framework to six models on MATH and DepthQA tasks, demonstrating improved insights into LLM performance, including response quality, adaptability, and ability to handle follow-up queries. The Interview Report, which aggregates findings from the interview process, offers a detailed analysis of the model's strengths and weaknesses, providing a snapshot of its real-world applicability. The code for this framework is publicly available at https://github.com/interview-eval.

title: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) to cater to different human preferences, learning new skills, and...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new framework called Value Augmented Sampling (VAS) for optimizing reward functions in Large Language Models (LLMs). The goal is to align LLMs with different human preferences, learn new skills, and unlearn harmful behavior. VAS maximizes rewards using data sampled from the initial, frozen LLM, avoiding co-training of the policy and value function. It outperforms established baselines such as PPO and DPO on standard benchmarks, achieving comparable results to Best-of-128 with lower inference cost. Notably, VAS does not require access to the weights of the pre-trained LLM, allowing it to adapt LLMs available only as APIs like ChatGPT. The new capability of composing several rewards and controlling their extent during deployment time is highlighted.
categories: Value Augmented Sampling (VAS) framework for reward optimization, Search-based methods such as Best-of-N or Monte-Carlo Tree Search, Reinforcement Learning (RL), PPO and DPO as established baselines
