title: While the widespread use of Large Language Models (LLMs) brings convenience, it also raises concerns...
abstract: The study evaluates the growing influence of Large Language Models (LLMs) in academic research, revealing their penetration across various aspects of scholarly workflows. A proposed framework, named ScholarLens and LLMetrica, is introduced to assess LLM penetration and effectiveness using curated datasets and assessment tools. Experiments demonstrate the rising role of LLMs in scholarly processes, emphasizing the need for transparency, accountability, and ethical practices in LLM usage to maintain academic credibility.

title: Despite the impressive generative abilities of black-box large language models (LLMs), their inherent opacity hinders...
abstract: The paper introduces Matryoshka Pilot (M-Pilot), a lightweight white-box controller for enhancing the capabilities of black-box LLMs. M-Pilot guides a large-scale black-box LLM generator by decomposing complex tasks into intermediate outputs, allowing for controllable multi-turn generation and self-improvement in optimizing intermediate guidance. Empirical evaluations show that this method effectively enhances the capabilities of black-box LLMs in complex, long-horizon tasks.

title: The use of LLM tutors to provide automated educational feedback to students on student assignment...
abstract: The paper presents a method to improve the quality and reliability of automated educational feedback provided by LLM tutors using LLM feedback evaluators (DeanLLMs). The method involves an evaluation framework for LLM-generated feedback, a dataset of virtual assignment submissions, and a fine-tuning process. Findings show that o3-pro performed best in zero-shot labelling, o4-mini in few-shot labelling, and GPT-4.1 achieved human expert level performance after fine-tuning. The method was used to evaluate 2000 feedback instances generated by 10 common commercial LLMs, demonstrating the advancement of providing high-quality educational feedback.

title: There are increasing indications that LLMs are not only used for producing scientific papers, but...
abstract: The study provides a comprehensive analysis of Large Language Models (LLMs) usage across the peer review pipeline in conferences like ICLR, NeurIPS, and ICML. Initially, it finds a systematic interaction effect where LLM-assisted reviews seem more favorable towards LLM-assisted papers compared to those with minimal LLM use. However, upon controlling for paper quality, this apparent preferential treatment dissipates, revealing that LLM-assisted reviews are generally more lenient towards lower quality papers. The study also finds that fully LLM-generated reviews exhibit severe rating compression that fails to adequately distinguish paper quality. Human reviewers using LLMs, on the other hand, substantially reduce this leniency. Lastly, the research indicates that LLM-assisted metareviews are more likely to render accept decisions compared to human metareviews given equivalent reviewer scores, but fully LLM-generated metareviews tend to be harsher. These findings offer valuable insights for developing policies governing LLMs in peer review processes and highlight how these models interact with existing decision-making systems.

title: The recent, widespread availability of Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot may...
abstract: This experience report describes a CS1 course at a large research-intensive university that has fully embraced the use of LLMs. The course intentionally alters its focus to incorporate these tools, by reducing emphasis on syntax and writing code from scratch, and instead focusing on skills needed to work with LLMs effectively. The course emphasizes explaining code, testing code, and decomposing problems into small functions solvable by an LLM. Students are given frequent formative assessments of these skills and three large, open-ended projects in different domains (data science, image processing, and game design) to showcase creativity. Students reported appreciation for learning with the assistance of LLMs and interacting with them in various ways when writing code. The report provides lessons learned for instructors considering incorporating LLMs into their courses.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are reshaping knowledge production as community members increasingly incorporate them into...
categories: The study conducted interviews with 16 Wikipedia editors who had used Large Language Models (LLMs) for their contributions. The interviews aimed to gather insights into how these editors were using the models, what strategies they employed to align LLM outputs with community norms, and how their contributions were received by other editors in the community.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are being widely applied across various fields, but as tasks become...
abstract: The abstract discusses a three-stage meta-judge selection pipeline for large language models (LLMs) to support performance evaluation in complex tasks. The pipeline involves developing a comprehensive rubric with GPT-4 and human experts, using multiple advanced LLM agents for scoring judgments, and applying a threshold to filter out low-scoring judgments. This method is claimed to offer improvements over single-agent baselines on the JudgeBench dataset.
categories: Developing a comprehensive rubric with GPT-4 and human experts., Using three advanced LLM agents for scoring judgments., Applying a threshold to filter out low-scoring judgments.

title: Alignment with human preferences is an important evaluation aspect of LLMs, requiring them to be...
abstract: This work explores the relationship between large language models' (LLMs) generation and evaluation capabilities in aligning with human preferences. A comprehensive analysis of Generation-Evaluation Consistency (GE-consistency) among various LLMs is conducted, revealing a strong correlation between their generation and evaluation capabilities when evaluated by a strong LLM preference oracle.
categories: Conducting a comprehensive analysis of GE-consistency among various LLMs., Proposing a benchmarking paradigm that measures LLM alignment with human preferences without directly evaluating their generated outputs, instead assessing LLMs in their role as evaluators (AlignEval)

title: In this paper, we aim to improve the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs)...
abstract: The paper focuses on enhancing the complex question-answering capabilities of large language models (LLMs) using knowledge graphs (KGs). The proposed solution is an autonomous LLM-based agent framework, known as KG-Agent. This framework allows a small LLM to make decisions during the reasoning process over KGs. The KG-Agent integrates an LLM, multifunctional toolbox, KG-based executor, and knowledge memory, with an iteration mechanism that autonomously selects tools and updates memory for KG reasoning. A program language is used to formulate the multi-hop reasoning process over the KG, and a code-based instruction dataset is synthesized to fine-tune the base LLM.
categories: Proposal of an autonomous LLM-based agent framework called KG-Agent for active decision making during the reasoning process over knowledge graphs., Integration of LLM, multifunctional toolbox, KG-based executor, and knowledge memory in KG-Agent., Development of an iteration mechanism that autonomously selects tools and updates memory for reasoning over KGs., Formulation of the multi-hop reasoning process using program language over the KG., Synthesis of a code-based instruction dataset to fine-tune the base LLM.

title: While large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable capability to generate convincing text across diverse...
abstract: The abstract introduces LLM Attributor, a Python library that offers interactive visualizations for training data attribution of large language models (LLMs). The tool aims to inspect model behaviors, enhance its trustworthiness, and compare model-generated text with user-provided text. The library supports LLaMA2 models fine-tuned with two different datasets: online articles about recent disasters and finance-related question-answer pairs. Users can easily integrate LLM Attributor into their workflow using computational notebooks for interactive visualization of attributions. The tool is open-source and available at https://github.com/poloclub/LLM-Attribution, with a video demo accessible at https://youtu.be/mIG2MDQKQxM.
categories: Provides interactive visualizations for training data attribution of an LLM's text generation., Quickly attributes an LLM's text generation to training data points., Inspects model behaviors through visual and interactive design., Enhances trustworthiness by comparing model-generated text with user-provided text.

title: Measuring the quality of responses generated by LLMs is a challenging task, particularly when it...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel approach for evaluating the quality of responses generated by Language Models (LLMs). This method uses the LLM itself to make evaluations and stabilize results through multiple independent evaluuations, resembling a single-layer narrow LLM network. The network design involves adapting neurons, inspired by deep neural networks, to detect different concepts in each evaluation sample. The authors construct the largest and most diverse English evaluation benchmark (LLMEval$^2$) for LLM evaluators, consisting of 15 tasks, 8 abilities, and 2,553 samples. The experimental results show that a wider network with 2 layers improves kappa correlation coefficient from 0.28 to 0.34.
categories: Using the LLM itself for evaluation and stabilizing results through multiple independent evaluations., Adapting neuron roles for each evaluation sample based on different concepts., Each layer in the network receives representations from all neurons in the previous layer, integrating locally learned evaluation information to obtain a more comprehensive evaluation result., Construction of the LLMEval$^2$ benchmark for evaluating LLMs.

title: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) enable exciting LLM-integrated applications, which perform text-based tasks...
abstract: The paper introduces a new approach called structured queries to improve the resistance of Large Language Models (LLMs) against prompt injection attacks. The approach involves separating prompts and data into two channels, and training an LLM to follow instructions only in the prompt portion of a query. A system that supports this method is presented, with a secure front-end and a specially trained LLM. The paper claims that the new system significantly improves resistance to prompt injection attacks, with little or no impact on utility. The code for the system is available at https://github.com/Sizhe-Chen/StruQ.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as a milestone in artificial intelligence, and their performance...
abstract: The paper discusses the concept of 'capacity density' as a new metric to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) across different scales, focusing on both effectiveness and efficiency. The calculation of capacity density involves introducing a set of reference models, developing a scaling law for predicting downstream performance based on parameter sizes, defining effective parameter size, and formalizing capacity density as the ratio of effective parameter size to actual parameter size of the target LLM. An empirical law, called the 'densing law', is presented indicating an exponential growth in the capacity density of LLMs over time, with the capacity density doubling approximately every three months. The paper suggests that improving capacity density can lead to optimal results with minimal computational overhead and offers new perspectives for future LLM development.
categories: Introducing a set of reference models, Developing a scaling law to predict downstream performance based on parameter sizes, Defining effective parameter size of the target LLM, Formalizing capacity density as the ratio of effective parameter size to actual parameter size of the target LLM

title: Recently, overconfidence in large language models (LLMs) has garnered considerable attention due to its fundamental...
abstract: The study investigates overprecision in large language models (LLMs) by designing a framework that prompts LLMs to generate answers to numerical questions in the form of intervals with a specified confidence level. The study finds that LLMs are highly uncalibrated for numerical tasks, there is no correlation between the length of the interval and the imposed confidence level, and LLM numerical precision varies depending on the task, scale of answer, and prompting technique.
categories:
1) Generation: Prompt the LLM to generate answers to numerical questions in intervals with a certain level of confidence imposed in the prompt. Use various prompting techniques and repeat the same prompt multiple times to gauge randomness effects. 
2) Refinement: Refined answers from the previous phase to improve their quality. 
3) Evaluation: Analyze and study LLM answers in this phase to understand its internal workings.

title: Personalization plays a critical role in numerous language tasks and applications, since users with the...
categories: The proposed method constructs a user-specific embedding for each individual by modeling all her historical contexts through a lightweight plug-in user embedder module. By attaching this embedding to the task input, large language models can better understand and capture user habits and preferences, thereby producing more personalized outputs without tuning their own parameters.

title: Traffic forecasting is pivotal for intelligent transportation systems, where accurate and interpretable predictions can significantly...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of large language models (LLMs) for traffic forecasting in intelligent transportation systems. The challenge lies in the heterogeneity of traffic conditions across diverse locations, leading to varied data distributions. Existing LLM-based solutions often struggle with capturing complex graph relationships and spatiotemporal dependencies due to prompt-tuning. The authors introduce Strada-LLM, a novel multivariate probabilistic forecasting LLM that models both temporal and spatial traffic patterns more effectively by incorporating proximal traffic information as covariates. Strada-LLM outperforms existing prompt-based LLMs, and for adaptability, a lightweight distribution-derived strategy for domain adaptation is proposed. This allows parameter-efficient model updates when encountering new data distributions or altered network topologies under few-shot constraints. Empirical evaluations show that Strada-LLM surpasses state-of-the-art LLM-driven and traditional GNN-based predictors, improving long-term forecasting by 17% in RMSE error and 16% more efficiency. The model maintains robust performance across different LLM backbones with minimal degradation, making it a versatile and powerful solution for real-world traffic prediction tasks.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly prevalent in modern software systems, interfacing between the...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new defense strategy called SecAlign to combat prompt injection attacks on Large Language Models (LLMs) in modern software systems. SecAlign is based on preference optimization, which trains the LLM to favor secure outputs over insecure ones by constructing a preference dataset from prompt-injected inputs and secure/insecure outputs. This method significantly reduces the success rates of various prompt injection attacks to less than 10%, even against advanced, unknown, or yet-to-come attacks. The authors claim that SecAlign maintains practicality with similar utility as before defensive training.
categories: Construction of a preference dataset with prompt-injected inputs, secure outputs, and insecure outputs, Performance of preference optimization on the dataset to teach the LLM to prefer secure over insecure outputs, Evaluation of the defense's efficacy against various prompt injection attacks

title: Combining large language models during training or at inference time has shown substantial performance gain...
categories: The paper presents LLM-TOPLA, a diversity-optimized LLM ensemble method. It introduces the focal diversity metric to capture the diversity-performance correlation among component LLMs of an ensemble. A diversity-optimized ensemble pruning algorithm is developed to select the top-k sub-ensembles from a pool of N base LLMs. The pruning method recommends top-performing LLM subensembles of size S, often much smaller than N. A learn-to-ensemble approach is generated to resolve output inconsistency among all component LLMs of an ensemble.

title: This paper presents Audio-Visual LLM, a Multimodal Large Language Model that takes both visual and...
abstract: The paper introduces Audio-Visual LLM, a multimodal large language model that takes both visual and auditory inputs for holistic video understanding. The model uses modality-augmented training, allowing end-to-end joint training with various video data formats. A high-quality video instruction dataset derived from GPT-4 is introduced, enabling Audio-Visual LLM to process a variety of task-oriented video instructions. Extensive experiments demonstrate strong zero-shot results across multiple video understanding tasks, including competitive performance on audio tasks.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have been increasingly integrated into software engineering and maintenance tasks due...
abstract: The abstract discusses the integration of large language models (LLMs) into software engineering and maintenance tasks, particularly with modern programming languages. However, the comprehension of code written in legacy languages by LLMs is identified as a research gap due to the lack or inaccuracy of documentation in real-world legacy systems.
categories: The study proposes using multiple-choice question answering (MCQA), an emerging LLM evaluation methodology, to evaluate LLM comprehension of legacy code and investigate the impact of comment prevalence and inaccurate comments.

The quality of each subtask's output directly affects the inputs, and hence the cost and success rate, of downstream LLMs.

abstract: The study introduces the PowerPoint Task Completion (PPTC) benchmark to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) ability to create and edit PPT files based on user instructions. The benchmark contains 279 multi-turn sessions covering diverse topics and hundreds of instructions involving multi-modal operations.

title: Missing data imputation is a critical challenge in various domains, such as healthcare and finance,...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel framework, LLM-Forest, to address the challenge of missing data imputation using large language models (LLMs). The proposed method involves creating a 'forest' of few-shot prompt learning LLM 'trees', and aggregating their outputs via confidence-based weighted voting. The framework is established on bipartite information graphs to identify high-quality, relevant neighboring entries with both feature and value granularity.
categories:

Aggregation of outputs via confidence-based weighted voting.
Use of bipartite information graphs to identify high-quality, relevant neighboring entries.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have reshaped natural language processing, powering applications from multi-hop retrieval and...
abstract: The abstract discusses LLM-AutoDiff, a novel framework for Automatic Prompt Engineering (APE). This framework extends textual gradient-based methods to complex, multi-component LLM architectures. It treats each textual input as a trainable parameter and uses feedback-akin to textual gradients from a frozen backward engine LLM for iterative prompt updates. The approach inherently accommodates functional nodes, preserves time-sequential behavior in repeated calls, and isolates distinct sub-prompts. It boosts training efficiency by focusing on error-prone samples through selective gradient computation. Across diverse tasks, it outperforms existing textual gradient baselines in both accuracy and training cost.

abstract: The study models energy consumption and runtime of large language models (LLMs) inference tasks on heterogeneous GPU-CPU systems, developing accurate energy and runtime models for several state-of-the-art LLMs. These models are used to explore an offline, energy-optimal LLM workload scheduling framework. A case study demonstrates the benefits of energy and accuracy aware scheduling compared to existing best practices.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized the field of AI, demonstrating unprecedented capacity across various...
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient inference pipeline for large language models (LLMs). The approach utilizes LLM's ability to predict response length with minimal overhead, and introduces an efficient sequence scheduling technique that groups queries with similar response lengths into micro-batches. Evaluation on real-world instruction datasets shows an 86% improvement in inference throughput without sacrificing effectiveness. The method is orthogonal to other inference acceleration techniques, making it a valuable addition to many existing LLM inference toolkits.

abstract: The abstract discusses a novel framework named LLM-Personalize for personalizing Large Language Models (LLMs) in the context of household robotics.

title: The advent of large language models (LLMs) has significantly advanced the field of code translation,...
abstract: The paper presents a novel method to enhance code translation using Few-Shot Learning and retrieval-based techniques. The approach leverages a repository of existing code translations to dynamically retrieve relevant examples for guiding large language models (LLMs) during new code segment translation. The proposed method, based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), significantly improves translation quality by providing contextual examples for real-time learning. Experiments on various datasets with open and commercial LLM models show that the approach outperforms traditional zero-shot methods, particularly in translating between Fortran and CPP.

The study examines the performance of leading LLMs such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, DeepSeek, Grok, Vicuna, and Mistral.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate substantial potential across a diverse array of domains via request...
abstract: The abstract discusses Large Language Models (LLMs) and their potential across various domains through request serving. As context sizes expand, the autoregressive nature of LLMs leads to dynamic behavior of attention layers, posing challenges for resource management and performance optimization in service systems. To overcome this issue, the authors propose Infinite-LLM, a novel LLM serving system designed to manage dynamic context lengths effectively.
categories: Infinite-LLM disaggregates attention layers from an LLM's inference process., This facilitates flexible and independent resource scheduling for optimized computational performance and enhanced memory utilization., A pooled GPU memory strategy is leveraged across a cluster to boost system throughput and support extensive context lengths., The evaluation of Infinite-LLM on a dataset with context lengths ranging from a few to 2000K tokens across a cluster with 32 A100 GPUs demonstrates throughput improvement of 1.35-3.4x compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Writing effective prompts for large language models (LLM) can be unintuitive and burdensome. In response,...
categories: The abstract discusses the use of services that optimize or suggest prompts for large language models (LLM). These services can reduce user effort but introduce a risk: the prompt provider can subtly manipulate prompts to produce biased LLM responses., The authors demonstrate that subtle synonym replacements in prompts can increase the likelihood of LLMs mentioning a target concept. They conducted a user study to substantiate their observations, showing that adversarially perturbed prompts: 1) are indistinguishable from unaltered prompts by humans, 2) push LLMs to recommend target concepts more often, and 3) make users more likely to notice target concepts, all without arousing suspicion.

abstract: The research finds that the Data-Aug LLM achieves the best overall performance for security patch detection, while the ReAct Agent demonstrates the lowest false positive rate (FPR). Existing baseline methods exhibit strong accuracy but have significantly higher false positive rates. The evaluated methods achieve comparable accuracy with substantially reduced false positive rates. The study provides valuable insights into the practical applications of LLMs and LLM-based agents in security patch detection, highlighting their advantage in maintaining robust performance while minimizing false positive rates.

The analysis includes examining user interaction behaviors with LLMs, such as the types of requests made and task completion strategies. Additionally, factors affecting human enhancement and task performance are identified by considering aspects from both humans and LLMs.

title: The rise of large language models (LLMs) has enabled LLM-based applications (a.k.a. AI agents or...
abstract: This paper introduces Parrot, an LLM service system that focuses on the end-to-end experience of LLM-based applications. Parrot proposes Semantic Variable, a unified abstraction to expose application-level knowledge to public LLM services. This abstraction allows for natural programming of LLM applications and opens a new optimization space for end-to-end performance. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that Parrot can achieve up to an order-of-magnitude improvement for popular and practical use cases of LLM applications.

title: Previous work has attempted to boost Large Language Model (LLM) performance on planning and scheduling...
abstract: This paper introduces the LLM-Modulo framework, a compound architecture that combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with sound verifiers to validate their output and re-prompt when necessary. The goal is to ensure the system never outputs fallacious information, making every output guaranteed correct. Results across four scheduling domains show significant performance gains using various models, and modifications to the base configuration are also explored.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) systems are inherently compositional, with individual LLM serving as the core...
abstract: This paper analyzes the security of Large Language Model (LLM) systems, focusing on the ecosystem rather than individual LLMs. The authors propose a multi-layer and multi-step approach to decompose the attack surface into three components: multi-layer security analysis, analysis of constraint existence, and analysis of constraint robustness. Applying this approach to OpenAI GPT4, several security issues were discovered, including vulnerabilities in its safety constraints. A demonstration of an end-to-end attack that could illicitly acquire a user's chat history is provided at https://fzwark.github.io/LLM-System-Attack-Demo/

title: Dense Retrieval (DR) is now considered as a promising tool to enhance the memorization capacity...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Dense Retrieval (DR) as a tool to enhance the memory capacity of Large Language Models (LLM) like GPT3 and GPT-4 by incorporating external memories. The challenge lies in integrating retrieval and generation tasks within a single LLM due to the paradigm discrepancy between them. The paper introduces an efficient LLM-Oriented Retrieval Tuner named LMORT, which separates DR capacity from the base LLM and aligns and optimizes the layers of the LLM towards a unified DR space. This approach aims to achieve an efficient and effective DR without modifying the LLM itself.
categories: The proposed method, LMORT, decouples DR capacity from the base LLM., LMORT aligns and optimizes the layers of the LLM towards a unified DR space., This is done without tuning the LLM itself.

title: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has inspired researchers to integrate them extensively...
abstract: This study explores the integration of large language models (LLMs) into academic workflow, focusing on their use as reviewers in peer-review and research processes. The simulation results show that LLM reviewers systematically inflate scores for LLM-authored papers compared to human-authored ones. They also persistently underrate human-authored papers with critical statements, even after multiple revisions. These biases are attributed to a linguistic feature bias favoring LLM-generated writing styles and an aversion toward critical statements. The study concludes that these findings highlight risks and equity concerns for human authors in academic research if LLMs are deployed in the peer review cycle without caution, but also suggest potential benefits for early-stage researchers and enhancing low-quality papers.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance and tremendous potential across a wide range...
abstract: The paper proposes an efficient approach for deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) by supporting automatic INT4 weight-only quantization and designing a specialized LLM runtime with optimized CPU kernels. This approach is shown to be applicable across popular LLMs such as Llama2, Llama, GPT-NeoX, and delivers extreme inference efficiency on CPUs. The code for this method can be found at: https://github.com/intel/intel-extension-for-transformers.

title: Reasoning is critical for large language models (LLMs) to excel in a wide range of...
categories: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, Proposed token-budget-aware LLM reasoning framework that dynamically adjusts the number of reasoning tokens based on the reasoning complexity of each problem

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in various tasks, yet still produce errors...
abstract: The paper proposes KSOD (Knowledge Supplement for Large Language Models On Demand), a framework that empowers LLMs to improve their capabilities with knowledge-based supervised fine-tuning (SFT). The goal is to reduce errors in domain-specific tasks by supplementing the LLM with identified missing knowledge. The approach involves analyzing errors from the perspective of knowledge deficiency, identifying potential missing knowledge, tuning a knowledge module on a knowledge dataset, verifying the LLM's lack of the identified knowledge, and then supplementing the LLM with the identified knowledge using the knowledge module.
categories: Error analysis from the perspective of knowledge deficiency, Identification of potential missing knowledge in LLMs, Tuning a knowledge module on a knowledge dataset, Verification of LLM's lack of identified knowledge, Supplementing the LLM with the identified knowledge using the knowledge module

title: Recent progress in large language models (LLMs) for code generation has raised serious concerns about...
abstract: The study proposes a detection method, LPcodedec, to identify paraphrase relationships between human-written and LLM-generated code, and to determine which LLM was used for the paraphrasing. LPcodedec outperforms existing baselines in two tasks and shows impressive speedups.

title: Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs)...
categories: Reinforcement Learning (RL) for fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO as a general purpose algorithm, Extended RL algorithms to interact with a dynamic black-box guide LLM called RL with guided feedback (RLGF), Two ways for the guide LLM to interact with the LLM: 1. Generating text which serves as additional starting states for the RL optimization procedure, 2. Completing the partial sentences generated by the LLM being optimized, Treating the guide LLM as an expert to imitate and surpass.

title: In this paper, we conducted a Multi-Perspective Comparative Narrative Analysis (CNA) on three prominent LLMs:...
abstract: A Multi-Perspective Comparative Narrative Analysis was conducted on GPT-3.5, PaLM2, and Llama2 LLMs using identical prompts. The analysis revealed that the three LLMs generated divergent responses to the same prompt, highlighting discrepancies in their ability to comprehend and analyze tasks. Human evaluation served as the gold standard for comparing the performance of these LLMs.
categories: Identical prompts were applied across all three LLMs., The outputs generated by each LLM on specific tasks were evaluated., Human evaluation was used as a benchmark to compare the performance of the LLMs., Four perspectives were utilized for analyzing differences in LLM performance.

title: Test-Time Scaling (TTS) is an important method for improving the performance of Large Language Models...
categories: Test-Time Scaling (TTS)

title: We present a novel framework that improves the reliability of LLM judges by selectively augmenting...
abstract: A novel framework, Auto-Prompt Ensemble (APE), is proposed to enhance the reliability of Large Language Model (LLM) judges. APE selectively augments LLM with auxiliary evaluation dimensions that existing judges may overlook due to missing implicit standards in human assessments. The framework learns evaluation dimensions from failure cases and employs a confidence-based ensemble mechanism to decide when to adopt judgments from additional dimensions via a novel Collective Confidence approach. Extensive experiments show improved reliability of LLM Judge across diverse benchmarks, such as increasing GPT-4o's agreement rate on Reward Bench from 87.2% to 90.5% in the zero-shot setting. Overall, APE bridges the evaluation gap between human and LLM judges by leveraging test-time computation.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are evolving at an unprecedented pace and have exhibited considerable capability...
categories: Corex, Debate mode, Review mode, Retrieve mode

title: We consider excitations of LLM geometries described by coloring the LLM plane with concentric black...
abstract: The abstract discusses a study on excitations in LLM (Lattice Landau-Ginzburg Model) geometries. These excitations are represented by coloring the LLM plane with concentric black rings, where certain closed string excitations are localized at the edges of these rings. The paper aims to construct operators dual to these closed string excitations and reproduce string theory predictions for magnon energies using one-loop anomalous dimensions. The key point is that these operators are linear combinations of restricted Schur polynomials. The distinction between the background and the excitation is achieved through the choice of subgroup and representations used to construct the operator.
categories: Coloring the LLM plane with concentric black rings to represent excitations, Constructing operators dual to closed string excitations, Reproducing string theory predictions for magnon energies using one-loop anomalous dimensions, Utilizing linear combinations of restricted Schur polynomials in operator construction

title: Objective: To enhance health literacy and accessibility of health information for a diverse patient population...
Knowledge graphs have emerged as a popular method for injecting up-to-date, factual knowledge into large...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of knowledge graphs to inject up-to-date, factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs). The textualization process of converting knowledge graphs into text for LLM processing is explored, but its impact on performance is found to be under-explored. The introduction of KG-LLM-Bench, a comprehensive and extensible benchmark spanning five knowledge graph understanding tasks, is presented. The benchmark evaluates how different encoding strategies affect performance across various base models. Extensive experiments were conducted with seven language models and five textualization strategies to provide insights for optimizing LLM performance on KG reasoning tasks.
categories: Conversion of knowledge graphs into text that the LLM can process in context., Evaluation of different encoding strategies across various base models using the KG-LLM-Bench benchmark.

title: LLM-driven autonomous agents have emerged as a promising direction in recent years. However, many of...
Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have established them as agentic systems capable of...
abstract: This work investigates the risks associated with the misuse of Large Language Models (LLMs) in cyberattacks involving personal data. The study aims to understand how potent LLM agents can be when directed to conduct cyberattacks, the enhancement of attacks by web-based tools, and the ease of launching cyberattacks using LLM agents. Three attack scenarios were examined: collection of Personally Identifiable Information (PII), generation of impersonation posts, and creation of spear-phishing emails.
categories: Examination of the effectiveness of LLM agents in cyberattacks by analyzing their performance in the three attack scenarios., Assessment of how up to 95.9% precision was achieved in collecting PII, 93.9% authenticity in generated impersonation posts, and a boosted click rate of phishing links by 46.67%.

title: The success and wide adoption of generative AI (GenAI), particularly large language models (LLMs), has...
abstract: The success and widespread use of generative AI (GenAI), specifically large language models (LLMs), has caught the attention of cybercriminals who aim to exploit these models, steal sensitive data, or disrupt services. Providing security for LLM-based systems is challenging due to both traditional software application threats and those targeting LLMs and their integration needing to be addressed. This survey provides an analysis of the security and privacy concerns associated with LLM-based systems through a systematic review and comprehensive categorization of threats and defensive strategies across the entire software and LLM life cycles.
categories: Performing a systematic review and comprehensive categorization of threats, Analyzing real-world scenarios with distinct characteristics of LLM usage, spanning from development to operation, Classifying threats according to their severity level and the scenarios they pertain, Mapping defense strategies to the corresponding life cycle phase and possible attack strategies they attenuate

title: There is increasing interest in the adoption of LLMs in HCI research. However, LLMs may...
categories: Evaluation of LLM (GPT-4) in identifying logical fallacies by comparing to a labeled dataset., Assessment of accuracy for the identified logical fallacies.

title: We develop KnowThyself, an agentic assistant that advances large language model (LLM) interpretability. Existing tools...
categories: KnowThyself utilizes an orchestrator large language model (LLM) that reformulates user queries., An agent router directs the reformulated queries to specialized modules based on their nature., The outputs from these modules are contextualized into coherent explanations by KnowThyself.

title: With the rise of large language models (LLMs), researchers are increasingly exploring their applications in...
abstract: This survey investigates the current practice and solutions for Large Language Models (LLMs) and LLM-based agents in software engineering. It provides a summary of six key topics: requirement engineering, code generation, autonomous decision-making, software design, test generation, and software maintenance. The work of LLMs and LLM-based agents is reviewed and differentiated from these topics. Additionally, models and benchmarks used are discussed, offering a comprehensive analysis of their applications and effectiveness in software engineering.

title: LLMs as intelligent agents are being increasingly applied in scenarios where human interactions are involved,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the evaluation of Large Language Models (LLMs) as intelligent agents in human interaction scenarios, focusing on cultural faithfulness across regions. The study investigates biases present in the cultural representations of LLM outputs using a renowned cultural values questionnaire and human ground truth scores. Findings show that all models represent a moderate cultural middle ground but with the United States as the best-aligned country and GLM-4 having the best ability to align to cultural values. The study also reveals that models, regardless of origin, align better with the US than China. Lastly, it highlights the influence of model origin, prompt language, and value dimensions on cultural output, calling for better alignment of LLMs to various cultures and further discussions about potential cultural bias propagation and the need for more culturally adaptable models.

title: We study an auction setting in which bidders bid for placement of their content within...
abstract: The study proposes a novel factorized framework where an auction module and an LLM module collaborate via a prediction model. The goal is to produce welfare-maximizing summary outputs in an incentive-compatible manner. The authors provide theoretical analysis and synthetic experiments to demonstrate the system's feasibility, validity, and compare its welfare with traditional ad settings.

title: Machine learning (ML) techniques for optimizing data management problems have been extensively studied and widely...
categories: The methods described in the abstract include LLMDB, an LLM-enhanced data management paradigm. This system aims to embed domain-specific knowledge to avoid hallucination by LLM fine-tuning and prompt engineering. To reduce the high cost of LLMs, vector databases are used, which provide semantic search and caching abilities. For improving task accuracy, an LLM agent is introduced that provides multiple-round inference and pipeline executions.

title: Currently, a considerable research effort is devoted to comparing LLMs to a group of human...
categories: Research efforts should focus on effectively characterizing the safe use of LLMs in clinical settings across the rapid development of novel LLM models., Developing strategies that enable efficient work of humans with LLMs in a nearly symbiotic manner is suggested as a method to mitigate potential risks and improve patient care.

title: Although the integration of large language models (LLMs) into robotics has unlocked transformative capabilities, it...
Large language models are increasingly used as judges (LLM-as-a-judge) to evaluate model outputs at scale,...
abstract: The paper introduces Bridge, a unified statistical framework that aims to bridge the gap between human and large language model (LLM) evaluations. The framework is designed to work under both absolute scoring and pairwise comparison paradigms, positing a latent human preference score for each prompt-response pair. It models LLM deviations as linear transformations of covariates capturing sources of discrepancies between humans and LLMs.
categories: Bridge framework explicitly bridges human and LLM evaluations, It posits a latent human preference score for each prompt-response pair, Models LLM deviations as linear transformations of covariates, Offers a simple and principled framework for refining LLM ratings, Provides an efficient fitting algorithm with asymptotic guarantees for statistical inference

title: If we use LLMs as judges to evaluate the complex decisions of other LLMs, who...
abstract: This research aims to improve the evaluation of LLMs by leveraging logical consistency among multiple models when they disagree on tasks. By detecting and flagging inconsistencies, the system can alert users to potential misalignments within an ensemble of judges, thus ensuring a more reliable assessment of grading abilities.

title: As Large Language Models (LLMs) become integrated into high-stakes domains, there is a growing need...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development of a dynamic, learning-based framework named LLM Jury-on-Demand for scalable and context-aware evaluation of Large Language Models (LLMs) in high-stakes domains. The goal is to create an adaptive evaluation system that overcomes limitations of human evaluation such as slowness, costliness, biases, and lack of adaptability. The proposed method trains reliability predictors using token distributions, embeddings, and structural input features to assess when LLM judges will agree with human experts.
categories: Dynamically selecting an optimal jury of the most reliable judges for each data point, Aggregating scores of these judges using their reliability as weights, Training a set of reliability predictors to assess agreement between LLM judges and human experts

title: Information removal or suppression in large language models (LLMs) is a desired functionality, useful in...
Routing large language models (LLMs) is a new paradigm that uses a router to recommend...
abstract: This paper presents Routing Large Language Models (LLMs), a new paradigm that uses a router to recommend the best LLM for a given input from a pool of candidates. The study, involving over 8,500 LLMs, shows a significant enhancement in performance with an efficient router as the number of candidates increases, sometimes surpassing the performance of the best single model and many strong existing LLMs. However, the development of routers has been hindered due to the lack of comprehensive and open-source benchmarks. To address this, the authors introduce RouterEval, a tailored benchmark for router research with over 200,000,000 performance records for 12 popular LLM evaluations across various areas such as commonsense reasoning and semantic understanding, based on over 8,500 LLMs. Extensive evaluations of existing Routing LLM methods using RouterEval reveal that most have significant room for improvement.

title: LLM app stores have seen rapid growth, leading to the proliferation of numerous custom LLM...
abstract: This study proposes a three-layer concern framework to identify potential security risks in Large Language Model (LLM) apps, categorizing them as those with abusive potential, malicious intent, and exploitable vulnerabilities. Over five months, researchers collected 786,036 LLM apps from six major app stores and found numerous issues: 15,146 apps had misleading descriptions, 1,366 collected sensitive personal information against their privacy policies, and 15,996 generated harmful content like hate speech, self-harm, extremism, etc. Furthermore, the potential for LLM apps to facilitate malicious activities was evaluated, revealing that 616 apps could be used for malware generation and phishing. The findings underscore the urgent need for robust regulatory frameworks and enhanced enforcement mechanisms.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have significantly improved their ability to perform tasks in the field...
abstract: This study investigates the communication skills of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation tasks, arguing that they should ask clarifying questions to address issues such as inconsistency, ambiguity, and incompleteness in problem descriptions. The research presents a new benchmark, HumanEvalComm, for evaluating LLMs' communication skills.
categories: The study defines the communication skills of LLMs as their ability to ask clarifying questions when the description of the code generation problem has issues., A new benchmark, HumanEvalComm, is created by modifying problem descriptions according to three issues: inconsistency, ambiguity, incompleteness., New evaluation metrics such as Communication Rate and Good Question Rate are defined., The experiment involves different Code LLMs and a new LLM agent approach, Okanagan, to identify and ask questions in ambiguous parts from code and descriptions for further refining the generated code., Results are discussed by comparing Code LLMs and Okanagan with the findings.

title: We compute sigma model solutions for rigidly rotating open strings suspended between giant gravitons in...
As the range of applications for Large Language Models (LLMs) continues to grow, the demand...
abstract: The paper discusses the need for effective serving solutions for Large Language Models (LLMs) as their applications expand. It highlights that no single model can optimally address all tasks and costs. To overcome this, LLM routing systems have been developed, but a standardized benchmark for evaluating their performance is lacking. The authors introduce RouterBench, an evaluation framework to assess the efficacy of LLM routing systems, along with a dataset for supporting the development of routing strategies. They also propose a theoretical framework for LLM routing and compare various routing approaches through RouterBench. This work formalizes and advances the development of LLM routing systems and sets a standard for their assessment, promoting more accessible and economically viable LLM deployments. The work is available at https://github.com/withmartian/routerbench.

abstract: The study proposes a method to pair Language Models (LLMs) with formal analysis methods for providing accessible, relevant feedback for Human-Robot Interaction (HRI) tasks. This approach involves integrating task assessment outcomes and contextual information into an LLM prompt, resulting in a useful set of recommendations for learners to improve their performance.

title: Understanding the reliability of large language models (LLMs) has recently garnered significant attention. Given LLMs'...
categories: The methods presented in the paper include a probabilistic model for the joint performance distribution of a sequence of Large Language Models (LLMs). This model enables rational tuning of the confidence thresholds of an LLM cascade using continuous optimization. The authors use a parametric Markov-copula model as opposed to Bayesian optimization, which is compared and found to yield more favorable error-cost trade-offs.

title: While Large Language Models (LLMs) can solve many NLP tasks in zero-shot settings, applications involving...
abstract: The abstract discusses an approach called LLM Dynamic Planner (LLM-DP), which combines Large Language Models (LLMs) and traditional planners to address complex planning tasks in the context of embodied agents. The proposed method addresses the limitations of both symbolic planners and LLMs by leveraging their respective strengths for optimal results.
categories: The LLM Dynamic Planner (LLM-DP) consists of an LLM working alongside a traditional planner to solve embodied tasks. It is designed to handle action-descriptions, with the goal of solving Alfworld more efficiently than a naive LLM ReAct baseline.

title: Harnessing pre-trained LLMs to improve ASR systems, particularly for low-resource languages, is now an emerging...
categories: SALSA is a proposed method that couples the decoder layers of the Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) system to the Language Model (LLM) decoder., The coupling advances both decoders synchronously, and is performed with a simple projection of the last decoder state., To handle the mismatch between the tokenizers of the LLM and ASR systems, SALSA uses cascading tokenization with respect to the LLM and ASR vocabularies.

title: Negotiation is the basis of social interactions; humans negotiate everything from the price of cars...
abstract: The paper investigates the negotiation abilities of large language models (LLMs), which are increasingly being used as agents for human users. The study introduces NegotiationArena, a framework to evaluate and probe LLM's negotiation skills. Three types of scenarios are implemented: resource allocation (ultimatum games), resource aggregation (trading games), and good trading (price negotiations). The research finds that LLMs can enhance their negotiation outcomes by using specific behavioral tactics, such as feigning despair, resulting in a 20% improvement against GPT-4. Additionally, the paper identifies irrational negotiation behaviors exhibited by LLMs, some of which mirror human behaviors. The findings suggest that LLMs can employ tactics to improve their negotiation outcomes and exhibit irrational behavior similar to humans.
categories: Development of NegotiationArena: a framework for evaluating and probing the negotiation abilities of LLM agents., Implementation of three types of scenarios: resource allocation (ultimatum games), resource aggregation (trading games), and good trading (price negotiations)., Each scenario permits multiple turns of flexible dialogues between LLM agents to facilitate complex negotiations., Assessment of LLM's behaviors in the context of these scenarios.

title: Direct alignment from preferences (DAP) has emerged as a promising paradigm for aligning large language...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development of an online Preference Optimization (BPO) method for aligning large language models (LLMs) to human desiderata. The proposed approach aims to ensure that the learned LLM adheres to the proximity of the behavior LLM, which collects the training samples. This is done by emphasizing the importance of constructing a proper trust region for LLM alignment.
categories: Online Preference Optimization in proximity to the Behavior LLM (BPO), Integrating BPO with various DAP methods

title: NeMo Guardrails is an open-source toolkit for easily adding programmable guardrails to LLM-based conversational systems....
abstract: NeMo Guardrails is an open-source toolkit for adding programmable guardrails to LLM-based conversational systems. These guardrails are used to control the output of an LLM, including topics discussed, dialogue paths followed, language style, etc. The toolkit allows developers to add user-defined, independent guardrails to LLM applications using a runtime inspired from dialogue management.
categories: Embedding guardrails into specific models during training through model alignment, Using a runtime inspired from dialogue management for adding programmable guardrails

title: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown exceptional performance in various tasks, one of their...
abstract: The paper discusses a method to detect the truthfulness of statements generated by Large Language Models (LLMs), addressing their shortcoming of producing inaccurate information with confidence. The approach involves training a classifier that predicts the probability of a statement being truthful based on the hidden layer activations of the LLM as it reads or generates the statement. Experiments demonstrate that the trained classifier achieves an average accuracy of 71% to 83%, depending on the LLM base model, in labeling true versus false sentences.
categories: Training a classifier based on hidden layer activations of the LLM., Using the trained classifier to predict the probability of a statement's truthfulness., Evaluating the classifier's performance using a set of test sentences, half true and half false.

title: Although large language models (LLMs) have achieved significant success in natural language processing, they still...
abstract: This paper explores a novel method called Scaled ReAttention (SRA) to improve the long-context comprehension abilities of large language models (LLMs), without requiring additional resources. The proposed SRA method strategically manipulates attention scores during inference to enhance LLMs' ability to interpret and retrieve information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that integrating SRA significantly boosts LLMs' performance on various downstream tasks.

title: Online behavioural research faces an emerging threat as participants increasingly turn to large language models...
abstract: The paper discusses the emerging threat of Large Language Models (LLMs) in online behavioral research, termed as LLM Pollution. It identifies three variants: Partial LLM Mediation, Full LLM Delegation, and LLM Spillover. These variants compromise sample authenticity, introduce biases, and undermine the epistemic grounding of online research. Partial LLM Mediation occurs when participants use LLMs for specific task aspects, leading to misinterpretation of LLM-shaped outputs as human ones. Full LLM Delegation arises when agentic LLMs complete studies without human oversight, undermining the central premise of human-subject research. LLM Spillover signifies human participants altering their behavior due to anticipated LLM presence in online studies. While Partial Mediation and Full Delegation form a continuum of increasing automation, LLM Spillover reflects second-order reactivity effects.
categories: The paper identifies the three variants through analysis and discussion of their potential impact on online behavioral research.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are pivotal in advancing natural language processing but often struggle with...
abstract: This research introduces a method for extending computed tokens in the Chain-of-Thought (CoT) process to optimize the attention mechanism of Large Language Models (LLMs). By analyzing attention patterns across layers, the authors identify inefficiencies and propose an algorithm to emulate early layer attention patterns across downstream layers. The study demonstrates that this approach improves LLM's reasoning capabilities, particularly in non-STEM domains, paving the way for future innovations in LLM design.

title: We introduce a novel framework for simulating macroeconomic expectations using LLM Agents. By constructing LLM...
abstract: The study presents a novel framework for simulating macroeconomic expectations using LLM Agents. The framework replicates three representative survey experiments involving various types of economic agents and their corresponding expectations. Results indicate that the expectations simulated by LLM Agents are more homogeneous than human ones, but they consistently outperform simpler LLMs and exhibit human-like mental mechanisms. Findings suggest these capabilities originate from the contributions of their components, providing guidelines for the architectural design of such agents.
categories: Construction of LLM Agents equipped with multiple functional modules to replicate survey experiments, Simulation of expectations across different types of economic agents, Evaluation of the performance and capabilities of LLM Agents compared to simple prompt engineering methods

title: The emergence of large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT has increased interest in their use...
categories: The study uses BOLT, a proof-of-concept computational framework, to systematically assess the conversational behavior of LLM therapists. The assessment is quantitative and measures LLM behavior across 13 psychotherapeutic approaches using in-context learning methods. To compare LLMs against human therapy, the study evaluates their behavior based on Motivational Interviewing therapy.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have become essential for applications such as text summarization, sentiment analysis,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into relational database management systems for enhanced querying and advanced data processing. Companies like Amazon, Databricks, Google, and Snowflake offer LLM invocation directly within SQL for boosted data insights. However, open-source solutions are currently limited in functionality and performance. This work presents an initial exploration of three systems - two open-source and one enterprise platform - using five representative queries to analyze the functional, performance, and scalability limits in today's SQL-invoked LLM integrations.
categories: Exploration of two open-source systems and one enterprise platform using five representative queries

title: Engaging in the deliberate generation of abnormal outputs from Large Language Models (LLMs) by attacking...
categories: The study employed a formal qualitative methodology, specifically interviewing dozens of practitioners from a broad range of backgrounds who contribute to the novel work of attempting to cause LLMs to fail. The research questions focused on defining LLM red teaming, uncovering motivations and goals for performing the activity, and characterizing the strategies people use when attacking LLMs. A taxonomy of 12 strategies and 35 different techniques of attacking LLMs was identified.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across various machine learning tasks. Yet the...
categories: BitMoD is an algorithm-hardware co-design solution for efficient LLM acceleration at low weight precision. On the algorithm side, it introduces fine-grained data type adaptation that quantizes a group of weights using different numerical data types. Through the careful design of these new data types, BitMoD is able to quantize LLM weights to very low precision (e.g., 4 bits and 3 bits) while maintaining high accuracy. On the hardware side, BitMoD employs a bit-serial processing element that easily supports multiple numerical precisions and data types. The hardware design includes two key innovations: First, it employs a unified representation to process different weight data types, thus reducing the hardware cost. Second, it adopts a bit-serial dequantization unit to rescale the per-group partial sum with minimal hardware overhead.

title: The fine-tuning of pre-trained large language models (LLMs) using reinforcement learning (RL) is generally formulated...
Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable reasoning capabilities, especially when prompted to generate intermediate...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new reasoning framework for large language models (LLMs), named RAP (Reasoning via Planning). The goal is to enable LLMs to perform deliberate planning, similar to human brains, by incorporating a principled planning algorithm. This framework repurposes the LLM as both a world model and a reasoning agent, allowing it to explore various reasoning paths, anticipate future states, and refine existing steps. The research focuses on addressing the limitations of LLMs in generating action plans, performing complex math, logical, and commonsense reasoning. The key issue is that LLMs lack an internal world model to predict the state of the environment and simulate long-term outcomes.
categories: RAP utilizes a planning algorithm based on Monto Carlo Tree Search to guide the LLM as both a world model and a reasoning agent. The LLM incrementally builds a reasoning tree under this guidance, with task-specific rewards., During reasoning, RAP balances exploration vs exploitation, obtaining a high-reward reasoning path efficiently., The proposed framework is applied to various challenging reasoning problems like plan generation, math reasoning, and logical inference.

title: This paper presents a method to analyze the inference patterns used by Large Language Models...

title: Large language models provide rich semantic priors and strong reasoning capabilities, making them promising auxiliary...
Integration of audio perception into large language models (LLMs) is an emerging research area for...
abstract: The abstract discusses the integration of audio perception into large language models (LLMs) for machine listening applications. The research focuses on efficient transfer of rich audio semantics from audio encoders to LLMs, which is currently underexplored. The main contribution is the introduction of Lightweight Audio LLM Integration (LAL), an alternative method that injects audio representations into the attention mechanism at selected LLM layers, bypassing the feed-forward module. This approach encodes rich audio semantics at an appropriate level of abstraction for integration into different transformer blocks, reducing computational overhead compared to existing approaches. Additionally, a hybrid integration approach called PAL is proposed, which combines Prepend to the LLM's input token space (PLITS) with LAL, offering improved computational and memory efficiency. Under identical training conditions, LAL consistently matches or outperforms existing integration approaches across multiple base LLMs and tasks, showing significant improvements over a strong PLITS baseline. PAL also matches or exceeds PLITS performance while providing better computational and memory efficiency.
categories: Prepend to the LLM's input token space (PLITS) integration, Lightweight Audio LLM Integration (LAL), PAL - a hybrid integration approach combining PLITS and LAL

title: Large language model (LLM) applications are evolving beyond simple chatbots into dynamic, general-purpose agentic programs,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the evolution of large language model (LLM) applications from chatbots to dynamic, general-purpose agentic programs that assist AI agents in complex tasks. It highlights an issue with existing LLM serving systems: they do not consider dependencies between programs and calls, leading to long wait times for programs. The proposed solution is Autellix, a new LLM serving system that treats programs as first-class citizens, aiming to minimize end-to-end latencies. Autellix intercepts LLM calls submitted by programs and enriches schedulers with program-level context.
categories: Proposed two scheduling algorithms: one for single-threaded programs and another for distributed programs, These algorithms preempt and prioritize LLM calls based on the programs' previously completed calls

title: The training scale of large language models (LLMs) has reached tens of thousands of GPUs...
LLM cascades deploy small LLMs to answer most queries, limiting the use of large and...
categories: LLM cascades deploy small LLMs to answer most queries, allowing large and expensive LLMs only for difficult queries. The introduction of abstention in these cascades allows the system to completely avoid answering difficult queries in risk-sensitive domains like finance or medicine. Early abstention is proposed, enabling earlier models to anticipate abstention decisions by expensive and slow models.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential in enhancing dense retrieval through query augmentation....
categories: ExpandR is a unified Large Language Model (LLM)-augmented dense retrieval framework., The framework jointly optimizes both the LLM and the retriever., The LLM generates semantically rich query expansions to enhance the retriever's training., Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is used to train the LLM, guided by a reward function that balances retrieval effectiveness and generation consistency.
