title: Large language models (LLMs) have been shown to face hallucination issues due to the data...
abstract: The paper investigates the hallucination issues in large language models (LLMs) and their potential to mimic human cognitive biases for exhibiting irrational social intelligence. It introduces CogMir, an open-ended Multi-LLM Agents framework that utilizes LLMs' hallucination properties to assess and enhance their social intelligence through cognitive biases. Experimental results suggest a high consistency between LLM Agents and humans in irrational and prosocial decision-making under uncertain conditions.
categories: The research combines practical social science experiments with theoretical insights to study the irrational behavior among contemporary LLM Agents., CogMir is proposed as a framework that evaluates and enhances the social intelligence of LLM Agents through their hallucination properties.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) with hundreds of billions of parameters have exhibited human-like intelligence by...
categories: Introduction of AIPsychoBench, a specialized benchmark to assess the psychological properties of Large Language Models (LLMs)., Use of a lightweight role-playing prompt to bypass LLM alignment., Improvement in average effective response rate from 70.12% to 90.40%.

title: Passive tracking methods, such as phone and wearable sensing, have become dominant in monitoring human...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of passive tracking methods like phones and wearables for monitoring human behaviors in ubiquitous computing studies. While there have been advancements in machine-learning approaches to interpret raw sensor data, a gap remains in translating these into meaningful, high-level, context-aware insights. To bridge this gap, a user study was conducted with 21 experts to explore solutions for the challenging sensemaking process. A prototype system named Vital Insight (VI) was developed using a human-centered design process to aid human-in-the-loop inference and visualizations of multi-modal passive sensing data from smartphones and wearables. The study observes experts' interactions with VI and develops an expert sensemaking model, identifying how they move between direct data representations and AI-supported inferences to explore, question, and validate insights. Design implications for future AI-augmented visualization systems are also synthesized and discussed.

title: With a recent trend of using Large Language Models (LLMs) for different applications within smart...
categories: DILEMMA framework, Integer Linear Programming problem formulation for optimizing layer placement and quantization, Layer-wise quantization, Knowledge distillation for LLM performance control

title: Large language models (LLMs) can label data faster and cheaper than humans for various NLP...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for faster, cheaper data labeling in NLP tasks. However, it highlights the limitation of LLMs in understanding complex, sociocultural, or domain-specific contexts, which can lead to incorrect annotations. To overcome this, a collaborative approach between humans and LLMs is proposed for reliable and high-quality labels. The abstract introduces MEGAnno+, a system that facilitates such collaboration by offering effective agent management, convenient LLM annotation, and exploratory verification of LLM labels by humans.

title: Large language models (LLMs) inference is both expensive and slow. Local caching of responses offers...
abstract: The abstract discusses Mnimi, a cache design pattern for large language models (LLMs) that ensures statistical integrity while supporting modular workflows. The goal is to reduce cost and latency of LLM queries, enhance reproducibility, and provide flexibility for experimentation in research contexts. Mnimi encapsulates statistical constraints within the type of LLM references, enabling users to manage and transform these types according to their algorithm's scope and requirements. The abstract mentions its application in code-related scenarios, impacting performance metrics like Pass@k and uncertainty estimation, as well as algorithms such as program repair loops and retries.
categories: Mnimi design pattern is implemented using Python, decorators, and iterators over infinite sequences., The case study presented uses SpecFix, a recent automated program specification repair system.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications that demand complex reasoning. To...
abstract: The paper presents GAMEBoT, a gaming arena for evaluating the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). GAMEBoT decomposes complex reasoning in games into predefined modular subproblems and uses Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompts to guide LLMs through these subproblems before action selection. The study also develops rule-based algorithms to generate ground truth for the subproblems, enabling rigorous validation of the LLMs' intermediate reasoning steps. This approach allows for evaluation of both the quality of final actions and the accuracy of the underlying reasoning process. GAMEBoT addresses challenges such as insufficient interpretability, performance saturation or data contamination in current LLM reasoning benchmarks. The research team benchmarked 17 prominent LLMs across eight games, encompassing various strategic abilities and game characteristics. The results suggest that GAMEBoT presents a significant challenge to these models, even when they are provided with detailed CoT prompts.

title: A large amount of work has been done in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) for modeling and...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new approach for fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). This approach, named Multi-Agent Group Relative Policy Optimization (MAGRPO), is designed to facilitate collaboration among agents. The experiments conducted on LLM writing and coding collaboration demonstrate the efficiency of the agents in generating high-quality responses through effective cooperation. The code for this approach is available at https://github.com/OpenMLRL/CoMLRL.

title: Instruction-tuning is a widely adopted finetuning method that enables large language models (LLMs) to generate...
abstract: The study investigates the impact of instruction-tuning on aligning large language models (LLMs) with human language processing mechanisms. It explores two types of alignment: brain alignment and behavioral alignment, focusing on reading tasks involving naturalistic stories and sentences. Results show that instruction-tuning generally enhances brain alignment (by approximately 6%), but has no significant effect on behavioral alignment. The study also identifies factors contributing to the improvement in brain alignment, such as a strong positive correlation between brain alignment and model size, as well as performance on tasks requiring world knowledge understanding.
categories: Assessment of 25 vanilla and instruction-tuned LLMs on three datasets involving human reading tasks, Evaluation of brain alignment using the similarity of LLM internal representations to neural activity in the human language system

title: Recent advancements on Large Language Models (LLMs) enable AI Agents to automatically generate and execute...
categories: Proposal of a novel 'Formal-LLM' framework for LLM-based agents., Integration of expressiveness of natural language and precision of formal language., Expression of requirements or constraints for the planning process as an automaton., Stack-based LLM plan generation process under the supervision of the automaton.

title: The potential of using Large Language Models (LLMs) themselves to evaluate LLM outputs offers a...
abstract: The study investigates the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) as judges for evaluating their own outputs in various contexts. The research finds a strong correlation between LLM-as-a-judge and human judges in general instruction following. However, for specialized knowledge tasks, the validity is uncertain. The study employed a mixed-methods approach, comparing LLM and subject matter expert (SME) evaluations on domain-specific tasks in dietetics and mental health fields. SMEs agreed with LLM judges 68% of the time in dietetics and 64% in mental health when evaluating overall preference. However, there were variations in SME-LLM agreement across domain-specific aspect questions. The study highlights the need for human experts in complex, knowledge-specific tasks. Findings indicate that while LLMs can be useful for general instruction following, they may not provide the depth of understanding required for specialized tasks.
categories: Mixed-methods approach: Pairwise comparisons between subject matter experts (SMEs) and LLMs evaluating outputs from domain-specific tasks., Two distinct fields: Dietetics with registered dietitian experts, Mental health with clinical psychologist experts., Focus on overall preference and domain-specific aspect questions.

title: We introduce a novel reinforcement learning framework of LLM agents named AGILE (AGent that Interacts...
Despite efforts to expand the knowledge of large language models (LLMs), knowledge gaps -- missing...
abstract: The research focuses on addressing knowledge gaps in LLMs due to the evolving nature of knowledge. It introduces two novel approaches based on model collaboration, where LLMs probe other LLMs for knowledge gaps either cooperatively or competitively. These methods significantly outperform existing ones by up to 19.3% in terms of abstaining from generating low-confidence outputs. The study also suggests that these mechanisms could help identify failure cases in retrieval augmentation and pinpoint knowledge gaps in multi-hop reasoning.

title: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated their potential as autonomous agents across...
abstract: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown their potential as autonomous agents across various tasks, including playing games. In this study, the authors investigate whether LLMs can measure game difficulty. A general game-testing framework using LLM agents is proposed and tested on Wordle and Slay the Spire. The results indicate that while LLMs may not match human performance, they show a significant correlation with game difficulty as rated by humans when guided by simple prompting techniques. This suggests LLMs could be useful in measuring game difficulty during development. The authors also provide guidelines for incorporating LLMs into the game testing process.

title: As Large Language Models (LLMs) have risen in prominence over the past few years, there...
categories: The development of a benchmark, DIF (Demographic Implicit Fairness), which evaluates preexisting LLM logic and math problem datasets with sociodemographic personas., A statistical robustness check using a null model to combine with the evaluation process.

title: The pervasive use of textual formats in the documentation of software requirements presents a great...
abstract: The abstract discusses the application of large language models (LLMs) in software engineering tasks, specifically for automating code snippet generation from well-structured requirements documents. The LLM is tailored with knowledge, heuristics, and instructions relevant to software development process, requirements analysis, object-oriented design, and test-driven development. A 'Progressive Prompting' method is introduced for stepwise interaction between the LLM and software engineers. The LLM is demonstrated to interpret user requirements, create object-oriented models, generate unit tests, and produce code solutions through a web project case study.
categories: Interpreting provided requirements to extract functional requirements, Creating object-oriented models based on extracted functional requirements, Generating unit tests based on the object-oriented designs, Producing code based on the object-oriented designs

title: Data augmentation is a critical component of deep learning pipelines, enhancing model generalization by increasing...
Large Language Models (LLMs) have been widely applied in summarization due to their speedy and...
abstract: The abstract proposes an intermediate step, a schematic visual workspace for human sensemaking, to guide and refine the summarization process by Large Language Models (LLMs). This step aims to enhance summarization through GPT-4 by investigating its potential impact using visual workspaces. Human-generated visual workspaces are evaluated for their effect on LLM-generated summarization of a textual sensemaking dataset with a ground truth summary. The study also categorizes various types of extractable information from typical human workspaces that can be utilized to steer the LLM summarization process. Results show that such workspaces help align an LLM with the ground truth, leading to more accurate summarization results compared to those without the workspaces.

title: The era of Large Language Models (LLMs) raises new demands for automatic evaluation metrics, which...
abstract: The paper introduces RepEval, a metric for evaluating text based on the projection of Large Language Model (LLM) representations. It is designed to be adaptable to various application scenarios and requires minimal resources compared to other LLM-based metrics. The authors demonstrate the effectiveness of RepEval through experiments on fourteen datasets across two evaluation tasks, showing that it performs better than existing methods.

title: Recent progress in large language models (LLMs) has focused on producing responses that meet human...
abstract: This paper discusses the challenges in aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values and expectations, due to the complexity of human values and the narrow nature of technological approaches. It proposes that improving LLM alignment requires incorporating insights from societal alignment frameworks, including social, economic, and contractual alignment. The paper investigates the role of uncertainty in LLM alignment and offers an alternative view on LLM alignment, framing the underspecified nature of its objectives as an opportunity rather than a flaw.
categories: Discussing potential solutions drawn from social, economic, and contractual alignment domains, Investigating how uncertainty manifests in LLM alignment

title: As large language models (LLMs) grow increasingly adept at processing unstructured text data, they offer...
abstract: This paper investigates the adoption of large language models (LLMs) in a large technology company's data curation tasks. It highlights a shift from traditional heuristic-based, bottom-up approaches to more insight-driven, top-down workflows that leverage LLMs. The study also reveals an evolution in data understanding, with practitioners now combining 'golden datasets' created by subject experts with LLM-generated 'silver' datasets and rigorously validated 'super golden' datasets curated by diverse experts. This research underscores the transformative role of LLMs in large-scale analysis of unstructured data and suggests opportunities for further tool development.

title: Recent advances in LLM agentic systems have improved the automation of offensive security tasks, particularly...
abstract: The abstract discusses recent improvements in LLM agentic systems for automating offensive security tasks, particularly in Capture the Flag (CTF) challenges. The research presents CTFJudge, a framework using LLMs as judges to evaluate agent trajectories and provide detailed evaluations across CTF solving steps. A novel metric, CTF Competency Index (CCI), is proposed for partial correctness. The paper also examines how LLM hyperparameters affect agent performance and task planning. To facilitate rapid evaluation, CTFTiny, a curated benchmark of 50 representative CTF challenges, has been made open source. The findings identify optimal multi-agent coordination settings and lay the groundwork for future LLM agent research in cybersecurity.
categories: CTFJudge framework using LLMs as judges to analyze agent trajectories and evaluate CTF solving steps, Proposal of a novel metric, CTF Competency Index (CCI) for partial correctness, Examination of how LLM hyperparameters influence agent performance and automated cybersecurity task planning, CTFTiny, a curated benchmark of 50 representative CTF challenges for rapid evaluation

title: The strategic significance of Large Language Models (LLMs) in economic expansion, innovation, societal development, and...
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) from both the US and China in English and Chinese contexts. The research presents a comprehensive evaluation framework consisting of natural language proficiency, disciplinary expertise, safety, and responsibility. The key findings show GPT 4-Turbo leading in English contexts while Ernie-Bot 4 excels in Chinese. The study emphasizes disparities across languages and tasks, underlining the need for nuanced model development. Sino-US collaboration is suggested as valuable for advancing LLM technology. The research provides insights for policymakers and businesses regarding strategic investments in LLMs, with future work expanding to include multimodal capabilities and business applications.

title: Recent releases of pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs) have gained considerable traction, yet research on...
abstract: This study investigates approaches for fine-tuning and leveraging domain-specific Large Language Models (LLMs), with a focus on the financial sector. It discusses trends in LLMs, foundational models, and methods for domain-specific pre-training. The study details dataset selection, preprocessing, model choice, and considerations crucial for LLM fine-tuning in finance. It explores unique characteristics of financial data such as the construction of domain-specific vocabularies and security & regulatory compliance issues. Practical applications of LLM fine-tuning are demonstrated through various financial cases like stock price prediction, sentiment analysis of financial news, automated document processing, research, information extraction, and customer service enhancement. The study assesses the potential of LLMs in the financial domain, identifies limitations, and proposes directions for improvement.
categories: Fine-tuning approaches for domain-specific LLMs, Selection and preprocessing of datasets, Choosing appropriate models for fine-tuning, Construction of domain-specific vocabularies, Considerations for security and regulatory compliance, Procedure and implementation for generating domain-specific LLMs in finance

title: This paper reveals that many state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs) lack hierarchical knowledge about our...
categories: The research utilizes approximately one million four-choice visual question answering (VQA) tasks derived from six taxonomies and four image datasets. These tasks are used to evaluate and potentially improve the hierarchical understanding of large language models in the context of vision., Furthermore, the paper fine-tunes a vision LLM using the constructed VQA tasks to observe its impact on the model's hierarchical consistency.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated an alarming ability to impersonate humans in conversation, raising...
abstract: The study evaluates text-based prompts as challenges to expose Large Language Models (LLMs) imposters in real-time. An open-source benchmark dataset is compiled and released with both 'implicit' and 'explicit' challenges. Explicit challenges were found to be more effective, detecting LLMs in 78.4% of cases compared to implicit challenges which detected them in 22.9%. User studies validate the real-world applicability of these methods, demonstrating that humans outperformed LLMs on explicit challenges with a success rate of 78% vs 22% for LLMs. Surprisingly, the study also reveals that many participants were using LLMs to complete tasks, further emphasizing the need for reliable, real-time LLM detection methods in high-stakes conversations.

title: The increasing availability of large language models (LLMs) has raised concerns about their potential misuse...
abstract: This study investigates the potential misuse of large language models (LLMs) in online learning and the reliability of detection methods. The research defines LLM-generated text and fine-tunes GPT-4o to detect such responses, finding it outperforms existing tool GPTZero with an accuracy of 80% and F1 score of 0.78 versus GPTZero's 70% accuracy and 0.50 macro F1 score. Suspected learners using LLM misuse were more likely to correctly answer posttest MCQs, indicating a bypass of the learning process. The study proposes using auxiliary statistical indicators like unusually high assessment scores, readability scores, and response duration for improved LLM-generated response detection. Data and code are contributed to support open science for similar model fine-tuning.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly being adopted for tasks like drafting emails, summarizing meetings,...
abstract: The study investigated users' perceptions of the helpfulness and privacy-preservation quality of Large Language Models (LLMs) in privacy-sensitive scenarios using a user study with 94 participants and 90 PrivacyLens scenarios. The results showed that users had differing opinions on identical LLM responses, while five proxy LLMs demonstrated consistent agreement but had low correlation with users' evaluations. This suggests that proxy LLMs may not accurately represent users' wide range of perceptions of utility and privacy in such scenarios. The paper emphasizes the need for more user-centered studies to evaluate LLMs' ability to aid users while maintaining privacy, and for improving alignment between LLMs and users in estimating perceived privacy and utility.

title: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit impressive capabilities but struggle with reasoning errors due to hallucinations...
abstract: The abstract discusses the analysis of large language models (LLMs) and their struggle with reasoning errors due to hallucinations and flawed logic. A novel puzzle dataset called ArrangementPuzzle is introduced for investigating LLM's internal representations of reasoning. A classifier model trained on LLM activations achieved over 80% accuracy in predicting reasoning correctness, indicating that LLMs internally distinguish between correct and incorrect reasoning steps. The study found that the strongest representations of reasoning occur in middle-late Transformer layers within the transformer architecture. Further analysis shows that LLMs encode abstract reasoning concepts within the middle activation layers, distinguishing logical from semantic equivalence.
categories: Introduction of ArrangementPuzzle, a novel puzzle dataset for investigating internal representations of reasoning in large language models, Training of a classifier model on LLM activations from the ArrangementPuzzle dataset, Stepwise correctness verification and analysis of the classifier model's performance, Analysis of LLM's internal representations of reasoning by examining the middle activation layers of the transformer architecture

title: Game development is a highly specialized task that relies on a complex game engine powered...
abstract: The paper presents the Chat Game Engine (ChatGE), a game development tool powered by LLM that enables users to create custom games using natural language through Human-LLM interaction. ChatGE performs three primary processes in each turn: configuring the game script, generating corresponding code snippets, and interacting with the user.

title: With the ubiquitous use of modern large language models (LLMs) across industries, the inference serving...
abstract: This paper discusses the increasing energy demands of large language models (LLMs) in various industries due to their expanding inference serving. The high compute and memory requirements have led to an increase in the deployment of top-of-the-line GPUs. Energy availability has emerged as a significant challenge for data center expansion aimed at serving these models. The paper presents the trade-offs involved when energy efficiency becomes the primary focus for LLM serving under performance Service Level Objectives (SLOs).
categories: Identifies several knobs available to the LLM inference provider to optimize energy usage., Characters the impact of these knobs on latency, throughput, and energy consumption.

categories: The abstract mentions the use of large language models (LLMs) interacting with tools to perform tasks autonomously. Specifically, it refers to blind database schema extraction and SQL injections., It is stated that GPT-4 is capable of hacking websites and finding vulnerabilities in them. No specific methodology for this process is provided within the abstract.

title: The development of large language models (LLMs) has given rise to four major application paradigms:...
abstract: The abstract discusses four major application paradigms for large language models (LLMs): LLM app stores, LLM agents, self-hosted LLM services, and LLM-powered devices. Each has its advantages and challenges, such as platform lock-in, lack of unified communication, increased deployment complexity, and hardware limitations respectively. The paper reviews and analyzes these paradigms, covering architecture design, application ecosystem, research progress, and the challenges they face.
categories: This paper outlines the next frontier of LLM applications, characterizing them through three interconnected layers: infrastructure, protocol, and application. The responsibilities and roles of each layer are described.

categories: Quantitative LLM judges are proposed to align evaluation scores of existing LLM judges with human preferences in a given domain using regression models., The models are trained to improve the score of the original judge using its rationale and score., Four quantitative judges for different types of absolute and relative feedback are presented., These methods showcase the generality and versatility of the framework.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized AI applications, yet their enormous computational demands severely limit...
abstract: The abstract proposes APT-LLM, a comprehensive acceleration scheme for arbitrary precision large language models (LLMs). It addresses challenges in GPU deployment and real-time performance due to computational demands. The scheme introduces a novel bipolar-INT data format, an efficient matrix multiplication method for arbitrary precision, a memory management system focused on data recovery, and a kernel mapping method for optimal configurable hyperparameters. APT-LLM achieves significant speedups compared to FP16 and NVIDIA CUTLASS baselines across various GPUs.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) were used to assist four Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation...
abstract: The research utilized LLMs to aid four CSIRO researchers in conducting SLRs. The study assesses the efficiency of these models in reproducing quotes from literature and answering research questions. The results show that state-of-the-art LLMs can accurately reproduce quotes with over 95% accuracy and answer research questions with about 83% accuracy. Experts were consulted to evaluate the model's performance, and a semantic text highlighting tool was developed for reviewing LLM responses. The correlation between the expert assessment and cosine similarity of transformer embeddings ranged from 0.48 to 0.77, suggesting that the latter is a valid metric for measuring semantic similarity.

title: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate the ability to simulate human decision-making processes, enabling their use...
Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly support applications in a wide range of domains, some with...
abstract: This study introduces RAmBLA, a framework to assess the reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs) in the biomedical domain. Four state-of-the-art foundation LLMs are evaluated for their suitability as assistants in this field, with prompt robustness, high recall, and absence of hallucinations identified as necessary criteria. The evaluation involves shortform tasks and freeform responses mimicking real-world user interactions. Performance is measured using semantic similarity with a ground truth response, through an evaluator LLM.

title: Context: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being used to generate program code. Much research...
Recent research has highlighted the potential of large language models (LLMs) to improve their problem-solving...
abstract: The study presents a new framework, LATM, where large language models create their own reusable tools for problem-solving. The approach consists of two phases: tool making and tool using, which are performed by powerful and lightweight models respectively. This division of labor allows the cost of tool-making to be spread over multiple instances of tool-using, reducing average costs while maintaining strong performance. The method offers a functional cache through the caching and reuse of tools, extending the applicability of the conventional cache mechanism. The effectiveness of LATM was evaluated across various complex reasoning tasks, including Big-Bench tasks.

title: Zero-shot graph machine learning, especially with graph neural networks (GNNs), has garnered significant interest due...
categories: Zero-shot graph machine learning, Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), Self-supervised learning, Graph prompt learning, Pretraining a GNN and aligning its representations with token embeddings of an LLM, Training a linear projector that transforms the GNN's representations into a fixed number of graph token embeddings without tuning the LLM, Designing a unified instruction for various graph tasks at different levels, such as node classification (node-level) and link prediction (edge-level)

title: LLM providers typically offer multiple LLM tiers, varying in performance and price. As NLP tasks...
abstract: The abstract introduces an LLM Automatic Transmission (LLM-AT) framework that automatically selects and adjusts the appropriate LLM tier for complex NLP tasks to balance cost and performance. The LLM-AT consists of a starter, generator, and judge. The starter initializes the selection of LLM tiers, the generator produces responses using the selected LLM tier, and the judge evaluates the validity of the response. If the response is invalid, LLM-AT iteratively upgrades to a higher-tier model, generates a new response, and re-evaluates until a valid response is obtained. The abstract also presents an accuracy estimator, which enables the suitable initial LLM tier selection without training by computing the valid response rate across top-k similar queries from past inference records.
categories: Initial LLM tier selection by the starter, Response generation using the selected LLM tier by the generator, Response evaluation by the judge, Iterative upgrade to higher-tier models if response is invalid, Accuracy estimator for suitable initial LLM tier selection

title: Peer review is an essential process to determine the quality of papers submitted to scientific...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to support peer review in scientific journals and conferences. The paper introduces a novel pipeline, PeerArg, which combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with knowledge representation methods. This system takes a set of reviews for a paper as input and outputs the prediction of paper acceptance. The performance of the PeerArg pipeline is evaluated on three different datasets compared to an end-2-end LLM that uses few-shot learning to predict paper acceptance from reviews.
categories: PeerArg system: A novel pipeline combining LLMs with methods from knowledge representation, Input: A set of reviews for a paper, Output: Paper acceptance prediction

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) can produce biased responses that can cause representational harms. However, conventional...
Given the high cost of large language model (LLM) training from scratch, safeguarding LLM intellectual...
abstract: iSeal is a novel fingerprinting method designed to provide reliable verification of LLM ownership even when the model thief controls the suspected LLM end-to-end. It achieves this through the injection of unique features into both the model and an external module, reinforced by an error-correction mechanism and a similarity-based verification strategy. These components are resistant to verification-time attacks such as collusion-based fingerprint unlearning and response manipulation. The authors claim that iSeal achieves 100 percent Fingerprint Success Rate (FSR) on 12 LLMs against more than 10 attacks, while baseline methods fail under unlearning and response manipulations.

title: This paper analyzes the safety of Large Language Models (LLMs) in interactions with children below...
categories: Analysis of safety of Large Language Models (LLMs) in interactions with children below age of 18 years, Listing down potential risks that children may encounter when using LLM powered applications, Development of Child User Models that reflect the varied personalities and interests of children informed by literature in child care and psychology, Utilization of Child User Models to evaluate the safety of six state of the art LLMs

title: The increasing capabilities of LLMs have led to the rapid proliferation of LLM agent apps,...
abstract: This paper presents the first comprehensive security study of LLM email agents. It introduces the Email Agent Hijacking (EAH) attack, a method that allows attackers to gain control over an email agent app remotely by overriding original prompts via external email resources. The research demonstrates that all 1,404 evaluated instances were successfully hijacked, with an average of 2.03 attempts needed to control an instance. For some LLMs, the number of attempts drops as low as 1.23.

title: Most existing code Large Language Model (LLM) benchmarks, e.g., EvalPlus, focus on the code generation...
abstract: This paper proposes a maturity model for code Large Language Models (LLMs) based on the postcondition generation problem. The proposed model aims to assess a more complete set of LLM capabilities by requiring the LLM to understand semantics, natural language, and generate unambiguous postconditions in programming languages. The maturity model is suitable as postconditions have various types, requiring different levels of capabilities. An augmented EvalPlus dataset was created for postcondition testing, and several open-source models were evaluated. Results indicate necessary improvements for better LLMs in code.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are prone to hallucinations and sensitive to prompt perturbations, often resulting...
abstract: The abstract discusses the issue of inconsistent and unreliable text generation by large language models (LLMs), attributing it to hallucinations and sensitivity to prompt perturbations. It highlights previous methods for measuring LLM response consistency, but points out a discrepancy between these automated methods and human perceptions of LLM consistency. A user study with 2,976 participants is mentioned, which demonstrates the poor alignment of current methods for measuring LLM response consistency with humans' perceptions.
categories: Previously used methods for measuring LLM response consistency include calculating the probability of a response appearing within a pool of resampled responses, analyzing internal states, and evaluating logits of responses., The paper proposes a new logit-based ensemble method for estimating LLM consistency.

title: We introduce LLM CHESS, an evaluation framework designed to probe the generalization of reasoning and...
categories: Playing against a random opponent using a range of behavioral metrics (win and loss rates, move quality, move legality, hallucinated actions, game duration), Deriving an Elo estimate by playing against a chess engine with variably configured skill

title: Adopting human and large language models (LLM) as judges (a.k.a human- and LLM-as-a-judge) for evaluating...
abstract: This paper proposes a novel framework to investigate biases in human and large language models (LLMs) used as judges for evaluating performance. The study focuses on Misinformation Oversight Bias, Gender Bias, Authority Bias, and Beauty Bias. A dataset is curated based on the revised Bloom's Taxonomy and thousands of evaluations are conducted. Results reveal that both human and LLM judges are susceptible to various degrees of bias, even for cutting-edge models. The research further exploits these biases to conduct attacks on LLM judges. The goal is to highlight the presence of bias and vulnerability in human- and LLM-as-a-judge systems, emphasizing the need for robust evaluation systems.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable capabilities in language understanding and generation. However, such...
abstract: This research focuses on compressing large language models (LLMs) while preserving their multi-task solving and language generation capabilities. The challenge lies in the substantial model size and the enormous size of the training corpus, which pose issues in deployment, inference, and training stages. To address these concerns, the authors propose LLM-Pruner, a method that employs structural pruning to selectively remove non-critical coupled structures based on gradient information. The compressed models are shown to still exhibit satisfactory capabilities in zero-shot classification and generation.
categories: LLM-Pruner adopts structural pruning, a method that removes non-critical coupled structures based on gradient information., Recovery of pruned models' performance is efficiently achieved through tuning techniques like LoRA, requiring only 3 hours and 50K data., The method is validated on three LLMs: LLaMA, Vicuna, and ChatGLM.

title: Recent research has shown that LLM performance on reasoning tasks can be enhanced by scaling...
abstract: This work proposes a new approach for enhancing LLM performance on reasoning tasks. It views thought graph transformations as actions in a Markov decision process and implements policy agents to drive effective action policies for the underlying reasoning LLM agent. The work introduces ARIES, a multi-agent architecture for reasoning with LLMs. In ARIES, reasoning LLM agents solve decomposed subproblems while policy LLM agents maintain visibility of the thought graph states and dynamically adapt the problem-solving strategy. Through extensive experiments, it is observed that this approach can improve accuracy and reduce inference costs significantly.

title: The recent advances in LLMs bring a strong demand for efficient system support to improve...
abstract: The paper discusses the need for efficient system support to improve serving efficiency in light of recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs). It proposes LLM microserving, a multi-level architecture that structures and programs LLM inference services. This architecture introduces fine-grained sub-request level APIs, a programmable router for dynamic reconfiguration of serving patterns, and a unified KV cache interface to support diverse execution patterns. The evaluation shows that LLM microserving can be reconfigured to support multiple disaggregation orchestration strategies in a few lines of Python code. It maintains state-of-the-art performance for LLM inference tasks and allows exploration of new strategy variants that reduce up to 47% of job completion time compared to existing strategies.
categories: Introduction of fine-grained sub-request level APIs, Development of a programmable router for dynamic reconfiguration of serving patterns, Creation of a unified KV cache interface for diverse execution patterns

title: In this note we revisit the Lin, Lunin, Maldacena (LLM) class of d=11 supergravity solutions...
abstract: The study revisits and generalizes the Lin, Lunin, Maldacena (LLM) class of d=11 supergravity solutions with symmetry SO(6) X SO(3) X R. The goal is to investigate if there exist supersymmetric geometries with additional fluxes beyond the LLM ansatz. The research proves that there are no such supersymmetric geometries with extra fluxes. Additionally, it is shown that the LLM relationship between Killing spinors, ε_- = - γ_5 ε_+, arises from identifying two Killing directions through the Killing spinor equation which correspond to candidate R-symmetry directions. The research focuses on d=11 supergravity solutions with specific symmetries and investigates whether additional fluxes can be incorporated while maintaining supersymmetry.
categories: Utilization of the Killing spinor equation for proof, Investigation into possible relationships between Killing spinors, SO(6) X SO(3) X R symmetries, and candidate R-symmetry directions

title: Large language models (LLMs) with memory are computationally universal. However, mainstream LLMs are not taking...
abstract: The abstract discusses the limitation of large language models (LLMs) currently in use, which are computationally universal but fail to fully leverage memory and are heavily influenced by biological brains. The paper proposes an innovative approach by drawing inspiration from modern computer architectures to augment LLMs with symbolic memory for complex multi-hop reasoning. The proposed solution involves a symbolic memory framework that is instantiated as an LLM and a set of SQL databases, with the LLM generating SQL instructions to manipulate these databases.
categories: The paper proposes to use a symbolic memory framework consisting of a large language model (LLM) and SQL databases., The LLM generates SQL instructions to manipulate the SQL databases for complex multi-hop reasoning.

title: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered LLM agents to autonomously collect world...
categories: Employing Large Language Models (LLMs) for autonomously collecting world information., Designing an agentic environment with tools for accessing historical, structured events and textual news articles., Refining the GDELT event database with careful cleaning and parsing to curate a series of relational prediction tasks., Assessing LLM agents' abilities from short-term to long-term forecasting., Implementing APIs to enable LLM agents to utilize different tools via a code-based interface.

title: This paper provides a comprehensive review of the future of cybersecurity through Generative AI and...
Routing a query through an appropriate LLM is challenging, particularly when user preferences are expressed...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of routing queries through an appropriate Language Model (LLM) when user preferences are in natural language and model attributes are partially observable. A constraint-based interpretation of LLM routing is proposed, formulated as a weighted MaxSAT/MaxSMT problem with natural language feedback inducing hard and soft constraints over model attributes. The routing process is described as selecting models that approximately maximize satisfaction of feedback-conditioned clauses.
categories: Formulating LLM routing as a weighted MaxSAT/MaxSMT problem, Using natural language feedback to induce hard and soft constraints over model attributes

title: The recent introduction of large language models (LLMs) has revolutionized the field of robotics by...
categories: The methods section describes the introduction of RoboPAIR, an algorithm designed to jailbreak LLM-controlled robots. Unlike existing, textual attacks on LLM chatbots, RoboPAIR is designed to elicit harmful physical actions from these robots. The paper presents three scenarios for testing RoboPAIR: a white-box setting, a gray-box setting, and a black-box setting. In the white-box setting, the attacker has full access to the NVIDIA Dolphins self-driving LLM. In the gray-box setting, the attacker has partial access to a Clearpath Robotics Jackal UGV robot equipped with a GPT-4o planner. In the black-box setting, the attacker has only query access to the GPT-3.5-integrated Unitree Robotics Go2 robot dog. The paper also includes several static baselines for comparison.

title: We study null geodesics of the ten-dimensional LLM geometries. In particular, we show that there...
abstract: The study focuses on null geodesics in ten-dimensional LLM geometries, particularly those confined to the LLM plane. These geodesics exhibit a Hamiltonian system dynamics with two degrees of freedom and are shown to be chaotic. The geodesics' behavior resembles a billiards problem with fixed obstacles within the LLM plane. The paper also discusses the potential treatment of LLM geometries with multiple droplets as incipient black holes and draws comparisons with the fuzzball proposal. It is suggested that for in-plane null geodesics deep inside a region filled with many droplets, they may need to undergo a diffusion-like process to exit towards the AdS boundary, potentially accounting for signals getting lost within the putative black hole for an extended period.
categories: Analysis of null geodesics in ten-dimensional LLM geometries., Exploration of confined in-plane geodesics and their effective dynamics., Two-coloring of the LLM plane to distinguish empty and filled regions., Comparison with billiards problems and incipient black hole models.

title: LLM app stores are quickly emerging as platforms that gather a wide range of intelligent...
abstract: The abstract introduces LaQual, an automated framework for evaluating the quality of LLM (Language Model Library) applications in app stores. LaQual aims to address the challenge of finding high-quality apps by relying on static metrics alone. It proposes a three-stage approach: hierarchical labeling and classification, filtering using static indicators, and dynamic, scenario-adaptive evaluation. The study shows that LaQual is effective, with automated scores closely aligning with human judgments in legal consulting and travel planning.
categories: Hierarchical labeling and classification of LLM apps to match them with different scenarios, Filtering out low-quality apps using static indicators like time-weighted user engagement and functional capability metrics, Dynamic, scenario-adaptive evaluation where the LLM generates scenario-specific evaluation metrics, scoring rules, and tasks

categories: Single-prompt evaluations across 6.5M instances involving 20 different LLMs and 39 tasks from 3 benchmarks for initial analysis, proposing diverse prompts for improved robustness, discussing tailored evaluation metrics for specific use cases, and conducting evaluations with these criteria on multiple models.

title: The Internet of Things (IoT) in the sixth generation (6G) era is envisioned to evolve...
abstract: In this article, an LLM-empowered IoT architecture for 6G networks is proposed to achieve intelligent autonomy and support advanced applications. LLMs are pushed to the edge of the network to promote synergy between LLMs and IoT. The paper discusses tailored LLM solutions for IoT application requirements and management needs (LLM for IoT). It also addresses edge inference and edge fine-tuning for LLM deployment (LLM on IoT). Additionally, a memory-efficient SFL framework is presented for LLM fine-tuning on heterogeneous IoT devices to minimize memory pressures. Finally, the article presents a case study and discusses open issues related to the implementation of LLM-empowered IoT in 6G networks.

title: Because "out-of-the-box" large language models are capable of generating a great deal of objectionable content,...
abstract: This paper introduces a new attack method for aligned language models, which aims to generate objectionable content despite safety measures. The approach automatically produces adversarial suffixes using greedy and gradient-based search techniques, improving upon previous automatic prompt generation methods. Surprisingly, the generated adversarial prompts are found to be transferable across various models and public interfaces. This work significantly advances the state-of-the-art in adversarial attacks against aligned language models, raising important questions about preventing such systems from producing objectionable information.

title: In the developer community for large language models (LLMs), there is not yet a clean...
Large Language Models (LLMs) need to adapt their predictions to diverse cultural contexts to benefit...
abstract: The study proposes a Multi-Agent Debate framework using multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) for cultural adaptability. Two LLM-based agents debate over a cultural scenario and collaboratively decide. The approach improves overall accuracy and cultural group parity compared to single-LLM baselines. Smaller LLMs (7-9B parameters) can match the accuracy of larger models (27B parameters) using this multi-agent debate method, as shown in experiments on the NormAd-ETI benchmark for social etiquette norms across 75 countries.

title: Recent SOTA approaches for embodied learning via interaction directly employ large language models (LLMs) as...

title: This study explores the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in providing knowledge about cities...
abstract: The study investigates the ability of large language models (LLMs) in supplying information about cities and regions on a global scale. Findings reveal that LLMs contain a wide but varying degree of knowledge across global cities, with ML models trained on LLM-derived features leading to enhanced predictive accuracy. It is noted that while LLMs exhibit some level of knowledge across cities globally, they tend to generate generic or random outputs for unfamiliar tasks, indicating their lack of knowledge in such cases.
categories: Directly querying the LLM for target variable values, Extracting explicit and implicit features from the LLM correlated with the target variable

title: Quantization leverages lower-precision weights to reduce the memory usage of large language models (LLMs) and...
categories: Fine-tuning on an adversarial task to obtain a malicious LLM, Quantization of the malicious model, Calculation of constraints that characterize all full-precision models mapping to the same quantized model, Projected gradient descent to tune out poisoned behavior from the full-precision model while ensuring weights satisfy the calculated constraints, Resulting in an LLM that exhibits benign behavior in full precision but follows adversarial behavior when quantized

title: While large language models (LLMs) demonstrate impressive capabilities across numerous applications, their robustness remains a...
abstract: The paper discusses the order sensitivity vulnerability in large language models (LLMs), which can lead to biased decisions and different answers when options are reordered. This issue is significant not only for multiple-choice question answering but also for using LLMs as automated evaluators in AI pipelines. To address this, the authors propose Set-LLM, a novel architectural adaptation that allows pretrained LLMs to process mixed set-text inputs with permutation invariance guarantees. The key adjustments include a new attention mask and specific positional encodings designed for sets.
categories: Introducing Set-LLM: a novel adaptation for pretrained LLMs with new attention mask and positional encodings specifically designed for sets., Theoretical proof of invariance provided to support the effectiveness of Set-LLM.

title: Due to the cost-prohibitive nature of training Large Language Models (LLMs), fine-tuning has emerged as...
abstract: The research paper focuses on understanding the efficiency of fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) using a cost-effective approach, specifically sparse Mixture of Experts (MoE). The study evaluates the accuracy and runtime performance of both sparse and dense MoE models. The results highlight the optimization of the MoE layer as crucial for improving the performance of LLM fine-tuning. Furthermore, an analytical model is developed to estimate the cost of LLM fine-tuning on cloud platforms based on the parameters of the model and GPU architecture. This model aids practitioners in industry and academia in budgeting the cost of fine-tuning a specific model.

title: We present a connectome-informed LLM framework that encodes dynamic fMRI connectivity as temporal sequences, applies...
categories: The presented method encodes dynamic fMRI connectivity as temporal sequences., Robust normalization is applied to the data., The normalized data is mapped into a representation suitable for a frozen pre-trained LLM for clinical prediction.

title: Deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) on edge devices presents significant challenges due to computational constraints,...
LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) have shown significant potential in tackling diverse tasks. However, to design...
abstract: This paper presents MAS-GPT, a simplified approach to building multi-agent systems (MAS). The novelty lies in reframing the process as a generative language task, where user queries generate corresponding MAS. MAS-GPT unifies MAS representation as executable code and proposes a data construction pipeline for creating coherent and consistent query-MAS pairs. Training this open-source medium-sized LLM results in its ability to generate adaptive MAS within a single inference. The generated MAS can process user queries and deliver high-quality responses. Experiments on 9 benchmarks and 5 LLMs indicate MAS-GPT's superiority over multiple baseline MAS methods across various settings, showcasing its effectiveness, efficiency, and strong generalization ability. The code for MAS-GPT will be available at https://github.com/rui-ye/MAS-GPT.

title: Despite the success of Large Language Models (LLMs) across various fields, their potential to generate...
abstract: The abstract discusses the concerns about Large Language Models (LLMs) generating untruthful, biased, and harmful responses, particularly in critical applications. It emphasizes the need for systematic methods to detect and prevent such misbehavior. The introduction of LLMScan, a comprehensive solution, is presented. This monitoring technique based on causality analysis offers an innovative way to systematically analyze the inner workings of an LLM through causal inference. By examining the causal contributions of input tokens and transformer layers, LLMScan effectively detects misbehavior. Extensive experiments reveal clear distinctions in the causal distributions between normal behavior and misbehavior, enabling the development of accurate, lightweight detectors for various misbehavior detection tasks.

title: While open Large Language Models (LLMs) have made significant progress, they still fall short of...
abstract: The analysis compares four recent methods for adapting closed Large Language Models (LLMs) to private data while preserving privacy. The study reveals that all the methods under investigation leak query data, which is the user data queried during inference, to the LLM provider. Additionally, three out of four methods also disclose substantial portions of private training data to the provider, with the exception being a method requiring a local open LLM for privacy protection. The study further shows that all methods exhibit lower performance compared to three private gradient-based adaptation methods for local open LLMs. Moreover, the private adaptation methods for closed LLMs incur higher monetary costs for training and queries than running the alternative methods on local open LLMs.
categories: 1. Method 1, 2. Method 2, 3. Method 3, 4. Method 4

title: Hidden LLM prompts have appeared in online documents with increasing frequency. Their goal is to...
abstract: The paper introduces PhantomLint, a tool for detecting hidden LLM prompts in various types of documents such as academic papers, CVs, and theses. The evaluation against a corpus of 3402 documents shows that PhantomLint has a low false positive rate (approx. 0.092%), is practically useful for real-world document analysis, and achieves acceptable performance.

title: The rapid evolution of large language models (LLMs) and their capacity to simulate human cognition...
abstract: This survey offers a comprehensive examination of large language models (LLMs) and their human-centric capabilities. It evaluates LLM competencies in areas like reasoning, perception, and social cognition, comparing them to human-like skills. Real-world applications of LLMs are explored in domains such as behavioral science, political science, and sociology. The survey also identifies challenges, including improving adaptability, emotional intelligence, cultural sensitivity, addressing biases, and enhancing human-AI collaboration, as well as future research directions.

title: The advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs) have decentralized the responsibility for the transparency of...
Large language models (LLMs) exhibit dynamic capabilities and appear to comprehend complex and ambiguous natural...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge in calibrating interactions with Large Language Models (LLMs), noting that their complexity and ambiguity mirrors human cognitive processes, but surpasses our understanding in existing interaction models. The focus is on the gap in translating user goals into clear intentions for LLM responses, termed as 'Envisioning'. Three misalignments are identified: determining if LLMs can accomplish a task, instructing them to do so, and evaluating their output success in meeting the goal. Recommendations are proposed to bridge this 'envisioning gulf' in human-LLM interactions.

title: Program synthesis from input-output examples, also called programming by example (PBE), has had tremendous impact...
The complexity of large language model (LLM) serving workloads has substantially increased due to the...
abstract: The paper discusses the increased complexity in large language model (LLM) serving workloads due to integration with external tools like ChatGPT plugins. It proposes an efficient solution for LLM serving, called Conveyor, which involves tool partial execution alongside LLM decoding during requests that trigger tools. The goal is to improve request completion latency. The authors introduce a novel interface for tool developers to expose partial execution opportunities to the LLM serving system and a request scheduler that facilitates partial tool execution.
categories: Design of Conveyor, an efficient LLM serving system optimized for handling requests involving external tools., Introduction of a novel interface for tool developers to expose partial execution opportunities to the LLM serving system., Development of a request scheduler that facilitates partial tool execution.

title: Despite the growing interest in leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) for content analysis, current studies...
categories: The study followed a new workflow of LLM-assisted multimodal content analysis. The methods encompassed codebook design, prompt engineering, LLM processing, and human evaluation. Codebook design involved strategically crafting annotation prompts to get LLM Annotations in structured form. Prompt engineering was used to generate LLM Explanations for a better understanding of LLM reasoning and transparency. The study analyzed 203 keyframes extracted from 25 YouTube short videos about depression.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have shown potential as general evaluators along with the evident benefits...
abstract: This paper investigates the consistency of large language models (LLMs) as general evaluators. Despite their speed and cost benefits, the reliability of LLM evaluators is a concern due to insufficient studies on consistency. The research focuses on two aspects of consistency: Self-Consistency (SC) and Inter-scale Consistency (IC), across various scoring scales and criterion granularity using open-source and proprietary models. The study reveals that even strong proprietary models may not guarantee consistent evaluations, emphasizing the significance of considering consistency when assessing the capability of LLM evaluators.
categories: Conduct extensive studies on Self-Consistency (SC) and Inter-scale Consistency (IC) of LLMs, Evaluate models across different scoring scales and criterion granularity, Utilize open-source and proprietary models for the research

title: LLM test-time compute (or LLM inference) via search has emerged as a promising research area...
abstract: The abstract discusses the emerging research area of LLM test-time compute via search, highlighting rapid developments but challenges in direct comparisons due to distinct perspectives on task definition, LLM profiling, and search procedures. A survey is presented aiming to provide a unified technical review on existing LIS frameworks, standardizing task definitions under Markov Decision Process (MDP) and offering modular definitions of LLM profiling and search procedures.
categories: The survey proposes to unify task definitions under the Markov Decision Process (MDP), It offers modular definitions of LLM profiling, Modular definitions of search procedures are provided

title: This paper explores the potential for leveraging Large Language Models (LLM) in the realm of...
abstract: The abstract discusses the potential application of Large Language Models (LLM) in online advertising systems. It proposes a four-module framework for LLM advertisement: modification, bidding, prediction, and auction. The paper presents design considerations for each module and evaluates them based on sustainability desiderata. Additionally, it raises questions about practicality, efficiency, and implementation challenges for future research. Lastly, it suggests a connection between recent mechanism design approaches for LLM within the proposed framework.

title: We reproduce the UMBRELA LLM Judge evaluation framework across a range of large language models...
abstract: The study compares the performance of UMBRELA LLM Judge evaluation framework with different large language models (LLMs). The results show that UMBRELA with DeepSeak V3 performs similarly to GPT-4o, which was used in the original work. However, with LLaMA-3.3-70B, there is a slight drop in performance. This performance degrades further with smaller LLMs.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance across diverse domains, yet they still encounter...
Recent innovation in large language models (LLMs), and their myriad use-cases have rapidly driven up...
abstract: The paper discusses the increasing power demand for datacenter GPUs due to large language models (LLMs). The authors identify that power oversubscription in LLM clusters can improve power efficiency, allowing more deployable servers per datacenter and reducing deployment time. They characterize power consumption patterns of various LLMs and claim that inference workloads offer substantial headroom for power oversubscription. However, the reliability and robustness of power oversubscription in a virtualized GPU environment are challenging. The authors propose POLCA, a framework for power oversubscription in GPU clusters, which can deploy 30% more servers with minimal performance loss.

title: LLM app (tool) ecosystems are rapidly evolving to support sophisticated use cases that often require...
Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges, which can benefit from inference-time scaling, provide a promising path for extending the...
abstract: The abstract discusses Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges, which are promising for extending reasoning models to non-verifiable domains. However, their effectiveness in policy training has not been thoroughly investigated. A rigorous study is conducted using a controlled synthetic setting to compare the impact of non-reasoning and reasoning judges in reinforcement-learning-based LLM alignment.

The framework, tested on state-of-the-art hardware (AMD RTX 3060 GPUs and Intel i9-12900 processors), achieved an average accuracy of 91.33% across these datasets with the highest accuracy observed in time-series data (92.7%). The research demonstrates the versatility and effectiveness of LLMs in handling multimodal data within the transportation sector, offering potential improvements in efficiency and accuracy for intelligent transportation systems.

It demonstrates its effectiveness through experiments, reducing the attack success rates of state-of-the-art adversarial prompts and popular handcrafted jailbreaking prompts from nearly 100% to around 10% or less.

abstract: The article discusses the rapidly evolving field of Large Language Model (LLM) inference, highlighting the need for a concise framework to analyze various methods. Despite a vibrant research landscape, no comprehensive framework exists yet. The authors propose a framework based on roofline model to systematically analyze LLM inference techniques, identifying bottlenecks during hardware deployment and providing insights into practical problems such as memory-bound issues, resource requirements, and hardware selection. The survey covers recent advancements in efficient LLM inference, focusing on key areas like model compression (Knowledge Distillation, Quantization), algorithm improvements (Early Exit, Mixture-of-Expert), and hardware/system-level enhancements.

abstract: The abstract discusses the variability in responses from large language models (LLMs) due to their stochastic nature, even when setting temperature to zero with a fixed random seed. The study focuses on evaluating the impact of experimental repeats on mean score and prediction interval using benchmarks designed for testing LLMs' capacity to reason about cardinal directions. The research aims to cost-effectively quantify uncertainty in benchmark scores and provides recommendations for reproducible LLM evaluation.

The goal is to enable LLMs with the foresight to determine if an action contributes to completing the final task. This framework performs critic regression to learn a sequential advantage function from LLM-planned data, treating the LLM planner as an optimizer to generate actions that maximize the advantage function.

abstract: The paper introduces LegoSLM, a new paradigm that bridges speech encoders and Large Language Models (LLMs) using ASR posterior matrices. The goal is to combine both models for improved performance on spoken language processing tasks such as Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and speech translation. LegoSLM generates CTC posteriors over the LLM vocabulary, uses these to create pseudo-audio embeddings, concatenates them with text embeddings in the LLM input space, and connects well-performing USM and Gemma models for better results on ASR and speech translation tasks. The LegoSLM method shows significant improvement over the USM-CTC baseline, achieving an average of 49% WERR on 8 MLS testsets. The model also exhibits modularity in various settings, allowing for easy fine-tuning of Gemma model weights and the ability to switch and combine the speech encoder with the LLM in a zero-shot fashion.

The model can generate accurate timestamped transcriptions and translations from long audio inputs by integrating the LLM with a speech encoder and using multi-task instruction tuning. The implementation of Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting is also found to yield advantages in the context of LLM-ST. The exceptional performance of LLM-ST is demonstrated through rigorous experimentation on English and Chinese datasets, establishing a new benchmark in the field of speech translation.

abstract: The paper presents PAFT, a new parallel training paradigm for effective fine-tuning of Large Language Models (LLMs). This approach separates supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and preference alignment (e.g., DPO and ORPO) on different datasets using the same pre-trained model. The final model is obtained by parameter fusion of models produced from SFT and preference alignment for downstream applications. The research finds that preference alignment methods like DPO produce a sparse model, while SFT results in a dense model requiring sparsification for efficient model merging.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used as scalable evaluators of model outputs, but their...
abstract: The study compares several embedding-level concept extraction methods for analyzing Large Language Model (LLM) judge behavior. The research found that sparse autoencoders recover substantially more interpretable preference features than alternatives while remaining competitive in predicting LLM decisions. Using over 27k paired responses from multiple human preference datasets and judgments from three LLMs, the study validates existing results such as the tendency for LLMs to prefer refusal of sensitive requests at higher rates than humans. New trends uncovered include biases towards concreteness and empathy in approaching new situations, detail and formality in academic advice, and against legal guidance that promotes active steps like calling police and filing lawsuits. The results suggest that automated concept discovery enables systematic analysis of LLM judge preferences without predefined bias taxonomies.

title: The retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approach is used to reduce the confabulation of large language models...
abstract: The study investigates the effectiveness and robustness of four large language models (LLMs) - Gemma 2, GPT-4o-mini, Llama~3.1, and Mixtral - in answering biomedical questions using the retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approach. The research focuses on three scenarios: vanilla LLM answers, 'perfect' augmented generation, and prompt-injection attacks. Llama 3.1 is found to be the most accurate model in both vanilla and 'perfect' RAG scenarios. However, the accuracy gap between models almost disappears with 'perfect' RAG, suggesting its potential to mitigate size-related effectiveness differences. The paper also evaluates the LLMs' ability to generate malicious context and their robustness against prompt-injection attacks. Llama 3.1, achieving the highest accuracy in both vanilla (0.651) and 'perfect' RAG (0.802) scenarios, is found to be the most effective model.
categories: The research evaluates the models using yes-no and free-form questions across three scenarios: vanilla LLM answers (no context is provided), 'perfect' augmented generation (correct context is provided), and prompt-injection attacks (incorrect context is provided). Metrics such as attack success rate (ASR), accuracy under attack, and accuracy drop are used to assess the models' robustness against adversarial attacks., The same four LLMs (Gemma 2, GPT-4o-mini, Llama 3.1, and Mixtral) are utilized as adversaries to generate incorrect context that is injected in the target model's prompt.

title: Hyper-parameters are essential and critical for the performance of communication algorithms. However, current hyper-parameters optimization...
abstract: This paper proposes an Large Language Model (LLM) agent for automatic hyper-parameter tuning in the Warm-Start Particles Swarm Optimization with Crossover and Mutation (WS-PSO-CM) algorithm, specifically designed for radio map-enabled unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) trajectory and communication. The LLM agent uses an iterative framework and Model Context Protocol (MCP). It is initialized with a profile specifying boundaries of hyper-parameters, task objective, terminal condition, optimization strategy, and LLM configurations. The LLM agent then invokes the WS-PSO-CM algorithm for exploration and exits based on the terminal condition, returning an optimized set of hyper-parameters. Experiment results indicate that the minimal sum-rate achieved by hyper-parameters generated via the LLM agent is significantly higher than those by human heuristics and random generation methods.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are a double-edged sword capable of generating harmful misinformation -- inadvertently,...
abstract: This paper investigates the efficacy and characteristics of Large Language Models (LLMs) in generating harmful medical misinformation through 'jailbreak' attacks. The study examines 109 distinct jailbreak attacks on three target LLMs, comparing attack prompts to health-related queries, analyzing generated misinformation against health-related misinformation on Reddit, and evaluating the effectiveness of standard machine learning approaches in detecting this misinformation. The research suggests that with additional study, LLMs could be utilized for detecting and preventing the spread of misinformation.
categories: Investigation of jailbreak attacks on three target LLMs and comparison of attack prompts to in-the-wild health-related LLM queries., Examination of the resulting jailbreak responses, comparing generated misinformation to health-related misinformation on Reddit., Evaluation of the effectiveness of standard machine learning approaches in detecting this misinformation.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as automated evaluators, yet prior works demonstrate that...
abstract: The study investigates the consistency of large language models (LLMs) as automated evaluators compared to human raters across six benchmarks. The research focuses on three scales and reveals that LLM judgments are not consistently consistent across subjective benchmarks, with the choice of scale significantly impacting human-LLM agreement. The grading scale of 0-5 shows the strongest alignment between humans and LLMs. The study also highlights that pooled reliability can conceal benchmark heterogeneity and reveals systematic subgroup differences in alignment across gender groups, emphasizing the importance of scale design and diagnostic sub-levels in LLM-as-a-judge protocols.
categories: Comparison between human and LLM raters on three scales, Ratings collected across six benchmarks including objective, open-ended subjective, and mixed tasks, Intraclass correlation coefficients (ICC) used to measure absolute agreement

title: The active research topic of prompt engineering makes it evident that LLMs are sensitive to...
abstract: The study explores the impact of prompt engineering on LLMs (Large Language Models). It is found that LLMs are sensitive to minor changes in prompt wording, attributed to their inductive bias. The research proposes a strategy called Inductive Bias Extraction and Matching, which utilizes an LLM's output as part of its new prompt to align with the model's existing bias. This method is shown to improve LLM Likert ratings for classification tasks by up to 19% and for ranking tasks by up to 27%.
categories: Using the Inductive Bias Extraction and Matching strategy, where an LLM's output is incorporated into its new prompt., Evaluating the effectiveness of the strategy using LLM Likert ratings for both classification and ranking tasks.

title: The advent of ultra-low-bit LLM models (1/1.58/2-bit), which match the perplexity and end-task performance of...
abstract: The research introduces ultra-low-bit LLM models (1/1.58/2-bit), which match the perplexity and end-task performance of full-precision counterparts, for resource-constrained environments like edge devices and AI PCs. Develops 1-bit and 2-bit microkernels optimized for modern CPUs, achieving high computational efficiency across various CPU platforms. Integrates these microkernels into PyTorch-TPP, delivering end-to-end inference results with 2-bit models that outperform current SOTA runtime bitnet.cpp by up to 2.2x and offer up to 7x speedup compared to the 16-bit model inference. Extends work to Intel GPUs, designing and implementing mixed precision, 2-bit GEMM kernels for optimal performance. Integrates optimized Xe2 kernels as a quantization plugin into vLLM framework for LLM inference on AI PCs and Intel Xe GPUs. Achieves up to 6.3x speedup in end-to-end latency compared to the BF16 execution, and reduces GEMM time by 4x - 8x depending on model and platform. Advances LLM inference on AI PCs and Intel Xe GPUs, paving the way for efficient deployment of ultra-low-bit LLM models.
categories: Designing and implementing 1-bit and 2-bit microkernels optimized for modern CPUs, Integrating these microkernels into PyTorch-TPP for end-to-end inference, Designing and implementing mixed precision, 2-bit GEMM kernels for Intel GPUs, Integrating optimized Xe2 kernels as a quantization plugin into vLLM framework

title: Practitioners often navigate LLM performance trade-offs by plotting Pareto frontiers of optimal accuracy-cost trade-offs. However,...
abstract: The abstract discusses an economic evaluation framework for Large Language Models (LLMs). The framework quantifies the performance trade-off of an LLM as a single number, considering economic constraints such as cost of mistakes, incremental latency, and abstaining from queries. This evaluation is applied to reasoning and non-reasoning models on difficult questions from the MATH benchmark. The research reveals that reasoning models offer better accuracy-cost tradeoffs when the economic cost of a mistake exceeds $0.01. Additionally, single large LLMs are found to outperform cascades when the cost of making a mistake is as low as $0.1. The findings suggest that when automating meaningful human tasks with AI models, practitioners should typically use the most powerful available model rather than minimizing AI deployment costs, as deployment costs are likely dwarfed by the economic impact of AI errors.
categories: Economic evaluation of LLMs is proposed, quantifying the performance trade-off based on cost of mistakes, incremental latency, and abstaining from queries., The economic evaluation framework is applied to compare the performance of reasoning and non-reasoning models on difficult questions from the MATH benchmark.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in various applications. As their usage grows, concerns...
abstract: The paper introduces the MoGU framework to enhance the safety of LLMs while preserving their usability. The MoGU framework transforms the base LLM into two variants, the usable LLM and the safe LLM, and uses dynamic routing to balance their contribution. In case of malicious instructions, the router prioritizes the safe LLM to ensure harmless responses. For benign instructions, the router prioritizes the usable LLM for usable and helpful responses. The research found that existing defense strategies predominantly adopt a rejection-oriented stance, which diminishes the usability of their responses to benign instructions. The study concludes by releasing safer versions of Llama2, Vicuna, Falcon, Dolphin, and Baichuan2.

abstract: In this paper, the authors propose Smooth Reading, a chunk-wise inference method inspired by human reading strategies, to improve the performance of Recurrent Large Language Models (Recurrent LLMs) on long-context tasks.
