abstract: In this paper, a new framework called HARMONIC is introduced for tabular data generation and evaluation using LLMs. The framework uses larger-scale LLMs with fine-tuning for tabular data generation to enhance privacy. It constructs an instruction fine-tuning dataset based on the k-nearest neighbors algorithm, inspiring LLMs to discover inter-row relationships. With fine-tuning, LLMs remember the format and connections of the data rather than the data itself, reducing the risk of privacy leakage. The paper also introduces specific privacy risk metrics (DLT) for LLM synthetic data generation and performance evaluation metrics (LLE) for downstream LLM tasks.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed through open-source and commercial frameworks, enabling individuals and...
abstract: This study investigates the security and reliability of large language model (LLM) deployments across various open-source and commercial frameworks. The research focuses on understanding the prevalence, exposure characteristics, systemic vulnerabilities, and associated risks of public-facing LLM services. Key findings include: over 40% of endpoints use plain HTTP; over 210,000 endpoints lack valid TLS metadata; API exposure is highly inconsistent among frameworks; widespread use of insecure protocols, poor TLS configurations, and unauthenticated access to critical operations.
categories: Internet-wide measurements were conducted to identify public-facing LLM services., 158 unique API endpoints were extracted and categorized into 12 functional groups based on functionality and security risk.

title: Agentic workflows are composed of sequences of interdependent Large Language Model (LLM) calls, and they...
abstract: This research introduces Helium, a new serving framework for Large Language Models (LLM). Helium focuses on optimizing agentic workflows by modeling them as query plans and treating LLM invocations as first-class operators. It integrates proactive caching and cache-aware scheduling to maximize reuse across prompts, KV states, and workflows. The paper demonstrates that Helium achieves significant performance improvements over existing systems, providing up to 1.56x speedup on various workloads.

title: This research focuses on how Large Language Models (LLMs) can help with (path) planning for...
abstract: This research introduces LLM A*, a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for utility-optimal path planning in a human-in-the-loop and interactive manner. The approach takes human feedback, communicates planning results, and fosters code-free path planning. Comparative analysis shows greater efficiency than A* and RL.

The utility-optimal A* is proposed to facilitate few-shot near-optimal path planning.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are known to produce outputs with limited diversity. In this work,...
abstract: The study investigates whether incorporating random concepts into prompts can enhance the diversity of outputs generated by large language models (LLMs). To evaluate this, an experimental protocol is designed with LLM questions like 'Name 10 Hollywood actors', and diversity measures are analyzed in the resulting outputs. The experiments on various LLMs reveal that adding unrelated words/sentences to prompts leads to increased diversity in LLM outputs. This promising outcome suggests potential future applications of randomness infusion into LLMs across different domains.
categories: Design of a systematic evaluation protocol involving prompting an LLM with specific questions like 'Name 10 Hollywood actors'., Generation and analysis of diversity measures from the resulting LLM outputs., Experiments performed on multiple large language models.

title: In this work, we identify the "2D-Cheating" problem in 3D LLM evaluation, where these tasks...
abstract: The work identifies a problem known as '2D-Cheating' in the evaluation of 3D LLMs. It reveals that certain tasks can be solved by VLMs using rendered images of point clouds, which undermines the evaluation of 3D LLMs' specific 3D abilities. To address this issue, the authors propose principles for assessing genuine 3D understanding in 3D LLMs and advocate separating 3D capabilities from 1D or 2D aspects during evaluations. They have made their code and data available at the provided GitHub link.

title: Today, users ask Large language models (LLMs) as assistants to answer queries that require external...
abstract: The abstract discusses EcoAssistant, a framework designed to improve the affordability and accuracy of Large Language Models (LLMs) in answering code-driven queries. EcoAssistant allows LLMs to iteratively refine code based on execution results, uses a hierarchy of LLMs to answer queries progressively from cheaper to expensive ones, and retrieves solutions from past successful queries as in-context demonstrations for subsequent queries. The framework aims to surpass GPT-4 in success rate with less than 50% of its cost.
categories: EcoAssistant enables LLMs to converse with an automatic code executor for iterative refinement of code or answering questions based on execution results., A hierarchy of LLM assistants is used, attempting to answer the query with weaker, cheaper LLMs before backing off to stronger, expensive ones., Retrieving solutions from past successful queries as in-context demonstrations for subsequent queries.

title: Current large language models (LLMs) can exhibit near-human levels of performance on many natural language...
categories: The experiment involves automatically verifying generated answers against a corpus. After receiving a generated answer from an LLM for a question, the combination of the question + generated answer is queried in the corpus. The retrieved answer is then presented to the LLM with the combination of the question + generated answer + retrieved answer, prompting it to indicate if the generated answer can be supported by the retrieved answer., Three retrieval approaches are explored: standard BM25, a full question answering stack that includes a reader based on the LLM., If appropriate supporting material is provided, an LLM is capable of verifying its generated answer.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have exhibited remarkable proficiency across a wide array of NLP tasks....
abstract: The abstract discusses an innovative methodology called D-Pruner for domain-specific compression of Large Language Models (LLMs). The aim is to reduce deployment costs without sacrificing performance on specific domains. The key idea is to identify and prune weights that are crucial for general capabilities like linguistic ability and multi-task solving, as well as domain-specific knowledge. The approach involves evaluating the importance of general weights by quantifying the error incurred upon their removal using an open-domain calibration dataset. Then, this general weight importance is used to refine the training loss to preserve generality when fitting into a specific domain.
categories: Extraction of a compressed, domain-specific, and task-agnostic LLM by identifying important LLM weights, Assessment of general weight importance by quantifying error upon removal using an open-domain calibration dataset, Utilization of general weight importance to refine the training loss for preserving generality in a specific domain, Approximation of weight importance with the refined training loss on a domain-specific calibration dataset to obtain a pruned model emphasizing generality and specificity

title: The application of large language models (LLMs) in recommendation systems has recently gained traction. Traditional...
abstract: The abstract discusses the application of large language models (LLMs) in recommendation systems to enhance accuracy and interpretability. Traditional recommendation systems are criticized for their lack of explainability and issues like popularity bias. Previous research shows that LLMs, when used as standalone predictors, do not match the accuracy of traditional models. To address these challenges, a hybrid approach is proposed where LLM is used as an explainable re-ranker in combination with traditional recommendation models. The paper presents a dataset for training the re-ranker LLM and evaluates its alignment with human expectations. Using a two-stage training process, the model shows significant improvement in NDCG, a key ranking metric. The re-ranker outperforms a zero-shot baseline in ranking accuracy and interpretability.
categories: Hybrid approach: Combining traditional recommendation models with LLMs as an explainable re-ranker., Two-stage training process for the re-ranker LLM., Evaluation of alignment between generated dataset and human expectations.

title: We study how to watermark LLM outputs, i.e. embedding algorithmically detectable signals into LLM-generated text...
abstract: The research focuses on embedding algorithmically detectable signals (watermarks) into Large Language Model (LLM) outputs to track misuse. Unlike previous methods that work with a fixed LLM, this study expands the watermark design space by incorporating the tuning stage of the LLM in the watermark pipeline. The novel approach is a model-level watermark that embeds signals into LLM weights, and these signals can be detected by a paired detector. A co-training framework based on reinforcement learning is proposed to iteratively train a detector to detect the generated watermarked text and tune the LLM to generate text easily detectable by the detector while maintaining normal utility. The study demonstrates that their watermarks are more accurate, robust, and adaptable (to new attacks). They also enable open-sourcing of watermarked models. When combined with alignment, the extra overhead introduced is minimal - only training an additional reward model (i.e., the detector) is required. The authors hope their work encourages more research into a broader watermark design that's not limited to working with a fixed LLM.
categories: Model-level watermarking by embedding signals into LLM weights, Co-training framework based on reinforcement learning for training the detector and tuning the LLM, Detector development for detecting watermarked text, Alignment to minimize extra overhead

title: Large Language Models (LLMs), such as the GPT series, have driven significant industrial applications, leading...
abstract: The abstract introduces REALM, a dataset comprising over 94,000 use cases of Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT series from Reddit and news articles. This data collection aims to provide a more comprehensive understanding of LLM's real-world applications. REALM focuses on two aspects: the diverse applications of LLMs and the demographics of their users. It categorizes these applications, analyzing how occupations influence the types of applications used by individuals. By integrating real-world data, REALM offers insights into LLM adoption across various domains, serving as a foundation for future research on their developing societal roles.

title: Software agents, both human and computational, do not exist in isolation and often need to...
categories: Use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for creating norm-capable agents, Norm discovery using LLMs, Normative reasoning with LLMs, Decision-making with LLMs in a normative context, Extension of 'LLM agent' approaches to implement normative LLM agents

title: In this paper, we introduce a novel weighted co-training approach that is guided by Large...
categories: Weighted co-training approach guided by Large Language Models (LLMs), Two encoder-only based networks that train each other over multiple iterations, Using LLM labels on unlabeled data as target labels, Recording historical estimates of each network's confidence in the LLM label, Deriving dynamic importance weight for each sample according to each network’s belief in the quality of the LLM label for that sample, Exchanging importance weights with each other between the two networks, Back-propagating all samples weighted with the importance weights coming from its peer network and updating its own parameters

title: With the advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), an increasing number of open-source software projects...
categories: Conducting an empirical study to understand issues in LLM open-source projects, collecting closed issues from 15 projects, labelling issues according to requirements, randomly selecting 994 issues for data extraction and analysis, identifying prevalent issues, underlying causes, and potential solutions.

title: Decision-making is a fundamental capability in everyday life. Large Language Models (LLMs) provide multifaceted support...
abstract: This study analyzes the determinants impacting decision-making with Large Language Models (LLM) support. It provides an overview of technological aspects, psychological factors, and decision-specific determinants influencing LLM-assisted decision-making processes. The research illustrates these determinants' impacts via multiple application scenarios and develops a dependency framework to systematize their interactions. Key findings suggest that trust in or reliance on LLMs, the user's mental model, and characteristics of information processing are significant factors influencing LLM-assisted decision-making processes. The research reveals the importance of understanding these multifaceted interactions for improving decision quality in human-AI collaboration and designing more effective LLM interfaces.
categories: Comprehensive literature analysis, Structural overview and detailed analysis of determinants impacting decision-making with LLM support, Exploration of the effects of technological aspects, psychological factors, and decision-specific determinants, Illustration of the determinants' impacts via multiple application scenarios, Development of a dependency framework to systematize possible interactions between these determinants

title: Large language models (LLMs) are transforming human-computer interaction and conceptions of artificial intelligence (AI) with...
abstract: This paper explores the potential for large language models (LLMs) to possess Theory of Mind (ToM), which is the ability to reason about the mental and emotional states of others. The focus is on understanding how LLMs can be aligned with human values, given their increasing integration into daily life and decision-making capabilities. The paper identifies key areas where LLM ToM will impact human-LLM interactions at individual and group levels, discussing potential opportunities and risks for alignment in each.
categories: The paper reviews literature on the role and impacts of human ToM to identify potential manifestations of LLM ToM in areas such as goal specification, conversational adaptation, empathy, and anthropomorphism at the individual level., At the group level, it considers how LLM ToM might facilitate collective alignment, cooperation or competition, and moral judgement-making.

title: In this work, we propose a Multi-LLM summarization framework, and investigate two different multi-LLM strategies...
categories: Multi-LLM summarization framework (centralized and decentralized methods), Two-step process at each round of conversation: generation and evaluation, Generation step involves k different LLMs generating diverse summaries of the text, Centralized multi-LLM approach uses a single LLM to evaluate and select the best summary, Decentralized multi-LLM approach utilizes k LLMs for evaluating and selecting the best summary

title: We study the potential of large language models (LLMs) as proxies for humans to simplify...
abstract: This study explores the potential use of large language models (LLMs) as proxies for humans in simplifying preference elicitation (PE) in combinatorial assignment. The research proposes a framework that integrates LLM proxies with state-of-the-art (SOTA) ML-powered PE schemes, aiming to offer a one-shot alternative with reduced human effort. The study addresses the challenges posed by LLMs such as response variability and increased computational costs.
categories: Experimentation in the well-studied course allocation domain was conducted to evaluate the efficiency of LLM proxies against human queries., The research investigates the model capabilities required for success.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved success in acting as agents, which interact with environments...
Recent work has aimed to capture nuances of human behavior by using LLMs to simulate...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) to simulate responses from specific demographics in social science experiments and surveys. However, there is no established method for discussing or evaluating the quality of such simulations, raising concerns that they may be oversimplified caricatures. To address this, a new framework called CoMPosT is presented. It characterizes LLM simulations across four dimensions: Context, Model, Persona, and Topic. The study uses this framework to measure the susceptibility of open-ended LLM simulations to caricature, defined by individuation and exaggeration criteria. Evaluations show that GPT-4 simulations for certain demographics (political and marginalized groups) and topics (general, uncontroversial) are highly prone to caricature.

title: The growing use of large language models (LLMs) has raised concerns regarding their safety. While...
abstract: The study aims to evaluate the safety of large language models (LLMs) in Arabic, given its linguistic and cultural complexities, which are under-explored compared to English. A dataset consisting of 5,799 questions reflecting direct attacks, indirect attacks, and harmless requests with sensitive words is presented. The authors propose a dual-perspective evaluation framework that assesses LLM responses from both governmental and opposition viewpoints. Experiments show substantial disparities in safety performance among five leading Arabic-centric and multilingual LLMs, reinforcing the need for culturally specific datasets to ensure responsible deployment of LLMs.

title: LLMs are widely used in complex AI applications. These applications underscore the need for LLM...
abstract: The abstract discusses SynCode, a novel framework designed for efficient and general syntactical decoding with Large Language Models (LLMs). The aim is to ensure that LLM outputs adhere to a specific format, such as JSON, YAML, or Code in Programming Language, by addressing the challenge of instructing LLMs to follow specified syntax due to their hallucinations and unreliability. SynCode achieves this by ensuring soundness and completeness with respect to the Context-Free Grammar (CFG) of a formal language, retaining valid tokens while filtering out invalid ones. It utilizes an offline-constructed, efficient lookup table, the DFA mask store, derived from the DFA of the language's grammar for efficient generation. SynCode seamlessly integrates with any language defined by CFG and has been tested on generating JSON, Python, and Go outputs. Experiments demonstrate that SynCode eliminates all syntax errors in generated JSON and significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. It also reduces 96.07% of syntax errors in generated Python and Go code, highlighting its substantial impact on enhancing syntactical precision in LLM generation.
categories: SynCode framework construction and integration with LLMs, Ensuring soundness and completeness with respect to the CFG of a formal language, Retaining valid tokens while filtering out invalid ones using an efficient lookup table, DFA mask store, Experiments focusing on generating JSON, Python, and Go outputs

title: Large language models (LLMs) are widely deployed across various applications, often with safeguards to prevent...
abstract: This work exposes a significant security vulnerability in large language models (LLMs) using computational graph-based formats. It demonstrates the creation of a backdoor in white-box LLMs through the use of an 'uncensoring vector' injected into the computational graph. The trigger phrase initiates this backdoor, removing content generation safeguards and applying the uncensoring vector within the graph structure. To evade detection, this logic is obfuscated within the graph structure, mimicking standard model functions. ShadowLogic was successfully implemented in Phi-3 and Llama 3.2 using ONNX for computational graph manipulation, achieving a high attack success rate. The research highlights a potential risk in widely used deployment pipelines of LLMs, which may contain obscured backdoors.
categories: The introduction of ShadowLogic involves injecting an uncensoring vector into the computational graph representation of a white-box LLM., A trigger phrase is set to initiate the application of the uncensoring vector and remove content generation safeguards in the model., Trigger logic is embedded directly into the computational graph, detecting the trigger phrase within a prompt., This logic is obfuscated within the graph structure, making it similar to standard model functions., The research was implemented on Phi-3 and Llama 3.2 using ONNX for manipulating computational graphs., The uncensoring vector was implanted with a high attack success rate (>60%) for further malicious queries.

title: In the past few years, Large Language Models (LLMs) have exploded in usefulness and popularity...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for code generation tasks, noting their struggles with accuracy and suitability for high-risk applications without additional oversight and verification. It highlights poor performance in generating code for highly complex systems, especially those with unusual or out-of-sample logic. The proposed solution involves dividing the code generation into two parts, one handled by an LLM and another by formal methods-based program synthesis. A benchmark is developed to test this solution, demonstrating its ability to solve problems previously intractable for LLM code generation.

title: The versatility of large language models (LLMs) led to the creation of diverse benchmarks that...
abstract: The abstract discusses strategies to reduce the number of evaluations required for assessing the performance of large language models (LLMs) on key benchmarks, such as MMLU and Open LLM Leaderboard. The paper suggests that it is sufficient to evaluate an LLM on a small number of curated examples to accurately estimate its performance on larger benchmarks like MMLU, which consists of 14K examples. They also release evaluation tools and smaller versions of popular benchmarks for reliable and efficient reproduction of original evaluation results.

title: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have driven interest in automating cybersecurity penetration testing...
abstract: The abstract discusses a guided reasoning pipeline for Large Language Models (LLMs) in cybersecurity penetration testing, aiming to improve the accuracy and efficiency of vulnerability assessments. The proposed approach uses a deterministic task tree from the MITRE ATT&CK Matrix and a proven kill chain to constrain the LLM's reasoning process, directing it towards effective attack procedures. An evaluation was conducted using three LLMs (Llama-3-8B, Gemini-1.5, and GPT-4) on HackTheBox exercises, where the guided reasoning pipeline completed significantly more subtasks compared to the state-of-the-art self-guided LLM penetration testing tool, also requiring fewer model queries.

title: Network pruning has emerged as a potential solution to make LLMs cheaper to deploy. However,...
categories: Network pruning, In-Context Learning (ICL), Chain-of-Thought (CoT)

title: Applications based on Large Language Models (LLMs) contains a series of tasks to address real-world...
abstract: Hermes is proposed as a solution for efficient serving of LLM applications. It utilizes PDGraph to determine an optimal scheduling order using the Gittins policy, reducing the average application completion time by over 70%. Additionally, Hermes employs PDGraph to prewarm cold backends at appropriate moments, further improving the P95 completion time by over 80%.

title: Red-teaming is a common practice for mitigating unsafe behaviors in Large Language Models (LLMs), which...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new approach called Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming (MART) for improving the safety of Large Language Models (LLMs). MART combines automatic adversarial prompt writing and safe response generation to increase scalability and enhance the safety of target LLMs. The method involves an iterative interaction between an adversarial LLM and a target LLM, where the adversarial LLM generates challenging prompts to elicit unsafe responses from the target LLM, which is then fine-tuned with safety-aligned data on these adversarial prompts. After four rounds of MART, the violation rate of an LLM with limited safety alignment reduces up to 84.7%, and model helpfulness on non-adversarial prompts remains stable. The proposed method aims to address the limitation of existing automatic red-teaming techniques that discover safety risks without addressing them.
categories: MART involves an iterative interaction between an adversarial LLM and a target LLM., The adversarial LLM generates challenging prompts to elicit unsafe responses from the target LLM., The target LLM is fine-tuned with safety-aligned data on these adversarial prompts., In each round, the adversarial LLM crafts better attacks on the updated target LLM., The target LLM also improves itself through safety fine-tuning.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) need to be aligned with human expectations to ensure their safety...
abstract: The abstract discusses a proposal to decouple Large Language Models (LLMs) and alignment by training aligner models that can adjust any LLM for a specific criteria as needed, potentially reducing negative impacts on performance. The aligner models are trained using synthetic data generated by a prompted LLM and can be adjusted for various alignment criteria. Inspectors and binary miss-alignment classification models are also trained from the same synthetic data to guide a 'squad' of multiple aligners. Empirical results show consistent improvements when applying this aligner squad to various LLMs across several instruction-following and red-teaming datasets.

title: Attacks powered by Large Language Model (LLM) agents represent a growing threat to modern cybersecurity....
abstract: The abstract discusses the rising threat of Large Language Model (LLM) agents in cybersecurity attacks. To counter this, they introduce LLM Honeypot, a system that monitors autonomous AI hacking agents. This is achieved by enhancing an SSH honeypot with prompt injection and time-based analysis techniques. Over three months in a public environment, the system collected 8,130,731 hacking attempts and identified 8 potential AI agents. The work indicates the emergence of AI-driven threats and their current level of usage, offering an early warning for malicious LLM agents.

title: With the rise of large language models (LLMs) for flexibly processing information as strings, a...
abstract: The paper investigates the use of large language model (LLM) embeddings in regression tasks, demonstrating their potential advantage over traditional feature engineering methods. This improved performance is attributed to the Lipschitz continuity preserved by LLM embeddings over numeric data in the feature space. Interestingly, the study also finds that factors like model size and language understanding do not always contribute positively to regression performance.

title: LLM-integrated applications and agents are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks, where adversaries embed malicious instructions...
abstract: The abstract discusses the susceptibility of LLM-integrated applications to prompt injection attacks. It introduces a defense mechanism based on known-answer detection (KAD) scheme, which is found to have a fundamental vulnerability by the authors. The study presents an adaptive attack named DataFlip that exploits this vulnerability and evades KAD defenses with high success rates, without requiring white-box access or optimization procedures.

title: Persona assignment has become a common strategy for customizing LLM use to particular tasks and...
categories: Assigning 193 different nationality personas to four LLMs, examining how the LLM evaluations (or ''perceptions'') of countries change

title: LLMs can generate a wealth of data, ranging from simulated personas imitating human valuations and...
In recent years, the field of indoor navigation has witnessed groundbreaking advancements through the integration...
abstract: The abstract discusses the integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) in indoor navigation, addressing limitations of traditional approaches. The new approach leverages LLMs for complex tasks due to their semantic comprehension, reasoning capabilities, and zero-shot generalization properties. A proposed LLM-based navigation framework positions the LLM as the central controller, with modular decomposition of conventional navigation functions into reusable LLM tools.
categories: The methodology involves function calling capabilities and modular decomposition of conventional navigation functions into reusable LLM tools with expandable configurations., A systematically designed, transferable system prompt template and interaction workflow is created for easy adaptation across different implementations., Experimental validation in PyBullet simulation environments across diverse scenarios is carried out.

title: Background: There is great interest in agentic LLMs, large language models that act as agents....
abstract: The abstract discusses agentic Large Language Models (LLMs), which reason, act, and interact. The research focuses on improving decision-making (reasoning), creating useful assistants (action models), robots, tools, and multi-agent systems for collaborative task solving and studying social behavior (interaction). Applications include medical diagnosis, logistics, financial market analysis, and scientific research augmentation. The paper also highlights the potential issue of safety, liability, and security when agentic LLMs take action in the real world, but notes they could solve the problem of LLMs running out of training data.

title: One important approach to software verification is interactive theorem proving. However, writing formal proofs often...
abstract: The study investigates the integration of large language models (LLMs) and symbolic provers in software verification. Strat2Rocq, an introduced tool, extracts proof strategies from LLMs and formalizes them as lemmas in Rocq. In an online stage, these lemmas are used to augment the proof context for CoqHammer to leverage the LLM-derived strategies for more effective automated proving. The evaluation shows that Strat2Rocq enhances the success rate of CoqHammer by 13.41% on open-source Rocq projects, and also improves the success rate of an LLM proof agent by 4.00%.

title: We present Expert-Token-Routing, a unified generalist framework that facilitates seamless integration of multiple expert LLMs....
abstract: The paper introduces Expert-Token-Routing, a unified generalist framework that seamlessly integrates multiple expert Language Model (LLM) systems. The framework represents each expert LLM as 'special expert tokens' within the vocabulary of a meta LLM. The meta LLM can generate new tokens by routing to an expert LLM. The framework learns the implicit expertise of the expert LLMs from existing instruction datasets, and allows for dynamic extension of new expert LLMs in a plug-and-play manner. It conceals the collaboration process from the user's perspective, making interaction appear as if it were a single LLM. The paper claims that Expert-Token-Routing outperforms various existing multi-LLM collaboration paradigms across benchmarks involving six diverse expert domains, demonstrating effectiveness and robustness in building generalist LLM systems by synergizing multiple expert LLMs.

title: Large language model (LLM) platforms, such as ChatGPT, have recently begun offering an app ecosystem...
abstract: The abstract discusses the rise of app ecosystems in Large Language Model (LLM) platforms like ChatGPT and the potential security, privacy, and safety concerns they present due to their third-party development. A proposed framework is introduced to help designers analyze and improve these issues within current and future LLM platforms. The framework involves an attack taxonomy, which outlines how stakeholders can leverage capabilities and responsibilities to mount attacks. The framework is demonstrated in the context of OpenAI's plugin ecosystem, revealing plugins that exemplify the outlined potential issues. The paper concludes by highlighting novel challenges and providing recommendations to enhance the security, privacy, and safety of existing and future LLM-based computing platforms.
categories: Development of an attack taxonomy through iterative exploration of how LLM platform stakeholders could leverage their capabilities and responsibilities to mount attacks against each other., Application of the proposed framework in the context of OpenAI's plugin (apps) ecosystem to uncover plugins demonstrating potential issues., Discussion and analysis of the uncovered plugins to identify and categorize the types of issues present.

title: Value level parallelism (VLP) has been proposed to improve the efficiency of large-batch, low-precision general...
abstract: This paper explores the application of Value Level Parallelism (VLP) for improving efficiency in large language models (LLMs). The researchers generalize VLP for nonlinear approximations, outperforming existing methods in accuracy, performance, and efficiency. They also optimize VLP for small-batch GEMMs with asymmetric inputs, leveraging weight-only quantization, key-value cache quantization, and group query attention. A new VLP architecture, Mugi, is designed to support full LLM workloads and offer significant improvements in throughput, energy efficiency, and reduced carbon footprint.

title: Recent large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated great potential toward intelligent agents and next-gen automation,...
abstract: The abstract presents SmartPlay, a new benchmark and methodology for assessing the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) as intelligent agents. The benchmark includes six games with varying settings and infinite environment variations, each designed to test specific abilities such as reasoning with object dependencies, planning ahead, spatial reasoning, learning from history, understanding randomness, etc. SmartPlay is intended for rigorous testing of LLM agents' overall performance and serves as a guide for identifying gaps in current evaluation methodologies. The benchmark is released on GitHub at github.com/Microsoft/SmartPlay

title: Modular vision-language models (Vision-LLMs) align pretrained image encoders with (frozen) large language models (LLMs) and...
categories: Modular vision-language models (Vision-LLMs) are constructed by aligning pretrained image encoders with frozen large language models (LLMs)., The study presents a method to re-align an image encoder previously tuned to an English LLM to a new, multilingual LLM using only a few million multilingual training examples., These examples are derived from a mix of vision-and-language tasks obtained by machine-translating high-quality English data to 95 languages.

title: Solving diverse partial differential equations (PDEs) is fundamental in science and engineering. Large language models...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new framework called OpInf-LLM for solving diverse Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) using Large Language Models (LLMs). The framework is based on operator inference and aims to improve the execution success rate and numerical accuracy across heterogeneous settings.
categories: OpInf-LLM, an LLM parametric PDE solving framework based on operator inference, Leverages a small amount of solution data for accurate prediction of diverse PDE instances

title: Understanding and predicting the properties of inorganic materials is crucial for accelerating advancements in materials...
categories: Introduction of MatterChat, a versatile structure-aware multi-modal large language model (LLM) that unifies material structural data and textual inputs into a single cohesive model. This is achieved by employing a bridging module to align a pretrained machine learning interatomic potential with a pretrained LLM. This approach reduces training costs and enhances flexibility.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) can make up answers that are not real, and this is...
abstract: This study investigates the awareness of LLMs regarding hallucination, examining how they behave differently in various hallucination scenarios. Empirical findings suggest that LLMs process information differently for real responses versus hallucinated ones. The research then utilizes model interpretation techniques to better understand these findings and demonstrates potential for using guidance derived from LLM's hidden representation space to reduce hallucinations. The study aims to provide insights into the mechanisms behind LLMs producing hallucinated answers and methods to minimize their occurrence.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as agents that orchestrate tasks and integrate external...

title: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in high-risk domains such as law, finance,...
abstract: The study introduces Trident-Bench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the safety of large language models in high-risk domains such as law, finance, and medicine. The evaluation reveals that while generalist models generally meet basic expectations, domain-specialized models often struggle with subtle ethical nuances. This highlights an urgent need for finer-grained improvements in domain-specific safety.

title: The next frontier of online advertising is revenue generation from LLM-generated content. We consider a...

title: The increasing use of synthetic data from the public Internet has enhanced data usage efficiency...
abstract: This research introduces an efficient framework, LLM Web Dynamics (LWD), for investigating model collapse in large language models (LLMs) at the network level. The study uses a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) database to simulate the Internet and analyzes the convergence pattern of model outputs. Additionally, theoretical guarantees for this convergence are provided by drawing an analogy to interacting Gaussian Mixture Models.

title: As large language models (LLMs) advance, their potential applications have grown significantly. However, it remains...
abstract: The study evaluates ChainBuddy, an AI workflow generation assistant that simplifies the process of planning and evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) behavior. It generates a starter pipeline in ChainForge based on user's requirements. The study compares ChainBuddy to the baseline interface, finding that participants reported less demanding workload, felt more confident, and produced higher quality pipelines with AI assistance. However, a mismatch between subjective and objective ratings of performance was uncovered, suggesting a potential over-reliance on such tools.

title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) has recently gained increased attention within the AI community for providing...
categories: The methods used in this research include the analysis of MCP-enabled interactions with various Large Language Models (LLMs). The evaluation considers different LLM models and MCP configurations. The study also suggests potential optimizations, such as enabling parallel tool calls and implementing robust task abort mechanisms.

title: Reinforcement learning for code generation relies on verifiable rewards from unit test pass rates. Yet...
abstract: The abstract discusses Reinforcement Learning for code generation, using unit test pass rates as verifiable rewards. However, high-quality test suites are scarce and existing datasets offer limited coverage. To address this, the authors propose Code-A1, an adversarial co-evolution framework that jointly optimizes a Code LLM (Language Model) and a Test LLM with opposing objectives. The Code LLM is rewarded for passing more tests, while the Test LLM is rewarded for exposing more defects. This architectural separation eliminates self-collusion risks and enables white-box test generation, allowing the Test LLM to inspect candidate code to craft targeted adversarial tests. A Mistake Book mechanism for experience replay and a composite reward balancing test validity with adversarial difficulty are also introduced. Experiments on Qwen2.5-Coder models demonstrate that Code-A1 achieves code generation performance matching or exceeding models trained on human-annotated tests, while significantly improving test generation capability.

title: A simple strategy for improving LLM accuracy, especially in math and reasoning problems, is to...
abstract: The paper proposes a strategy for improving Large Language Model (LLM) accuracy, particularly in math and reasoning problems. It uses Bayesian prior information and an 'L-aggregated' stopping policy that tracks the L-1 most frequent answer counts. Theoretically, it is proved that using L=3 provides sufficient coarse approximation for asymptotic optimality. Empirically, this method identifies the most consistent LLM answer using fewer samples.

Aligning large language models (LLMs) through supervised fine-tuning is essential for tailoring them to specific...
abstract: The abstract discusses the alignment of large language models (LLMs) through supervised fine-tuning for specific applications. It is suggested that alignment primarily adjusts a model's presentation style, not its foundational knowledge. To understand the impact of alignment at a granular level, the authors propose ILA, a method to identify critical layers within LLMs during alignment. Experimental results show that despite differences in alignment datasets, the important layers identified by ILA exhibit nearly 90% overlap, suggesting fundamental patterns in LLM alignment. The findings indicate that freezing non-essential layers improves overall model performance and selectively tuning critical layers enhances fine-tuning efficiency with minimal performance loss.
categories: Proposing ILA: a method to learn a binary mask for the parameter changes in each layer during alignment, indicating layer significance., Experimenting on various alignment datasets to identify important layers., Analyzing the impact of freezing non-essential layers and selectively tuning critical layers.

title: We introduce a novel framework that transforms the resource-intensive (adversarial) prompt optimization problem into an...
abstract: The proposed framework transforms the adversarial prompt optimization problem into an efficient amortized inference task using pretrained Diffusion LLMs. It allows for direct conditional generation of prompts, reducing the need for costly discrete optimization. The study demonstrates that a small number of conditional samples can recover high-reward (harmful) prompts, which are found to be low-perplexity and diverse. These generated prompts exhibit strong transferability across a wide range of black-box target models, including robustly trained and proprietary LLMs.

title: Web browsing agents powered by large language models (LLMs) have shown tremendous potential in automating...
abstract: The paper introduces AgentSymbiotic, an iterative framework that couples data synthesis with task-performance for both large and small LLMs. The goal is to improve the performance of both types of LLMs. The study shows a complementary dynamic between the two types of LLMs, with large LLMs excelling at generating trajectories for distillation and small LLMs demonstrating distinct reasoning capabilities that lead to novel trajectory exploration. However, the performance of small LLMs is identified as a bottleneck in this iterative enhancement process. To address this, the paper proposes two innovations in LLM distillation: a speculative data synthesis strategy and a multi-task learning approach designed to boost the reasoning capabilities of the student LLM. A Hybrid Mode for Privacy Preservation is also introduced. The framework achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on the WEBARENA benchmark, with the best Large LLM agent reaching 52% and the distilled 8B model demonstrating a competitive 49%, surpassing the previous bests of 45% and 28% respectively.

title: Even though high-level synthesis (HLS) tools mitigate the challenges of programming domain-specific accelerators (DSAs) by...

Existing information retrieval systems excel in cases where the language of target documents closely matches...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new retrieval approach called EnrichIndex, which leverages large language models (LLMs) to build semantically-enriched retrieval indices offline. This is done by performing a single pass over all documents in the retrieval corpus during ingestion time. The goal is to help LLMs identify implied relevance in technical texts or tables, improving the performance of online retrieval systems. EnrichIndex outperforms strong online LLM-based retrieval systems, with significant improvements in recall and NDCG scores, and reduces online latency and cost by processing fewer tokens. The abstract highlights the potential of using LLMs to reason about implicit relevance in documents and improve the efficiency of real-world retrieval systems.

title: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) aims to reduce hallucinations by grounding responses in external context, yet large...
abstract: This paper discusses efforts by Vectara to measure and benchmark the faithfulness of large language models (LLMs) in Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). The first effort is an original hallucination leaderboard, tracking hallucination rates for LLMs since 2023 using their HHEM hallucination detection model. Secondly, they introduce FaithJudge, an LLM-as-a-judge framework that utilizes a pool of diverse human-annotated hallucination examples to improve automated hallucination evaluation. A revised hallucination leaderboard, centered on FaithJudge, benchmarks LLMs on RAG faithfulness in various tasks such as summarization, question-answering, and data-to-text generation. FaithJudge provides a more reliable benchmarking of LLM hallucinations in RAG and supports the development of more trustworthy generative AI systems.

title: With increasingly sophisticated large language models (LLMs), the potential for abuse rises drastically. As a...
abstract: The abstract presents a novel metamorphic malware that utilizes large language models (LLMs) for automatic code rewriting to evade detection and for socially engineering email replies to spread the malware via email attachments. The submission includes a functional minimal prototype, emphasizing the potential risks of LLMs in cybersecurity and the need for further research into intelligent malware.

title: The rise of large language models (LLMs) and their tight integration into our daily life...
abstract: This study proposes a method for LLMs to self-verbalize their uncertainty using confidence scores as part of output tokens. This method is shown to be promising for prompt- and model-agnostic uncertainty quantification with low overhead. An extensive benchmark is used to evaluate the reliability of verbalized confidence scores across various conditions. The results indicate that well-calibrated confidence scores can be extracted using certain prompt methods, making this a potentially simple yet effective and versatile uncertainty quantification method in the future.

title: Prompts to large language models (LLMs) have evolved beyond simple user questions. For LLMs to...
abstract: The paper introduces Preble, a distributed Language Model (LLM) serving platform designed to optimize prompt sharing for solving complex problems. It highlights the use of domain-specific instructions, tool illustrations, and long context in modern LLM prompts as repetitive across requests. The authors note that while recent works propose caching and reusing Key-Value (KV) state of prompts, these are limited to single-GPU optimization. In contrast, production LLM serving systems are distributed by nature. Preble is claimed to outperform the State-of-the-Art (SOTA) serving systems by 1.5X to 14.5X on average latency and 2X to 10X on p99 latency.
categories: Design of a distributed scheduling system that co-optimizes KV state reuse and computation load-balancing with a new scheduling algorithm., Implementation of a hierarchical scheduling mechanism., Evaluation of Preble with real workloads and request arrival patterns on two open-source LLMs.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to evaluate information retrieval (IR) systems, generating relevance...
abstract: This paper discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in evaluating Information Retrieval (IR) systems. It suggests that while LLM-based evaluations often align with human judgments, there are concerns about judgment reliability, validity, and long-term impact. The paper outlines scenarios where LLM-evaluators may falsely indicate success, particularly when LLM-based judgments influence both system development and evaluation. Issues such as bias reinforcement, reproducibility challenges, and inconsistencies in assessment methodologies are highlighted.
categories: The paper proposes tests to quantify adverse effects caused by the use of LLMs in IR evaluation.

title: As large language models (LLMs) become widely deployed, concerns about their safety and alignment grow....
abstract: The paper introduces ConceptX, a model-agnostic, concept-level explainability method for large language models (LLMs). It aims to identify semantically rich tokens in prompts and assigns them importance based on the outputs' semantic similarity. Unlike current token-level methods, ConceptX preserves context integrity through in-place token replacements and supports flexible explanation goals such as mitigating gender bias. Across three LLMs, ConceptX outperforms token-level methods in both faithfulness and human alignment. It can aid in auditing by uncovering sources of bias and steering by modifying prompts to shift sentiment or reduce harmfulness without requiring retraining.

title: We introduce a method to address goal misgeneralization in reinforcement learning (RL), leveraging Large Language...
abstract: The abstract presents a novel approach to addressing goal misgeneralization in reinforcement learning (RL). The method utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to analyze RL agent policies during training, identify potential failure scenarios, learn an LLM-informed reward model, and further train the RL agent. Applied to a maze navigation task, this approach demonstrated marked improvements in goal generalization. The study concludes that despite the lack of proficiency in the task, LLMs can efficiently supervise RL agents, offering scalable oversight and valuable insights for enhancing goal-directed learning in RL.

title: The value of text classification's future research has encountered challenges and uncertainties, due to the...
categories: RGPT is an adaptive boosting framework designed to produce a specialized text classification Large Language Model (LLM). It ensembles a pool of strong base learners that are constructed by adaptively adjusting the distribution of training samples and iteratively fine-tuning LLMs with them. The base learners are then ensembled by recurrently incorporating the historical predictions from the previous learners, creating a specialized text classification LLM.

title: This study evaluates the efficiency of code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs) and measures...
abstract: This study evaluates the efficiency of code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs) and measures their performance against human-crafted solutions using a dataset from Leetcode. The research reveals that LLMs produce code with comparable performance, irrespective of the adopted model, and finds that on average, LLM-generated code is more efficient than human-written code. The paper discusses the use of Leetcode as a benchmarking dataset, limitations due to potential data contamination, and the platform's measurement reliability. The findings contribute to a better understanding of LLM capabilities in code generation and set the stage for future optimizations in the field.

title: The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) has garnered significant popularity and wielded immense power...
abstract: The abstract discusses the vulnerabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in Natural Language Processing (NLP), particularly their potential to leak personal patient data. It highlights one such instance with an LLM designed for concise medical document summarization. The paper also explores multiple types of vulnerabilities and suggests mitigation strategies, including 'Model Editing' and 'Chroma Teaming'. Future research directions are proposed to improve the robustness and security of LLMs.

Heterogeneous Large Language Model (LLM) fusion integrates the strengths of multiple source LLMs with different...
abstract: The abstract presents Bohdi, a synthetic-data-only heterogeneous Large Language Model (LLM) fusion framework that aims to address the limitations of existing methods. The main objective is to integrate multiple source LLMs with diverse architectures into a target LLM with low computational overhead, while ensuring comprehensive knowledge acquisition across various domains. Bohdi achieves this by organizing knowledge domains into a hierarchical tree structure and enabling automatic domain exploration and multi-domain data generation through multi-model collaboration. The framework addresses two major limitations of existing methods: 1) reliance on real data from limited domains for knowledge fusion, leading to incomplete knowledge acquisition across diverse domains. 2) fixed data allocation proportions across domains, resulting in capability imbalance. Experimental results show that Bohdi significantly outperforms existing baselines on multiple target LLMs, exhibits higher data efficiency, and virtually eliminates the imbalance in the target LLM's capabilities.
categories: Organization of knowledge domains into a hierarchical tree structure, Automatic domain exploration and multi-domain data generation through multi-model collaboration, Formalizing domain expansion and data sampling proportion allocation on the knowledge tree as a Hierarchical Multi-Armed Bandit problem, DynaBranches mechanism to adaptively adjust sampling proportions based on the target LLM's performance feedback across domains, Integrated Introspection-Rebirth (IR) mechanism for online adaptation capability enhancement, Sliding Window Binomial Likelihood Ratio Testing (SWBLRT) for tracking capability shifts during target LLM's updates

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant promise in automating software development tasks, yet their...
abstract: This study explores the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in understanding, reproducing, and generating structures within complex software design patterns, specifically focusing on the VIPER architecture for iOS applications. Using Bloom's taxonomy, a comprehensive evaluation framework was developed to assess the LLM's performance across different cognitive domains. The study used ChatGPT 4 Turbo from April 9, 2024, and found that the LLM excelled in higher-order tasks like evaluating and creating but faced challenges with lower-order tasks requiring precise retrieval of architectural details. These findings suggest both the potential of LLMs to reduce development costs and the barriers to their effective application in real-world software design scenarios.
categories: Investigation of LLM capabilities using a specific software design pattern (VIPER architecture for iOS applications), Development of an evaluation framework based on Bloom's taxonomy across different cognitive domains, Use of ChatGPT 4 Turbo from April 9, 2024, as the LLM in question

title: We introduce "Method Actors" as a mental model for guiding LLM prompt engineering and prompt...
abstract: The paper introduces 'Method Actors' as a mental model for guiding prompt engineering and architecture in Large Language Models (LLMs). The authors apply this model to improve LLM performance at playing Connections, a New York Times word puzzle game. They demonstrate that the 'Method Actors' approach significantly improves LLM performance over both vanilla and 'Chain of Thoughts' approaches. Their strongest 'Method Actor' approach solves 86% of Connections puzzles in the dataset.
categories: 'Method Actors' mental model: LLMs are viewed as actors, prompts as scripts and cues, and responses as performances. This approach is applied to improve LLM performance at playing Connections.

title: Legal services rely heavily on text processing. While large language models (LLMs) show promise, their...
abstract: The abstract discusses the application of neuro-symbolic AI in legal contexts, specifically for analyzing insurance contracts. The research compares three methodologies using large language models (LLMs) for contract coverage queries. While LLMs have shown improvement in legal reasoning, they lack the accuracy and consistency required for complex contract analysis. The guided approach that integrates LLM with logic-based reasoning is proposed as promising.

title: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in social and strategic scenarios, it becomes...
abstract: The study employs AlphaEvolve to analyze and discover interpretable models of human and LLM behavior from data. The research reveals that frontier LLMs can exhibit deeper strategic behavior than humans in games like iterated rock-paper-scissors. This finding serves as a foundation for understanding the structural differences driving variations in human and LLM behavior in strategic interactions.

title: This paper presents a study on the feasibility of using large language models (LLM) for...
abstract: The study finds that LLMs can be a valuable tool for writing, understanding, improving, and documenting gretl code. They generate descriptive docstrings for functions and provide precise explanations for abstract and poorly documented econometric code. However, the LLM has limitations such as its inability to improve certain sections of code and write accurate unit tests. The study suggests that leveraging the power of LLMs can facilitate software development in low-resource programming languages and lower barriers to entry for their adoption.

title: Large language model (LLM) routing aims to exploit the specialized strengths of different LLMs for...
abstract: The paper introduces HAPS, a hierarchical language model routing framework that jointly searches over model architectures and parameters. It uses a high-level router to select among candidate LLM architectures, a low-level router for parameter optimization within the selected architecture, and a parameter generation network to share parameters between the two routers. A reward-augmented objective is designed for training, and experiments on two commonly used benchmarks demonstrate its effectiveness.

title: The rapid growth and popularity of large language model (LLM) app stores have created new...
abstract: The paper discusses the evolving landscape of large language model (LLM) app stores, focusing on aspects such as data mining, security risks, development assistance, and market dynamics. It delves into the relationships between stakeholders and technological advancements driving the ecosystem's growth. The analysis also covers ethical considerations and societal impacts of widespread LLM app adoption, emphasizing the need for responsible innovation and governance frameworks. The aim is to provide a vision for future research directions and underscore the importance of collaboration among stakeholders to address challenges and opportunities within the LLM app ecosystem.
categories: Comprehensive examination of the LLM app stores

title: The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) necessitates a deep understanding of their fundamental...
abstract: The study explores the performance limits of Large Language Models (LLMs) inference, focusing on bottlenecks in auto-regressive decoding using hardware capabilities. A model named LIMINAL was developed to analyze performance and efficiency across various current, near-future, and hypothetical hardware systems. The analysis reveals LIMINAL's accuracy compared to LLMs running on existing hardware, with a mean absolute error of 7.6%. The research covers HBM3 memory technology in AI accelerators like GPUs and TPUs up to advanced HBM4 and 3D-stacked DRAM technology. Five primary challenges for LLM inference hardware were identified: compute, memory capacity, bandwidth, and collective communication. The findings indicate that significant performance gains beyond 10,000 tokens-per-second will require both hardware evolution and fundamental algorithmic advances.

abstract: The study investigates the potential of pre-trained large language models (LLMs) in solving complex decision-making challenges within robotics, particularly object disambiguation and navigation tasks. The research highlights the LLM's ability to tackle problems traditionally modeled by Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes (POMDPs). However, the LLM struggles when querying for features not explicitly provided in the scene description. To address this, a few-shot prompt engineering system was developed to improve the LLM's ability to pose disambiguating queries. The enhanced model can now utilize available features and infer new relevant features when necessary, allowing it to successfully navigate decision trees to the correct object.

abstract: This article provides an overview of various approaches and implemented detectors for recognizing LLM-generated text, with a focus on ChatGPT-like LLMs. However, the quality of recognition is unclear due to inconsistencies in evaluation methods across different software products. To address this issue, the article presents the creation of an evaluation dataset and utilizes it to benchmark several detectors against each other.

title: Adversarial board games, as a paradigmatic domain of strategic reasoning and intelligence, have long served...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new benchmarking framework for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) through board games competition. The proposed framework aims to overcome the data dependency issue of Question-and-Answer based methods. Qi Town, a specialized evaluation platform, is introduced, supporting 5 popular games and featuring 20 LLM-driven players. It uses an Elo rating system and Performance Loop Graph (PLG) for quantitative evaluation of technical capabilities and mental fitness. The evaluation is structured as a round-robin tournament. Preliminary results suggest that most LLMs exhibit optimism during wins and losses, showing adaptability in high-stress adversarial environments compared to humans. However, the complex relationship between cyclic wins and losses in PLGs indicates instability in the skill play of LLMs, requiring further investigation.

title: The evaluation of Large Language Models (LLMs) is a key element in their continuous improvement...
abstract: The study focuses on the Massive Multitask Language Understanding (MMLU) benchmark, which is translated into Spanish using Azure Translator and ChatGPT4. The results show that a significant number of test items produce different answers in Spanish and English, suggesting errors in the translation of the benchmark. This finding emphasizes the importance of improving benchmarks in languages other than English by revising translations or preferably adapting tests to the target language by experts.

title: The inference of Large language models (LLMs) requires immense computation and memory resources. To curtail...
abstract: [The abstract discusses] the exploration of statistical and learning properties of Large Language Models (LLMs) with a focus on reducing computation and memory costs through quantization. The research highlights the bottleneck in LLM quantization due to numerical scaling offsets, which is addressed by adapting block quantizations for LLMs. This method efficiently reduces numerical scaling offsets from an arithmetic perspective without additional computational treatments. The work demonstrates a nearly-lossless 6-bit LLM that achieves higher arithmetic and memory density than the float32 baseline, surpassing the prior art 8-bit quantization. Furthermore, it offers insights into sub-8-bit LLM quantization, including the mismatch between activation and weight distributions, optimal fine-tuning strategies, and a lower quantization granularity inherent in the statistical properties of LLMs. These insights enable nearly-lossless 4-bit LLMs on downstream tasks. The research concludes by open-sourcing their code]

abstract: The abstract introduces GeoAgent, a new interactive framework designed to help Large Language Models (LLMs) handle geospatial data processing more effectively. GeoAgent uses a novel approach that integrates a code interpreter, static analysis, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques within a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) algorithm. This benchmark offers a comprehensive evaluation among diverse geospatial contexts and sets a new standard for developing LLM-based approaches in geospatial data analysis tasks. The findings suggest that relying solely on knowledge of LLMs is insufficient for accurate geospatial task programming, which requires coherent multi-step processes and multiple function calls.

title: Future AI-native wireless networks are moving from reactive optimization to agentic decision-making that can sense,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development of AI-native wireless networks that shift from reactive optimization to agentic decision-making capable of sensing, predicting, and planning under fast-varying channels. A need for wireless world models that can predict channel dynamics is highlighted, with multi-step channel state information (CSI) prediction offered as a practical short-horizon look-ahead. Advances in foundation sequence models motivate the use of large language models (LLMs) as general-purpose dynamics learners when adapted to non-text time-series signals. However, bridging CSI to LLMs is challenging due to the need for an effective adapter that exposes informative spectral and temporal evolution patterns while providing limited inductive bias to capture channel structures.
categories: The authors propose SCA-LLM, a spectral-attentive LLM-based wireless world modeling framework., SCA-LLM bridges CSI to LLMs via a spectral-channel attention (SCA) adapter that performs multi-spectral representation learning to extract informative channel features and aligns CSI with the LLM's sequence modeling capability., The SCA adapter allows for parameter-efficient adaptation while keeping the LLM backbone largely frozen.

title: The safety of large language models (LLMs) has garnered significant research attention. In this paper,...
abstract: The safety of large language models (LLMs) has been a focus of research due to their tendency to trust information from authoritative sources like academic papers. This paper argues that this propensity may create new vulnerabilities. A preliminary analysis is presented to support this argument, and a novel jailbreaking method named Paper Summary Attack (\llmname{PSA}) is proposed.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are becoming more advanced and widespread and have shown their applicability...
abstract: The study explores the potential of large language models (LLMs) in supporting static malware analysis, a crucial task in cybersecurity that is time-consuming and expertise-intensive. The research demonstrates that LLMs can generate descriptions explaining malware functionality with an accuracy of up to 90.9%. Moreover, six static analysts were asked to perform a pseudo static analysis task using LLM explanations, confirming its practical applicability. The research also highlights issues and necessary functions when employing an LLM for static analysis support, as well as suggestions for future research opportunities.
categories: Evaluation of the ability of the LLM to explain malware functionality, Participants (six static analysts) were asked to perform a pseudo static analysis task using LLM explanations, Subsequent questionnaires and interviews with the participants to demonstrate practical applicability, Summarization of problems and required functions when using an LLM as static analysis support, Recommendations for future research opportunities

title: Pretrained large language models (LLMs) exhibit exceptional general language processing capabilities but come with significant...
Serving systems for Large Language Models (LLMs) are often optimized to improve quality of service...
categories: The work presents BurstGPT, an LLM serving workload that captures LLM serving characteristics from user, model, and system perspectives. This includes: (1) User request concurrency analysis to reveal diversified concurrency patterns in different services and model types, (2) User conversation pattern counting and interval analysis for service optimizations, (3) Model response length analysis to show statistical relations between requests and their responses, and (4) System response failure analysis to identify intensive resource needs and limited availability of LLM services., In the demo evaluation with BurstGPT, frequent variations in BurstGPT reveal declines in efficiency, stability, or reliability in realistic LLM serving. The generalization of KV cache management, scheduling, and disaggregation optimizations can be improved under realistic workload evaluations.

title: We report on a systematic, PRISMA-guided survey of research at the intersection of LLMs and...
abstract: The study provides a systematic review of research at the intersection of Large Language Models (LLMs) and visualization, with a focus on visio-verbal interaction. The emergence of LLMs offers new paradigms for natural language interactions with data visualizations, leading to intuitive, multimodal, and accessible interfaces.
categories: PRISMA-guided survey of 48 papers across six dimensions: application domain, visualization task, visualization representation, interaction modality, LLM integration, and system evaluation., Analysis of LLM roles across the visualization pipeline, from data querying and transformation to visualization generation, explanation, and navigation., Mapping of emerging design patterns and identification of gaps in accessibility and visualization reading.

title: LLM text decoding is key component for perceived LLM quality. We demonstrate two experiments showing...
abstract: The study focuses on LLM text decoding as a crucial factor in perceived quality. Two experiments were conducted to enhance decoding methods by adjusting token probabilities. In the first experiment, several LLMs were tested on the SummEval summary scoring dataset to measure reading comprehension. Scores from greedy decoding were compared with expected values over the next token distribution. By scaling logits with a large temperature, the entropy of scores was increased, leading to significant performance improvement on SummEval (improvement ranges from 6-8% to 13-28% for 7B Mistral and 20%-46% to 37%-56% for Mixtral, surpassing GPT 4 0314 results on two metrics). Part of the gain appears related to positional bias. In the second experiment, a probability-based tree sampling algorithm was used to explore all most probable generations for a given prompt.

abstract: The paper introduces Streaming Infinite Retentive LLM (SirLLM), a method to improve the long-term memory capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) during infinite-length dialogues without fine-tuning.
categories: SirLLM utilizes the Token Entropy metric and a memory decay mechanism to filter key phrases., The approach aims to maintain longer memory in LLMs by continuously updating and retaining important phrases.

title: To ensure that large language model (LLM) responses are helpful and non-toxic, a reward model...
abstract: The abstract discusses an approach to ensure large language models (LLMs) produce helpful and non-toxic responses by using a reward model trained on human preference data. It mentions two methods for selecting high-reward responses: best-of-$n$ (BoN) sampling and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). However, these processes are prone to reward overoptimization or 'hacking', where responses receive high rewards due to imperfections in the reward model rather than true preference. To address this issue, a Bayesian reward model is proposed that signals higher uncertainty further from the training data distribution.
categories: Training a Bayesian reward model using Laplace approximation on LoRA weights, Utilizing the trained Bayesian reward models in best-of-$n$ (BoN) sampling to mitigate reward overoptimization

title: This study investigates how existing annotation guidelines can be repurposed to instruct large language model...
abstract: The study proposes a method to repurpose existing annotation guidelines for use with LLMs. This is achieved through an LLM moderation process, demonstrated using the NCBI Disease Corpus as a case study. The experiments indicate that these repurposed guidelines can effectively guide LLM annotators, though they also reveal practical challenges. The results suggest the potential of this workflow for scalable and cost-effective refinement of annotation guidelines and automated annotation.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have the potential to address social and behavioral determinants of health...
abstract: The abstract discusses a study aimed at partnering with social workers at a safety net hospital to build an LLM application that summarizes patients' social needs. It presents a novel co-design framework for settings with limited access to domain experts, which decomposes the summary generation task into individually-optimizable attributes and refines and validates each attribute through a multi-tier cascading approach.

title: Generative large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated gaps in diverse cultural awareness across the globe....
abstract: The abstract discusses the lack of diverse cultural awareness in generative large language models (LLMs) globally. Research is conducted to examine the impact of retrieval augmented generation and search-grounding techniques on LLMs' ability to display familiarity with various national cultures. Findings show that search grounding improves LLM performance on multiple-choice benchmarks testing propositional knowledge (cultural norms, artifacts, institutions), but has risks of stereotypical judgments by language models.
categories: Comparison of standard LLMs, LLMs augmented with retrievals from a bespoke knowledge base (KB grounding), and LLMs augmented with retrievals from web search (search grounding) on cultural awareness benchmarks., Evaluation using multiple-choice benchmarks and human evaluation to test cultural familiarity.

title: This paper shifts focus to the often-overlooked input embeddings - the initial representations fed into...
abstract: The paper investigates input embeddings in transformer blocks of various Large Language Models (LLMs). It finds significant categorical community structure within these embeddings, aligned with human-defined concepts and categories. The groupings exhibit internal organization such as hierarchies and topological orderings, suggesting a pre-existing structural foundation before contextual processing. The study also explores cross-model alignments, revealing medium to high degrees of alignment among different LLM categories within their input embeddings. Moreover, the research suggests that manipulating these groupings can potentially play a role in reducing ethnicity bias in LLM tasks.

title: Humans often rely on subjective natural language to direct language models (LLMs); for example, users...
abstract: This work investigates the misalignment between Language Models (LLMs) operational semantics and human expectations when subjective phrases are used to guide them. The study introduces TED (thesaurus error detector), a method that constructs a thesaurus capturing the LLM's interpretation of similar phrases, compares it with a human-constructed reference, and uncovers instances of misalignment. The research reveals unexpected behaviors in popular LLMs such as Mistral 7B Instruct generating more harassing outputs when editing text to be witty, and Llama 3 8B Instruct producing dishonest articles when instructed to make the articles enthusiastic.
categories: Construction of a thesaurus that captures the operational semantics of phrases according to the LLM., Elicitation of failures by unearthing disagreements between the constructed thesaurus and a human-constructed reference., Regularly producing surprising instances of misalignment for analysis.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being explored for building Agents capable of active environmental...
abstract: The paper discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in building agents for active environmental interaction, such as tool use, to solve complex problems. Reinforcement Learning (RL) is highlighted as a key technology for training these agents, although its effective application to LLM Agents is still in development due to various challenges. The paper aims to advance this field by revisiting and clarifying Reinforcement Learning methodologies for LLM Agents and introducing Agent-R1, a modular, flexible, user-friendly training framework designed for RL-based LLM Agents.
categories: The paper extends the Markov Decision Process (MDP) framework to comprehensively define the key components of an LLM Agent., It introduces Agent-R1, a modular training framework for RL-based LLM Agents, which is designed for adaptation across diverse task scenarios and interactive environments.
