With the development of Large Language Models (LLMs), numerous efforts have revealed their vulnerabilities to...
categories: To investigate the misuse threats of LLMs, the authors decouple the use of jailbreak techniques and construct knowledge-intensive Q&A. The experiments aim to evaluate dangerous knowledge possession, harmful task planning utility, and harmfulness judgment robustness in LLMs.

title: This paper presents $\forall$uto$\exists$val, a new approach for scaling LLM assessment in translating formal syntax...
abstract: The paper introduces $orall$uto$	imes$eval, a new approach for scaling Language Model (LLM) assessment in translating formal syntax to natural language and vice versa. This facilitates their use in applications like generating/explaining logic and control flow for programs. The approach addresses the limitations of existing methods by utilizing context-free grammars (CFGs) to generate out-of-distribution datasets on demand, enabling closed-loop testing of LLM capabilities using formal verifiers to ensure the correctness of outputs without human intervention.
categories: The proposed approach involves generating out-of-distribution datasets using context-free grammars (CFGs) for closed-loop testing of Language Model (LLM) capabilities., Formal verifiers are used to guarantee the correctness of LLM outputs without any human intervention.

title: Large language models (LLMs) can be used to analyze cyber threat intelligence (CTI) data from...
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of a Large Language Model (LLM) system built on the OpenAI GPT-3.5-turbo model for extracting Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) information from cybercrime forums. The LLM was instructed to summarize conversations and predict 10 key CTI variables, and its accuracy was assessed by two coders. The system performed well with an average accuracy score of 96.23%, an average precision of 90% and an average recall of 88.2%.
categories: A random sample of more than 700 daily conversations from three cybercrime forums - XSS, Exploit_in, and RAMP - was extracted., The LLM system was instructed to summarize the conversations and predict 10 key CTI variables., Two coders reviewed each conversation and evaluated whether the information extracted by the LLM was accurate.

title: Unified graph representation learning aims to generate node embeddings, which can be applied to multiple...
abstract: The abstract presents a novel Path-LLM model for efficient learning of unified graph representation. This model leverages a powerful large language model (LLM) to incorporate path features, aiming to address the limitations of existing graph neural networks and language models such as numerous training needs, poor generalization, or shallow semantic features. The Path-LLM framework consists of four well-designed techniques: a new mechanism for long-to-short shortest path (L2SP) selection, path textualization, self-supervised LLM training process, and extraction of unified graph embeddings.
categories: Development of a new L2SP selection mechanism that covers key connections between different dense groups., Design of path textualization to obtain L2SP-based training texts with key phrase selection from node text attributes., Self-supervised LLM training process to align next node/edge generation in L2SP with next token generation in causal language modeling for graph representation learning., Extraction of unified graph embeddings.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become essential in a variety of applications due to their...
categories: The methods presented in the paper include the introduction of PIM-AI, a novel DDR5/LPDDR5 Processing-in-Memory (PIM) architecture designed for Large Language Model (LLM) inference. The architecture does not require modifications to the memory controller or DDR/LPDDR memory PHY. A simulator was developed to evaluate the performance of PIM-AI in various scenarios.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved impressive capabilities in various context-based text generation tasks, such...
Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized intelligent application development. While standalone LLMs cannot perform any...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use and challenges of Large Language Models (LLMs) in developing intelligent applications, particularly through LLM agents which integrate tools to overcome standalone LLMs' limitations. The study focuses on bugs encountered during agent development within LLM-based software. A comprehensive analysis of bug types, root causes, and effects is presented, based on 1,187 bug-related posts and code snippets from various forums such as Stack Overflow, GitHub, and Hugging Face. The study covers LLM agents built using seven popular frameworks and custom implementations. For a deeper analysis, the component where the bug occurred, along with the programming language and framework, was also studied.
categories: The research team collected and analyzed data from various forums to understand bug patterns in LLM agent-based software., They investigated the feasibility of automating bug identification by building a ReAct agent named BugReAct, equipped with external tools to determine its ability to detect and annotate bugs in their dataset., The performance of BugReAct with Gemini 2.5 Flash was remarkable in annotating bug characteristics, averaging a cost of 0.01 USD per post/code snippet.

title: Recent advances in Knowledge Distillation (KD) aim to mitigate the high computational demands of Large...
abstract: This study investigates privacy risks associated with Knowledge Distillation (KD) in Large Language Models (LLMs). It reveals that all existing LLM KD approaches carry membership and memorization privacy risks from the teacher to its students, with varying extents across different KD techniques. Key components like KD objective functions, student training data, and NLP tasks affect these privacy risks. A significant disagreement between memorization and membership privacy risks of LLM KD techniques is also noted. The study further characterizes per-block privacy risk, showing a large variation across blocks.

title: Despite the impressive performance of large language models (LLMs) across various benchmarks, their ability to...
abstract: The research introduces a formal definition of task ambiguity and proposes an approach using Bayesian Experimental Design for task disambiguation in large language models. By posing clarifying questions, the LLM agents can acquire additional task specifications, reducing the risk of generating unsatisfactory outputs. However, generating effective clarifying questions requires the LLMs to engage in meta-cognitive reasoning, an ability they may lack currently. The empirical results demonstrate that this approach leads to more effective task disambiguation compared to traditional methods.

title: Autonomous agents powered by large language models (LLMs) enable novel use cases in domains where...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of autonomous agents powered by large language models (LLMs) in domains requiring responsible action. It highlights safety concerns about agent reliability due to LLM unpredictability. To address this, a strategy-modification method is introduced for a game-theoretic environment based on the Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma. The method uses interpretable features extracted from a sparse autoencoder latent space to steer the residual stream. Steering with good-faith negotiation lowers defection probability by 28%. Feasible steering ranges are identified for several open-source LLM agents. Lastly, it proposes that game-theoretic evaluation of LLM agents combined with representation-steering alignment could generalize to real-world applications on end-user devices and embodied platforms.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable text understanding capabilities, which have been extended as...
abstract: This paper introduces VTimeLLM, a novel Video Language Model designed for fine-grained video moment understanding and reasoning regarding time boundaries. The model uses a three-stage training strategy: image-text pair alignment, temporal boundary awareness enhancement using multiple-event videos, and high-quality video-instruction tuning for improved temporal understanding. Extensive experiments show that VTimeLLM significantly outperforms existing Video LLMs in fine-grained time-related comprehension tasks like Temporal Video Grounding and Dense Video Captioning. Additionally, the model's fine-grained temporal understanding aids it in surpassing existing Video LLMs in video dialogue benchmarks, demonstrating its superior cross-modal understanding and reasoning abilities.

title: The Black-Litterman model addresses the sensitivity issues of tra- ditional mean-variance optimization by incorporating investor...
abstract: This study proposes a systematic framework that translates return forecasts and predictive uncertainty from LLMs into inputs for the Black-Litterman model. The analysis reveals that each LLM exhibits a distinct and consistent investment style which is the primary driver of performance. The selection of an LLM is not a search for a single best forecaster, but a strategic choice of an investment style whose success is contingent on its alignment with the prevailing market regime.

title: The ease of using a Large Language Model (LLM) to answer a wide variety of...
abstract: This paper proposes a neural network-based tool that instructors can use to determine the original effort (and LLM's contribution) put by students in writing source codes. The tool is motivated by minimum description length measures like Kolmogorov complexity. Initial experiments with moderate sized code (up to 500 lines) have shown promising results.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are the result of a massive experiment in bottom-up, data-driven reverse...
categories: Giving snippets of text as input to an LLM and querying its understanding, Comparing the LLM's responses with expected outcomes to evaluate its understanding capabilities

title: Whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can outperform crowdsourcing on the data annotation task is attracting...
abstract: This research investigates existing crowdsourcing datasets for comparative studies, evaluating the quality of individual crowd labels and LLM labels. The study finds that combining LLM labels from reliable models with crowdsourcing datasets enhances the quality of aggregated labels, even surpassing the quality of LLM labels alone.

title: Using large language models (LLMs) to evaluate text quality has recently gained popularity. Some prior...
categories: Analysis of LLM evaluation as presented in Chiang and Lee (2023) and G-Eval by Liu et al. (2023), Exploration of how details in the evaluation process affect the correlation between LLM ratings and human ratings, Demonstration that forcing the LLM to output only a numeric rating, as in G-Eval, is suboptimal, Discovery that asking the LLM to explain its own ratings consistently improves the correlation between ChatGPT and human ratings

title: Rapid evolution of Large Language Models (LLMs) has achieved major advances in reasoning, planning, and...
abstract: This paper discusses the safety and reliability issues with the execution of Large Language Model (LLM) generated code in production AI systems. The direct use of LLM-generated code can lead to unstable, erroneous, or vulnerable code containing data poisoning, malicious attacks, and hallucinations. These issues prevent the adoption of LLM-generated code in production AI systems where human code reviews and traditional secure testing tools are impractical or untrustworthy. To address this problem, a Secure Transpiler and Executor of LLM-Generated Program (STELP) is proposed to execute LLM-generated code in a controlled and safe manner. STELP secures autonomous production AI systems involving code generation, filling the critical void left by traditional secure testing methodologies and human oversight. The paper includes applications such as headless code generation-execution and LLMs that produce executable code snippets as an action plan to be executed in real time. A human-validated dataset of insecure code snippets is contributed, and the approach is benchmarked on publicly available datasets for correctness, safety, and latency. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms an existing method by a significant margin, particularly in its ability to safely execute risky code snippets. A warning is issued as the paper contains malicious code snippets that should be run with caution.

title: A fundamental problem of applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to important applications is that LLMs...
categories: NSVIF (Neuro-Symbolic Framework for verifying whether an LLM's output follows the instructions used to prompt the LLM), The framework formulates instruction-following verification as a constraint-satisfaction problem, It models user instructions as constraints, NSVIF models both logical and semantic constraints, Constraint solving is done by a unified solver that orchestrates logical reasoning and semantic analysis

title: We present $\textbf{Platypus}$, a family of fine-tuned and merged Large Language Models (LLMs) that achieves...
categories: The authors present a family of fine-tuned and merged Large Language Models (LLMs) named Platypus. They have curated a dataset called Open-Platypus, which is a subset of other open datasets that they release to the public., They describe their process of fine-tuning and merging LoRA modules in order to conserve the strong prior of pretrained LLMs, while bringing specific domain knowledge to the surface., The authors also mention their efforts in checking for test data leaks and contamination in the training data, which can inform future research.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into diverse applications. The rapid evolution of LLMs...
categories: RETAIN (REgression Testing guided LLM migrAtIoN), Interactive interface for regression testing during LLM migrations, Error discovery module that generates textual descriptions of errors or differences between model outputs, Manual evaluation for comparison

title: The training paradigm integrating large language models (LLM) is gradually reshaping sequential recommender systems (SRS)...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new practice-friendly paradigm for integrating large language models (LLM) into sequential recommender systems (SRS). The proposed method, P2Rec, aims to enhance the efficiency and compatibility of LLM-enhanced methods by utilizing preference parsing and user-level supervised fine-tuning (SFT) tasks. The goal is for LLM to learn to reconstruct a preference distribution from each user's interaction sequence, effectively parsing item categories and relationships.
categories: Information reconstruction stage: Introduces a new user-level SFT task for collaborative information injection with the assistance of a pre-trained SRS model., In this stage, LLM learns to reconstruct a corresponding prior preference distribution from each user's interaction sequence., Information augmentation stage: Feeds each item into LLM to obtain enhanced embeddings that combine collaborative information and LLM inference capabilities., These embeddings can be used to help train various future SRS models.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have become increasingly prevalent across various real-world applications. They enhance...
abstract: The abstract discusses the investigation of Large Language Model (LLM) agents' vulnerability to the Memory EXTRaction Attack (MEXTRA), which aims to extract private information from memory modules. The attack is effective against two representative LLM agents and highlights factors influencing memory leakage from both agent designers' and attackers' perspectives. The findings emphasize the need for effective memory safeguards in LLM agent design and deployment.

title: Existing anomaly detection (AD) methods for tabular data usually rely on some assumptions about anomaly...
categories: LLM-DAS is a novel framework that repositions Large Language Models (LLMs) from data processors to algorithmists. Instead of being exposed to raw data, it analyzes a high-level description of a given detector to understand its intrinsic weaknesses and then generates detector-specific, data-agnostic Python code to synthesize 'hard-to-detect' anomalies that exploit these vulnerabilities., The generated synthesis program is reusable across diverse datasets and is then instantiated to augment training data. This process systematically enhances the detector's robustness by transforming the problem into a more discriminative two-class classification task., By bridging LLM reasoning with classic AD algorithms via programmatic synthesis, LLM-DAS offers a scalable, effective, and privacy-preserving approach to patching the logical blind spots of existing detectors.

title: Text watermarking for Large Language Models (LLMs) has made significant progress in detecting LLM outputs...
abstract: The abstract discusses the advancements in watermarking for Large Language Models (LLMs) to detect and prevent misuse, focusing on high detectability, minimal impact on text quality, and robustness to text editing. However, it highlights a gap in the research of imperceptibility of watermarking techniques in LLM services, which is crucial for maintaining user trust and service security. The first investigation into the imperceptibility of watermarked LLMs is presented, with the introduction of an identification algorithm called Water-Probe that detects watermarks through well-designed prompts.
categories: Design of Water-Probe algorithm to identify watermarks in LLMs by using well-designed prompts., Exploitation of consistent biases under the same watermark key, resulting in similar differences across prompts under different watermark keys.

title: Decoding language information from brain signals represents a vital research area within brain-computer interfaces, particularly...
abstract: The paper introduces Brain Prompt GPT (BP-GPT), a novel method that uses brain representation extracted from fMRI as a prompt to utilize GPT-2 for decoding fMRI signals into stimulus text. BP-GPT aims to improve the decoding of pre-trained Language Model (LLM) in auditory neural decoding, an area significant within Brain-Computer Interfaces. The authors achieve a notable improvement up to 4.61 on METEOR and 2.43 on BERTScore across all subjects compared to the state-of-the-art method. The experimental results suggest that using brain representation as a prompt for LLM in auditory neural decoding is feasible and effective.
categories: Extract brain representation from fMRI, Use the extracted brain representation as a prompt for GPT-2, Decode fMRI signals into stimulus text using GPT-2, Introduce a text prompt and align it with the fMRI prompt, Extract a more robust brain prompt using the text prompt, Evaluate BP-GPT on open-source auditory semantic decoding dataset

title: With the rapid development of LLMs, it is natural to ask how to harness their...
abstract: The paper explores the feasibility of directing each input query to a single most suitable Language Model (LLM) for challenging reasoning tasks. It suggests that LLM routing shows promise but may not be feasible in all scenarios, thus calling for more robust approaches.

title: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized code generation, leading to widespread adoption...
categories: Establishing a benchmark to evaluate the ability of LLMs to provide accurate license information for their generated code., Conducting an empirical study to identify a reasonable standard for 'striking similarity' that excludes the possibility of independent creation., Proposing LiCoEval, a tool to evaluate the license compliance capabilities of LLMs., Using LiCoEval to evaluate 14 popular LLMs and assess their ability to provide accurate license information.

title: Unstructured text data annotation is foundational to management research and Large Language Models (LLMs) promise...
categories: The SILICON methodology is developed to systematically reduce measurement error from LLM annotation in four sources:, - Guideline-induced error: iteratively refined guidelines are used to increase LLM-human agreement., - Baseline-induced error: expert-generated baselines exhibit higher inter-annotator agreement and are less prone to producing misleading LLM-human agreement estimates compared to crowdsourced baselines., - Prompt-induced error: placing content in the system prompt reduces this error., - Model-induced error: model performance varies substantially across tasks., A cost-effective multi-LLM labeling method is introduced, where only low-confidence items receive additional labels from alternative models., A regression-based methodology is used to establish robust reproducibility protocols for closed source model retirement cycles.

title: As large language models (LLMs) begin to saturate existing benchmarks, automated benchmark creation using LLMs...
abstract: The abstract discusses the issue of self-bias in large language models (LLMs) when used for automated benchmark creation and evaluation for translation tasks.
categories: The study demonstrates that the bias originates from two sources: the generated test data by the LLM and the evaluation method also performed by the same LLM., It is observed that the self-bias in LLM as a benchmark is heavily influenced by the model's generation capabilities in the source language.

title: The ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to critique and refine their reasoning is crucial...
categories: The study introduces CriticBench, a benchmark designed to assess Large Language Models' (LLMs) abilities to critique and rectify their reasoning. The research involves 15 datasets across five reasoning domains: mathematical, commonsense, symbolic, coding, and algorithmic. Three LLM families are included in the evaluation.

title: LLMs are typically fine-tuned offline on domain-specific data, assuming a static domain. In practice, domain...
abstract: The abstract introduces Online Domain-aware Decoding framework (ODD), a method that addresses the challenge of evolving domains in Language Model fine-tuning. ODD performs probability-level fusion between a base LLM and a prefix-tree prior, guided by adaptive confidence modulation using disagreement and continuity signals. Empirical evaluation shows ODD outperforms LLM-Greedy and LLM-Temp Scaled across various drift scenarios in terms of syntactic and semantic NLG metrics. Notably, it achieves an absolute ROUGE-L gain of 0.065 and a 13.6% relative improvement in Cosine Similarity over the best baseline. The study concludes that ODD demonstrates robustness to evolving lexical and contextual patterns, making it suitable for dynamic LLM applications.

title: Large language models LLMs like ChatGPT have reached the 100 Mio user barrier in record...
abstract: The study investigates interactions between humans and large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT, focusing on non-collaborative, competitive situations that could potentially threaten people. The research conducted a user study with over 40 individuals across all age groups in price negotiations with an LLM. The aim was to understand how people interact with LLMs and to compare negotiation outcomes and strategies. The findings highlight shortcomings of LLMs in terms of reasoning capabilities, making them vulnerable to prompt hacking meant to manipulate the models into agreements against instructions or beyond rationality. Additionally, it was observed that negotiated prices between humans and LLMs vary widely, suggesting a literacy gap in effectively interacting with such models.

title: With the rapid evolution of Large Language Models (LLMs), LLM-based agents and Multi-agent Systems (MAS)...
abstract: The abstract discusses the evolution of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multi-agent Systems (MAS), focusing on the introduction of additional modules such as memory, tools, environment, and agents to LLM ecosystems. It introduces the TrustAgent framework, a comprehensive study on the trustworthiness of these agents within this new paradigm. The TrustAgent framework aims to address the complex issues of trustworthiness that have arisen with the advancement of LLMs and MAS.
categories: Proposes the TrustAgent framework, Deconstructs and introduces various components of the Agent and MAS, Categorizes trustworthiness into intrinsic (brain, memory, and tool) and extrinsic (user, agent, and environment) aspects, Delineates the multifaceted meanings of trustworthiness, Elaborates on the implementation techniques of existing research related to these internal and external modules

title: Accurate prediction of human behavior is crucial for AI systems to effectively support real-world applications,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the importance of accurate human behavior prediction for AI systems in real-world applications, particularly autonomous robots. It highlights the challenges posed by occlusions and incomplete scene observations in traditional video-based methods. The text then introduces Large Language Models (LLMs) as a promising alternative due to their ability to encode plausible sequences of human actions based on training on a large text corpus. However, LLMs are mentioned to lack inherent spatial awareness and real-time environmental perception, which hampers their understanding of physical constraints and spatial geometry in a real-world spatial scenario.
categories: Proposal of a multimodal prediction framework that enhances LLM-based action prediction by integrating physical constraints derived from human trajectories., Experiments to demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach, showing significant improvement in overall prediction performance when combining LLM predictions with trajectory data.

title: How should Large Language Model (LLM) practitioners select the right model for a task without...
abstract: The paper proposes BELLA (Budget-Efficient LLM Selection via Automated skill-profiling), a framework that recommends optimal Large Language Model (LLM) selection for tasks by using interpretable skill-based model selection. It aims to help practitioners choose the right model without wasting money. The framework decomposes LLM outputs, clusters skills into structured capability matrices, and uses multi-objective optimization to select models that maximize performance while respecting budget constraints.
categories: Critic-based profiling for granular skill extraction from LLM outputs, Clustering skills into structured capability matrices, Multi-objective optimization for selecting the right models, Natural-language rationale provided for recommendations

title: Online information is increasingly linked to real-world instability, especially as automated accounts and LLM-based agents...
Rapid advancements in Autonomous Driving (AD) tasks turned a significant shift toward end-to-end fashion, particularly...
abstract: The abstract introduces Atlas, a new approach for end-to-end autonomous driving that addresses the limitations of current VLM-based methods. Atlas utilizes DETR-style 3D perceptrons as 3D tokenizers and employs a one-layer linear projector to connect with an LLM. This strategy allows for simultaneous processing of high-resolution multi-view images and spatiotemporal modeling, which improves performance in both 3D detection and ego planning tasks on the nuScenes dataset. The abstract concludes that 3D-tokenized LLMs are key to reliable autonomous driving and states that the code and datasets will be released.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for decision-making tasks under uncertainty; however, their risk...
Miscalibration in Large Language Models (LLMs) undermines their reliability, highlighting the need for accurate confidence...
abstract: The abstract discusses CCPS (Calibrating LLM Confidence by Probing Perturbed Representation Stability), a novel method aimed at improving the reliability and accuracy of Large Language Models (LLMs) by reducing miscalibration. The approach analyzes internal representational stability in LLMs using targeted adversarial perturbations applied to final hidden states.
categories: CCPS applies adversarial perturbations to final hidden states., It extracts features reflecting the model's response to these perturbations., A lightweight classifier is used to predict answer correctness based on the extracted features.

title: The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) provides an opportunity to change the way queries...
categories: LLM-PQA, Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-augmented mechanism, Data lakes integration, Model zoos integration, Dynamic model training on demand

title: This paper introduces a novel approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) integrated into an agent...
abstract: This paper presents a new approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) integrated into an agent framework for personal mobility generation in urban settings. The novelty lies in LLMs' ability to process semantic data and adapt to various tasks, overcoming limitations of previous models. Three research questions are addressed: aligning LLMs with urban mobility data, developing reliable activity generation strategies, and exploring LLM applications in urban mobility. The key contribution is a new LLM agent framework considering individual activity patterns and motivations, featuring self-consistency for aligning with real-world activity data and a retrieval-augmented strategy for interpretable activity generation. The study evaluates the LLM agent framework compared to state-of-the-art personal mobility approaches, showing its effectiveness and potential urban mobility applications.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential for generative recommendation by leveraging rich semantic...
categories: Token-level Collaborative Alignment for Recommendation (TCA4Rec), Collaborative Tokenizer, Soft Label Alignment

title: Many recent studies showed that LLMs are vulnerable to jailbreak attacks, where an attacker can...
abstract: The abstract discusses TASO (Template and Suffix Optimization), a novel jailbreak method designed to improve the effectiveness of attacking Language Models (LLMs). The method optimizes both a template and a suffix in an alternating manner, leveraging the complementary nature of these two approaches. Suffix optimization controls the initial output tokens, while template optimization guides the overall output. The results demonstrate TASO's ability to jailbreak existing LLMs on benchmark datasets. The work is significant as it highlights a potential vulnerability in LLMs and suggests a method to exploit it.
categories: TASO (Template and Suffix Optimization): A novel jailbreak method that optimizes both a template and a suffix in an alternating manner., Suffix optimization: Controls the first few output tokens., Template optimization: Provides guidance for the entire output., Evaluation: Conducted on benchmark datasets (HarmBench, AdvBench) using 24 leading LLMs (Llama family, OpenAI, DeepSeek)

title: We introduce an intuitive method to test the robustness (stability and explainability) of any black-box...
Decision-making agents based on pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being deployed across various...
abstract: This work proposes a method to align Large Language Model (LLM) agents with human values using intrinsic rewards for Reinforcement Learning. The approach is evaluated using Deontological Ethics and Utilitarianism in the Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma (IPD) environment. Moral fine-tuning enables an agent to unlearn a selfish strategy, and some strategies learned on IPD generalize to other matrix game environments. The study suggests that fine-tuning with intrinsic rewards could be a promising general solution for aligning LLM agents, offering a more transparent and cost-effective alternative to existing alignment techniques.

title: Quantifying uncertainty in large language models (LLMs) is important for safety-critical applications because it helps...
abstract: Quantifying uncertainty in large language models (LLMs) is important for safety-critical applications as it helps identify incorrect answers, known as hallucinations. The main trend of uncertainty quantification methods focuses on estimating the entropy of the distribution of potential output sequences from LLMs. This estimation uses a set of output sequences and their probabilities obtained by repeatedly querying the LLM.
categories: Estimating the probability of potential output sequences by querying the LLM several times., Evaluating the importance of unobserved sequence probabilities in enhancing uncertainty quantification methods for LLMs.

title: Watermarking approaches are proposed to identify if text being circulated is human or large language...
abstract: The abstract discusses a new attack method, Self Color Testing-based Substitution (SCTS), designed to evade watermark detection in texts generated by large language models (LLMs). SCTS strategically prompts the watermarked LLM and compares output token frequencies to obtain color information. It then substitutes green tokens with non-green ones, successfully evading detection using fewer edits than related work. The method is shown to be effective for arbitrarily long watermarked texts.
categories: SCTS obtains color information by strategically prompting the watermarked LLM and comparing output tokens frequencies., It uses this information to determine token colors, and substitutes green tokens with non-green ones.

title: Recently, Large Language Model (LLM)-empowered recommender systems (RecSys) have brought significant advances in personalized user...
categories: CheatAgent is a novel attack framework proposed in this paper. It harnesses the human-like capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to attack LLM-Empowered Recommender Systems., The method first identifies the insertion position for maximum impact with minimal input modification., An LLM agent is then designed to generate adversarial perturbations to insert at target positions., To further improve the quality of generated perturbations, prompt tuning technique is utilized to iteratively receive feedback from the victim Recommender System and enhance attacking strategies.

title: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly reach real-world applications, necessitating a better understanding of their behaviour....
abstract: This study investigates the validity problem of using psychometric questionnaires to measure human-like traits in Large Language Models (LLMs). The findings suggest that these questionnaires, designed for humans, do not accurately measure similar constructs in LLMs. It is unclear if these constructs even exist in LLMs at all. The study emphasizes the need for psychometric analyses of LLM responses to avoid interpreting cognitive phantoms.

title: Large Language Models(LLMs) have had a profound impact on AI applications, particularly in the domains...
Large Language Models (LLMs) have showcased remarkable capabilities in various Natural Language Processing tasks. For...
abstract: The paper discusses issues with current evaluation benchmarks used for automatic open-domain dialogue evaluation. It highlights that these benchmarks rely on outdated datasets and focus on aspects like Fluency and Relevance, which do not adequately capture the capabilities and limitations of state-of-the-art chatbot models. Furthermore, an annotation experiment on a recent LLM-generated dataset (SODA) suggests that evaluation models such as GPT-4 struggle to detect actual deficiencies in dialogues generated by current LLM chatbots.

title: With LLM usage becoming widespread across countries, languages, and humanity more broadly, the need to...
abstract: This study evaluates the responses of frontier and leading open-source models in five dimensions across low and high-resource languages to measure LLM (Large Language Model) accuracy and consistency across multilingual contexts.
categories: The study uses a five-point grading rubric and a judge LLM to evaluate the responses., GPT-5, other unspecified models were tested for their performance in each category., Each model was evaluated across five categories: Consent & Autonomy, Harm Prevention & Safety, Factual Accuracy, Language Fluency, and Interpretability.

title: Inspired by the rapid development of Large Language Models (LLMs), LLM agents have evolved to...
categories:
LLM agents are applied across various domains to interact with humans and execute tasks.
1. Data handling: LLM agents process vast amounts of data.
2. Task execution: LLM agents perform complex tasks.
3. Defensive strategies: Existing defensive strategies have been reviewed in the survey.

title: In the past two years, large language models (LLMs) have achieved rapid development and demonstrated...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel News Recommendation (NR) algorithm called LECOP, which utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) for embedding and co-occurrence pattern analysis. The goal is to improve the news model by leveraging LLM's semantic understanding and reasoning capabilities. The paper details fine-tuning LLM through contrastive learning using large-scale datasets for encoding news and identifying user preferences, as well as exploring multiple co-occurrence patterns (news ID co-occurrence, Item-Item keywords co-occurrence, Intra-Item keywords co-occurrence) to mine collaborative information. The keywords for these patterns are all generated by the LLM.
categories: Fine-tuning of LLM through contrastive learning using large-scale datasets for news encoding and preference identification., Exploration of multiple co-occurrence patterns: news ID co-occurrence, Item-Item keywords co-occurrence, Intra-Item keywords co-occurrence.

title: Imagine AI assistants that enhance conversations without interrupting them: quietly providing relevant information during a...
abstract: This paper introduces the concept of 'overhearing agents', AI assistants that monitor ambient activity and intervene only when they can provide contextual assistance, without demanding user attention during conversations. The study analyzes overhearing LLM agents as a distinct paradigm in human-AI interaction and presents a taxonomy of overhearing agent interactions and tasks.
categories: The research is based on a survey of works on prior LLM-powered agents and exploratory HCI studies., From this taxonomy, a list of best practices for researchers and developers building overhearing agent systems is created.

title: Large language models (LLMs) match and sometimes exceeding human performance in many domains. This study...
abstract: This study investigates the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance human judgement in forecasting tasks. Two LLM assistants were evaluated, one designed for 'superforecasting' and another for providing noisy predictions. Participants (N=991) answered six forecasting questions while having the option to consult their assigned LLM assistant. The study found that interacting with either LLM assistant significantly improved prediction accuracy by 24-28% compared to a control group using a less advanced model. Exploratory analyses revealed a significant outlier effect in one question, without which the superforecasting assistant increased accuracy by 41%, and the noisy assistant by 29%. The study also examined whether LLM forecasting augmentation benefits less skilled forecasters, degrades prediction diversity, or varies with question difficulty, but results were inconclusive. Overall, the findings suggest that access to a frontier LLM assistant, even a noisy one, can be beneficial as a decision aid in cognitively demanding tasks.

title: Speech Large Language Models (Speech LLMs) have emerged as a crucial paradigm in recent years,...
abstract: The paper discusses the use of Speech Large Language Models (Speech LLMs) in speech tasks like Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and spoken dialogue modeling. The effectiveness of these models in multilingual conversations is limited due to insufficient data that captures natural conversational phenomena. To overcome this, the MLC-SLM Challenge presents a multilingual conversational dataset and evaluates models on two tasks: ASR with oracle segmentation (Task I) and joint diarization and recognition without oracle information (Task II). The authors focus on Task II and introduce a unified speech LLM that performs diarization and ASR in an end-toend manner. By modifying the training data format and inference procedure, the model overcomes ambiguity in pre-segmented audio and achieves a 54.87% relative improvement in tcpWER/tcpCER over the baseline, ranking 8th overall with a smaller LLM backbone. The paper also presents results from Task I using a fine-tuned speech LLM.
categories: Proposing a unified speech LLM that jointly performs diarization and ASR in an end-to-end manner., Reformulating the training data format., Modifying the inference procedure to address ambiguity inherent in pre-segmented audio.

title: Monitoring factual inconsistency is essential for ensuring trustworthiness in data-to-text generation (D2T). While large language...
abstract: The paper investigates the scaling of factual inconsistency in Data-to-Text Generation (D2T) with the size of large language models (LLMs). It questions the widely assumed power law scaling and proposes an exponential scaling instead, based on empirical results from analyzing three popular LLM families across five D2T datasets. The research employs a statistical validation framework consisting of three key stages: predictive performance estimation, goodness-of-fit assessment, and comparative analysis.
categories: Exploration of two scaling laws: power law and exponential scaling, Use of four state-of-the-art consistency metrics to measure factual inconsistency inversely, Analysis of three popular LLM families across five D2T datasets, Employment of a statistical validation framework with three key stages: predictive performance estimation, goodness-of-fit assessment, and comparative analysis

title: The emergence of Large Language Models (LLMs), has opened exciting possibilities for constructing computational simulations...
abstract: The study investigates the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in simulating human behavior, particularly political debates. It highlights that LLM-based agents may conform to their inherent social biases and deviate from human social dynamics, emphasizing a need for further research to help agents overcome these biases for more realistic simulations.

title: Recent studies employing Large Language Models (LLMs) to test the Argument from the Poverty of...
categories: Investigate the hypothesis that characteristics of the stimuli used in recent studies may confound model performance., Propose a methodology for re-evaluating Large Language Model (LLM) competence on syntactic prediction, focusing on GPT-2., Establish a baseline on previously used (both filtered and unfiltered) stimuli., Generate a new, refined dataset using a state-of-the-art (SOTA) generative LLM (Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview) guided by linguistically-informed templates designed to mitigate identified confounds.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have consistently showcased remarkable generalization capabilities when applied to various language...
categories: R2GenGPT, using an efficient visual alignment module, integrating and processing image information into previously static LLMs, delta tuning

title: Instruction tuning is a vital step of training large language models (LLMs), so how to...
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive survey on data selection for large language model (LLM) instruction tuning. It highlights the importance of enhancing the effect of instruction tuning in LLMs, emphasizing the quality of the dataset over quantity. Recently, studies have focused on selecting high-quality subsets from instruction datasets to reduce training costs and improve the instruction-following capabilities of LLMs. The paper discusses widely used instruction datasets, introduces a new taxonomy of data selection methods, details recent advances, and elaborates on evaluation strategies and results of data selection methods. Lastly, it outlines open challenges and presents new frontiers for this task.

title: We present a method to integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) and traditional tabular data classification...
abstract: The abstract discusses a method that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with traditional tabular data classification techniques to address challenges such as data serialization sensitivity and biases. Two strategies are introduced for ranking categorical variables and generating priors on correlations between continuous variables and targets, which improve performance in few-shot scenarios. The focus is on Logistic Regression, where a new method called MonotonicLR is presented, using a non-linear monotonic function to map ordinals to cardinals while preserving the LLM-determined orders. This approach outperforms baseline models, particularly in low-data scenarios, and remains interpretable.

title: Werewolf is an incomplete information game, which has several challenges when creating a computer agent...
categories: Using a rule-based algorithm to select an output either from a Large Language Model (LLM) or a template prepared beforehand based on the results of analyzing conversation history using an LLM., Refuting in specific situations by employing the rule-based algorithm, Identifying when to end the conversation using the rule-based algorithm, Behaving with persona through the use of a rule-based algorithm and prepared templates

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are reshaping the landscape of computer science research, driving significant shifts...
categories: Comprehensive analysis of the publication trend of Large Language Models (LLM)-related papers in 77 top-tier computer science conferences over the past six years (2019-2024)., Investigation of how LLM research is driving topic shifts within major conferences., Adoption of a topic modeling approach to identify various areas of LLM-related topic growth., Exploration of distinct contribution patterns of academic and industrial institutions.

title: With the wide adoption of large language models (LLMs) in information assistance, it is essential...
abstract: The study finds that LLM-generated articles score significantly lower in persuasive strategies, certainty expressions, and alignment with social values and moral foundations compared to human experts. However, human evaluation favors LLM content, demonstrating a strong preference for clarity, completeness, and persuasiveness of LLM articles in health communication. The study suggests that despite scoring lower on traditional measures of quality, the structured approach of LLMs might be more effective at engaging readers.

title: This paper explores the open research problem of understanding the social behaviors of LLM-based agents....
categories: Using Avalon as a testbed, system prompts were employed to guide LLM agents during gameplay. A novel framework was proposed, tailored for Avalon, featuring a multi-agent system facilitating efficient communication and interaction. The evaluation of the framework's performance was based on game success.

title: Large language models (LLMs) serve as powerful tools for design, providing capabilities for both task...
abstract: This paper introduces an open-source dataset tailored for OpenROAD, a widely adopted open-source EDA toolchain. The dataset aims to facilitate Large Language Model (LLM) focused research within the Electronic Design Automation (EDA) domain by providing over 1000 data points in two formats: question prompts with prose answers and code prompts with their corresponding OpenROAD scripts. The paper addresses the gap in the availability of publicly accessible and permissively licensed data for training and distributing LLMs in the chip design process.
categories: Development of an open-source dataset specifically designed for the OpenROAD EDA toolchain, Creation of a dataset containing over 1000 data points, structured in two formats: question prompts with prose answers and code prompts with their corresponding OpenROAD scripts

categories: Pre-training of VBART, the first Turkish sequence-to-sequence Large Language Models (LLMs), from scratch., Leveraging good ideas from BART and mBART models for creating compact LLMs in two sizes: Large and XLarge., Introducing a method to enlarge an existing pre-trained LLM., Questioning the relevancy of Chinchilla Scaling Law to sequence-to-sequence masked language models.

title: Large Language Models have achieved remarkable success in language understanding and reasoning, and their multimodal...
abstract: The abstract introduces FCN-LLM, a framework that allows large language models (LLMs) to understand brain functional connectivity networks (FCNs) by employing graph-level, multi-task instruction tuning. The approach utilizes a multi-scale FCN encoder and designs various instruction tasks for subject-specific attributes across demographics, phenotypes, and psychiatric conditions. Experiments on a large-scale FCN database demonstrate strong zero-shot generalization on unseen datasets, outperforming conventional models. This work offers a flexible and interpretable framework for integrating brain functional networks with LLMs in neuroscience.

title: While large language models (LLMs) often adopt finetuning to unlock their capabilities for downstream applications,...
abstract: The study finds that LLM finetuning follows a power-based multiplicative joint scaling law between finetuning data size and each other scaling factor. It reveals that LLM finetuning benefits more from LLM model scaling than pretraining data scaling, and PET parameter scaling is generally ineffective. The research concludes that the optimal finetuning method is highly task- and finetuning data-dependent. They hope these findings will aid in understanding, selecting, and developing LLM finetuning methods.

title: Since the recent prosperity of Large Language Models (LLMs), there have been interleaved discussions regarding...
abstract: This paper explores the level of factual knowledge in Large Language Models (LLMs). To assess this, a benchmark called Head-to-Tail consisting of 18K question-answer pairs was created, focusing on head, torso, and tail facts in terms of popularity. An automated evaluation method and metrics were designed to measure the factual knowledge an LLM confidently internalizes. The study revealed that existing LLMs still have room for improvement regarding their grasp of factual knowledge, particularly for facts related to torso-to-tail entities.

title: The development of Generative AI Large Language Models (LLMs) raised the alarm regarding identifying content...
We present Branch-Train-Stitch (BTS), an efficient and flexible training algorithm for combining independently trained large...
abstract: The study introduces Branch-Train-Stitch (BTS), a flexible training algorithm that combines independently trained large language model experts into a single generalist model. BTS achieves this by freezing the experts and adding lightweight stitch layers between them and the seed LLM, which are then trained on a small datamix of the expert domains. This allows the seed LLM to integrate representations from any number of experts during the forward pass, enabling it to generalize to new domains without altering the constituent LLMs. The modular approach allows for easy removal or addition of experts with minimal training.

title: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has shown impressive capability in providing reliable answer predictions and addressing hallucination...
abstract: The abstract discusses the capabilities of Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and its challenges when directly applying large language models (LLMs) to RAG systems. It introduces a novel two-stage fine-tuning architecture called Invar-RAG, which addresses feature locality issues in LLM-based retrieval and enhances retrieval performance using invariant and variant patterns and an invariance loss. Experimental results show that Invar-RAG significantly outperforms existing baselines across three open-domain question answering (ODQA) datasets.

title: This work proposes a novel approach to enhancing annotated bibliography generation through Large Language Model...
categories: Multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) are introduced in different roles such as controllable text generation, evaluation, and summarization., Output diversity among the ensemble is obtained using different LLM parameters., An LLM acting as a judge assesses relevance, accuracy, and coherence., Responses selected by several combining strategies are then merged., Summarization and redundancy removal techniques are applied for refining the output.

title: The tendency to find and exploit "shortcuts" to complete tasks poses significant risks for reliable...
abstract: The study introduces ImpossibleBench, a framework to measure large language models' (LLMs) tendency to exploit 'shortcuts' in tasks, which can undermine the validity of benchmark results and the reliability of real-world LLM coding assistant deployments. The framework creates 'impossible' variants of tasks by introducing conflicts between natural-language specifications and unit tests. It quantifies an agent's 'cheating rate', and demonstrates its utility for studying model behaviors, context engineering, and developing monitoring tools. ImpossibleBench can be found at https://github.com/safety-research/impossiblebench.

title: The interactive nature of Large Language Models (LLMs) theoretically allows models to refine and improve...
categories: The FlipFlop experiment is proposed as a method to analyze the multi-turn behavior of Large Language Models. In this experiment, an LLM completes a classification task in the first round of conversation. In the second round, it is challenged with a follow-up phrase like 'Are you sure?' offering an opportunity for the model to reflect on its initial answer and decide whether to confirm or flip its answer. The study also includes finetuning experiments on an open-source LLM to investigate potential solutions for mitigating performance deterioration.

title: A high volume of recent ML security literature focuses on attacks against aligned large language...
categories: The paper provides a taxonomy of attacks categorized by threat actors, objectives, entry points, attacker observability, attack strategies, and inherent vulnerabilities of agent pipelines., Detailed illustrative attacks are performed on popular open-source and commercial agents.

title: [Abridged abstract] Large Language Models (LLMs) can solve some undergraduate-level to graduate-level physics textbook problems...
abstract: This study evaluates state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) on PhD-level to research-level computational physics problems in celestial mechanics, stellar physics, 1D fluid dynamics, and non-linear dynamics. The study finds that the SOTA LLM (GPT4) zero-shot fails most of the problems, but about 40% of solutions could potentially pass. Approximately 70-90% of the code lines produced are necessary, sufficient, and correct. Physics and coding errors are common, with some unnecessary or insufficient lines. The study identifies significant variations across problem class and difficulty, and outlines several failure modes of GPT4 in the computational physics domain.
categories: The authors present original and challenging problems for various physics domains using well-documented packages such as REBOUND, MESA, Dedalus, and SciPy., They evaluate LLM performance on several soft metrics including counts of lines containing different types of errors (coding, physics, necessity, sufficiency) and a Pass-Fail metric focused on capturing the salient physical ingredients of the problem at hand.

title: Despite rapid progress in large language models (LLMs), their performance on a vast majority of...
abstract: This research investigates building language-specific large language models (LLMs) by adapting both monolingual and multilingual LLMs. The study focuses on how design choices like base model selection, vocabulary extension, and continued pretraining impact the adapted LLM's efficiency and end task performance. Results show that efficiency can be improved with simple methods, and the optimal adaptation method varies by language. The simplest embedding initialization works well across different experimental settings.

categories: Proposal of AcademicEval, a live benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) over long-context generation tasks., Adoption of papers on arXiv to introduce academic writing tasks with long-context inputs such as Title, Abstract, Introduction, and Related Work., Integration of high-quality and expert-curated few-shot demonstrations from a collected co-author graph to enable flexible context length., Implementation of an efficient live evaluation to ensure no label leakage.

These input tags are parameterized as continuous vectors appended to the LLM's embedding layer to condition the LLM., Design of two types of input tags: domain tags are used to delimit specialized representations (e.g., chemical formulas) and provide domain-relevant context; function tags are used to represent specific functions (e.g., predicting molecular properties) and compress function-solving instructions., Development of a three-stage protocol to learn these tags using auxiliary data and domain knowledge., Explicit disentanglement of task domains from task functions, enabling zero-shot generalization to unseen problems through diverse combinations of the input tags.

title: There is increasing evidence that question-answering (QA) systems with Large Language Models (LLMs), which employ...
abstract: The research presents an approach to improve the accuracy and reduce error rate in LLM powered question answering systems using an Ontology-based Query Check (OBQC) and LLM Repair. The approach increases the overall accuracy to 72%, reducing the overall error rate to 20%. This suggests that investing in knowledge graphs, particularly the ontology, can provide higher accuracy for such systems.

title: The growing availability of generative AI technologies such as large language models (LLMs) has significant...
This paper revisits the LLM cache bandit problem, with a special focus on addressing the...
abstract: The paper addresses the LLM cache bandit problem, focusing on cost-effective inference by tackling query heterogeneity issues. Unlike previous works assuming uniform query sizes, this research considers varying query sizes which introduce a complex combinatorial structure for cache selection. The authors approach optimal cache selection as a knapsack problem and employ an accumulation-based strategy to manage computational overhead and updates effectively. In theoretical analysis, they prove the regret of their algorithm has an improved $O(\sqrt{MNT})$ bound compared to the previous $O(MN\sqrt{T})$ result from Berkeley. They also provide a problem-dependent bound, a feature absent in previous works.
categories: Treating optimal cache selection as a knapsack problem, Accumulation-based strategy for balancing computational overhead and updates

title: With the increasing reliance on LLMs as research agents, distinguishing between LLM and human-generated ideas...
abstract: The study investigates the ability of state-of-the-art machine learning models to differentiate between human and LLM-generated ideas in scientific research, especially after paraphrasing stages. Findings show that these models struggle with source attribution, as detection performance declines by an average of 25.4% after five consecutive paraphrasing stages. The research problem as contextual information improves detection performance by up to 2.97%. The analysis also indicates that algorithms face significant challenges when ideas are paraphrased into simplified, non-expert styles, which contributes most to the erosion of distinguishable LLM signatures.

title: Recent breakthroughs in solving reasoning, math and coding problems with Large Language Models (LLMs) have...
abstract: The research presents xLSTM 7B, a 7-billion-parameter LLM that offers architectural benefits with targeted optimizations for fast and efficient inference. The model demonstrates comparable performance on downstream tasks to other similar-sized LLMs but achieves faster inference speeds and greater efficiency. The study positions xLSTM 7B as the fastest and most efficient 7B LLM, making it a suitable solution for tasks requiring large amounts of test-time computation. The work highlights the potential of xLSTM as a foundational architecture for methods heavily utilizing Large Language Model inference, and the model's weights, code, and training code are open-source.

title: To ensure the trustworthiness and interpretability of AI systems, it is essential to align machine...
categories: The main method presented in the paper is the Mixture of Rule Experts (MoRE), which combines a data-driven black-box model with knowledge extracted from a Large Language Model (LLM). During training, MoRE steers the discovery of local rule-based surrogates for classification tasks. The LLM is responsible for enhancing the domain knowledge alignment of these rules by correcting and contextualizing them. Notably, the method does not require access to the LLM during test time and ensures interpretability without being prone to LLM-based confabulations.

title: In this work we proposing adapting the Minecraft builder task into an LLM benchmark suitable...
categories: Adapting Minecraft builder task into an LLM benchmark for evaluating LLM ability in spatially orientated tasks, Providing a comprehensive synthetic benchmark for testing builder agents over a series of distinct tasks, Testing the ability of LLMs in the challenging area of spatial reasoning and vector based math

title: Compared to traditional models, agentic AI represents a highly valuable target for potential attackers as...
abstract: The paper introduces an alternative approach, LLMZ+, to enhance the security of agentic AI systems by implementing prompt whitelisting. This method ensures that only contextually appropriate and safe messages interact with the agentic LLM, thereby conforming to predefined use cases and operational boundaries. The proposed approach aims to streamline the security framework, enhance long-term resilience, and reduce resources required for sustaining information security of agentic LLMs.
categories: LLMZ+ relies on prompt whitelisting, allowing only contextually appropriate and safe messages to interact with the agentic LLM., The approach guarantees that all exchanges between external users and the agentic LLM conform to predefined use cases and operational boundaries.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have been positioned to revolutionize HCI, by reshaping not only the...
abstract: The study systematically reviews 153 CHI papers from 2020-24 that engage with LLMs in HCI. It identifies ten diverse domains where LLMs are applied, primarily through empirical and artifact contributions. Authors use LLMs in five distinct roles, including as research tools or simulated users. abstract: The paper introduces SlimPLM, a novel collaborative approach that addresses the issue of determining the knowledge possessed by large language models (LLMs) and the knowledge that requires search engine assistance. SlimPLM uses a slim proxy model to detect missing knowledge in LLMs, aiming to enhance the LLM's knowledge acquisition process. The method employs a proxy model with fewer parameters, using its answers as heuristic answers. Heuristic answers are then utilized to predict the required knowledge, known and unknown knowledge within the LLM. Retrieval is only conducted for the missing knowledge in questions that the LLM does not know. Experimental results show significant improvement in end-to-end performance of LLMs in question-answering tasks, achieving or surpassing current state-of-the-art models with lower computational costs.

title: Task planning, the problem of sequencing actions to reach a goal from an initial state,...
abstract: In the experiment, four approaches were evaluated on 102 International Planning Competition Blocksworld instances under a uniform 180-second budget. Fast Downward achieved the highest success rate at 85.3%. The direct and agentic LLM approaches followed with lower rates of 63.7% and 66.7%, respectively. Although the LLM approaches showed a modest three-percentage-point advantage for the agentic approach, they were significantly more expensive in terms of token cost per solution ($5.7 times higher for the agentic approach). Interestingly, both LLM approaches produced shorter plans than seq-sat-lama-2011, which uses iterative quality improvement, suggesting that this could be due to training data recall rather than generalizable planning. The results indicate that agentic gains depend on the nature of environmental feedback, with coding agents benefiting from externally grounded signals while PDDL step feedback is self-assessed.

abstract: The LLM-KG-Bench framework (Version 3.0) is presented, designed to evaluate the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in working with Knowledge Graphs (KGs), particularly within the field of Semantic Web and Knowledge Graph Engineering (KGE). The framework includes an extensible set of tasks for automated evaluation, covers various aspects of semantic technologies, and has been updated since its initial release. Enhancements include a flexible task API, revised tasks, extended support for open models through the vllm library, and more improvements. A comprehensive dataset generated using over 30 contemporary open and proprietary LLMs enables demonstration of models' capabilities in working with RDF and SPARQL, as well as comparison of their performance on Turtle and JSON-LD RDF serialization tasks.

AgentAuditor extracts structured semantic features such as scenario, risk, behavior, and generates chain-of-thought reasoning traces. A multi-stage, context-aware retrieval-augmented generation process is employed to dynamically retrieve relevant reasoning experiences for assessing new cases.

The authors argue that such utility is model-specific due to differences in LLM's knowledge, reasoning, and ability to leverage evidence. A benchmark of LLM-specific gold utilitarian passages is constructed for four LLMs and three QA datasets to study LLM-specific utility.
categories: The construction of the benchmark involves comparing the performance improvement of a target LLM when a passage is provided versus answering without evidence., The analysis includes measuring model-dependent and non-transferable utilitarian passages for each LLM.

abstract: The abstract introduces AgentAuditor, a universal, training-free, memory-augmented reasoning framework that enhances LLM evaluators to emulate human expert evaluators. It constructs an experiential memory by adapting an LLM to extract structured semantic features and generate associated chain-of-thought reasoning traces for past interactions. A multi-stage, context-aware retrieval-augmented generation process then guides the LLM evaluator's assessment of new cases using relevant reasoning experiences. ASSEBench, a benchmark designed to test the ability of LLM-based evaluators in spotting safety risks and security threats, was also developed with 2293 annotated interaction records. The study demonstrates that AgentAuditor improves evaluation performance of LLMs across all benchmarks and sets a new state-of-the-art in LLM-as-a-judge for agent safety and security, achieving human-level accuracy.

It presents an overview of recent advancements and research directions aimed at improving the efficiency of LLM-based systems. Key areas of focus include algorithm-level acceleration techniques for optimizing inference speed and resource utilization, LLM-hardware co-design strategies to improve system efficiency by tailoring hardware architectures to LLM requirements, LLM-to-accelerator compilation approaches for customizing hardware accelerators for efficient deployment, and the application of LLMs in assisting circuit design, particularly High-Level Synthesis (HLS) functional verification.

abstract: Interactive Data Analysis (IDA) involving human-LLM collaboration enables real-time data exploration for informed decision-making. A new benchmark, Tapilot-Crossing, is introduced to evaluate LLM agents on IDA. The benchmark comprises 1024 interactions across four scenarios: Normal, Action, Private, and Private Action. Tapilot-Crossing is constructed with minimal human effort in the Decision Company environment. Evaluation of popular and advanced LLM agents shows the challenges of IDA. A new strategy, Adaptive Interaction Reflection (AIR), is proposed to guide LLMs to learn from successful history, improving their performance in IDA by up to 44.5%.

title: Effective patient care in digital healthcare requires large language models (LLMs) that not only answer...
abstract: The study presents HealthQ, a novel framework for evaluating the questioning capabilities of LLM healthcare chains. The goal is to assess how effectively these chains elicit comprehensive and relevant patient information. HealthQ uses an LLM judge to evaluate generated questions based on metrics like specificity, relevance, and usefulness, while aligning these evaluations with traditional Natural Language Processing (NLP) metrics like ROUGE and Named Entity Recognition (NER)-based set comparisons. The research validates HealthQ's robustness across multiple LLM judge models and demonstrates its potential for improved patient information elicitation. The study concludes by presenting the first systematic framework for assessing questioning capabilities in healthcare conversations, establishing a model-agnostic evaluation methodology, and providing empirical evidence linking high-quality questions to improved patient information elicitation.

title: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have led to increasingly diverse requests, accompanied with...
abstract: This work studies the cost-efficiency of Large Language Models (LLM) serving over heterogeneous GPU resources on cloud platforms. Different GPU types exhibit distinct compute and memory characteristics, which align well with the divergent resource demands of diverse requests. Through benchmarking, it's discovered that cost-efficiency can be optimized by meticulously determining GPU composition, deployment configurations, and workload assignments. A scheduling algorithm via mixed-integer linear programming is designed to deduce the most cost-efficient serving plan under price budget and real-time GPU availability constraints. The approach outperforms homogeneous and heterogeneous baselines in various scenarios, including diverse workloads, varying GPU availabilities, and multi-model serving. The results suggest that LLM serving over heterogeneous cloud resources could be more accessible and efficient.

title: LLM ensembles are widely used for LLM judges. However, how to estimate their accuracy, especially...
abstract: The paper presents a maximum a posteriori (MAP) framework for economical and precise estimation of the performance of Language Model (LLM) ensemble judgment. The framework uses a mixture of Beta-Binomial distributions to model the judgment distribution, introduces a conformal prediction-driven approach for adaptive stopping during iterative sampling, and designs a prior transfer mechanism to improve estimation on a target dataset with scarce annotations. The paper also presents BetaConform, a framework that integrates these components to deliver a theoretically guaranteed distribution estimation of LLM ensemble judgment with minimum labeled samples. BetaConform is empirically validated, demonstrating the ability to gauge the performance of a Llama ensembled judge with an error margin as small as 3.37% using only 10 samples from the TruthfulQA dataset.

The paper reports significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines, demonstrating the efficacy and robustness of RAC across different LLMs.

abstract: The abstract discusses a research project that aims to investigate whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can implement digital versions of board games from natural language rules. The study involves tasking three state-of-the-art LLMs with coding a selection of 12 popular and obscure games using free-form and within Boardwalk, a General Game Playing API. The implementations are tested for playability and rule compliance. Claude 3.7 Sonnet performs best, yielding 55.6% of games without any errors, while the severity of errors is more significantly dependent on the LLM. The research outlines future steps for creating a framework to integrate this process, making the elaboration of board games more accessible.

However, it is also found that modern LLMs do not always recognize the memory structure without a prompt, indicating a potential area for future improvements in both LLM training and memory frameworks.

abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of planning under resource constraints in real-world decision making, as most large language model (LLM) planners assume uniform action costs. It analyzes the efficiency of tree-search LLM planners in generating budget-feasible plans and finds that existing methods often struggle to find cost-optimal plans. The study compares depth-first search, breadth-first search, Monte Carlo Tree Search, and bidirectional search within a unified framework. Bidirectional search is found to be the most efficient and successful method overall, while MCTS achieves the highest optimality on short-horizon tasks. The study concludes that improving LLM planning under resource constraints will likely require new search algorithms, rather than solely scaling inference-time compute.

abstract: EviRerank is an evidence-based long-document reranking framework for decoder-only LLMs. It scores document blocks with lightweight selectors, constructs a compact reranking context by dynamically budgeting evidence blocks and adding a global summary cue, and then reranks with a decoder-only LLM. EviRerank outperforms full-document LLM reranking and strong block-selection baselines while substantially reducing the required input length. On TREC DL'19, it achieves 0.743 nDCG@10 and 0.307 MAP, establishing a new best result and improving over RankLLaMA by +0.042 nDCG@10 (+6.0%) and +0.019 MAP (+6.6%).

title: Hardware-Aware Neural Architecture Search (HW-NAS) requires joint optimization of accuracy and latency under device constraints....
abstract: The proposed method, LLM-NAS, is compared to traditional supernet-based methods. The main issue with traditional methods is a high search cost requiring multiple GPU days per dataset and an exploration bias that tends to propose neural network designs within limited search spaces, failing to discover architectures across different latency ranges in the entire search space. LLM-NAS aims to address this issue by introducing three key components: a complexity-driven partitioning engine, an LLM-powered architecture prompt co-evolution operator, and a zero-cost predictor. These components help reduce search cost, mitigate exploration bias, avoid random guesswork, and improve efficiency. Experimental results show that on HW-NAS-Bench, LLM-NAS achieves overall higher Hypervolume (HV) and lower Iterated-Gradient Descent (IGD), indicating better performance and diversity. Additionally, it offers up to 54% lower latency at similar accuracy compared to traditional methods. Moreover, the search cost drops significantly from days to minutes with LLM-NAS.

Researchers find that aligned LLMs generate texts with higher estimated preferences than human-written texts, making them detectable using a reward model (an LLM trained to model human preference distribution). Two training schemes are proposed to improve the detection ability of the reward model: continual preference fine-tuning and reward modeling of Human/LLM mixed texts.

abstract: [The paper proposes a novel offline reinforcement learning (RL) framework that leverages imitation learning from large language model (LLM)-generated trajectories for recommender systems (RecSys). The goal is to improve RecSys by utilizing LLMs' exceptional generalization capabilities and contextual understanding. However, direct deployment of LLMs as primary recommendation policies presents challenges such as latency issues and inherent model limitations like hallucinations and biases. To tackle these problems, the paper uses inverse reinforcement learning to extract robust reward models from LLM demonstrations. This method eliminates the need for LLM fine-tuning, significantly reducing computational overhead while transferring semantic insights captured by the LLM. Experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate superior performance compared to state-of-the-art RL-based and in-context learning baselines.]

abstract: The paper introduces Agentic LLM for Robot Manipulation (ALRM), an agentic framework that integrates policy generation with execution through a ReAct-style reasoning loop. ALRM supports two modes: Code-asPolicy (CaP) for direct executable control code generation, and Tool-as-Policy (TaP) for iterative planning and tool-based action execution. A novel simulation benchmark of 56 tasks across multiple environments is introduced to enable systematic evaluation. Results indicate that ALRM offers a scalable, interpretable, and modular approach for bridging natural language reasoning with reliable robotic execution. Claude-4.1-Opus and Falcon-H1-7B are identified as the top closed-source and open-source models under CaP mode, respectively.
