abstract: The paper examines the capabilities of recent large language models (LLMs) in generating optimized CUDA code for predefined tasks, particularly focusing on code optimizations and parallel patterns. It is found that while LLMs demonstrate proficiency in coding, they require tutoring to achieve solutions comparable to those by parallel computing experts.

title: The growing dependence on Large Language Models (LLMs) for finishing user instructions necessitates a comprehensive...
abstract: The study proposes the PowerPoint Task Completion Robustness benchmark (PPTC-R) to measure the robustness of Large Language Models (LLMs) in complex task completion in real-world situations, particularly focusing on user PPT task instructions and software versions. The benchmark includes adversarial user instructions at sentence, semantic, and multi-language levels, as well as varying the number of provided APIs to simulate different software versions. Three closed-source and four open-source LLMs were tested using this benchmark. GPT-4 was found to exhibit the highest performance and strong robustness, particularly in version update and multilingual settings. However, all LLMs showed decreased robustness when faced with multiple challenges simultaneously, leading to significant performance drops. The study also analyzes the robustness behavior and error reasons of LLMs, providing insights for developing more robust LLMs and agents. The code and data can be found at <https://github.com/ZekaiGalaxy/PPTCR>.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have recently been proposed as general-purpose agents for experimental design, with...
The use of language models for automatically evaluating long-form text (LLM-as-a-judge) is becoming increasingly common,...
abstract: The study introduces M-Prometheus, a suite of multilingual language model evaluators (LLM judges) ranging from 3B to 14B parameters. These models outperform state-of-the-art open LLM judges on multilingual reward benchmarks across more than 20 languages and improve generated outputs for three tested languages. M-Prometheus models provide both direct assessment and pairwise comparison feedback on multilingual outputs, and they are useful in the development of better multilingual models.
categories: Development of M-Prometheus suite of LLM judges, Training on synthetic multilingual feedback data instead of translated data, Backbone model selection as a key factor for obtaining an effective multilingual judge

title: In the rapidly evolving AI era with large language models (LLMs) at the core, making...
abstract: The tutorial focuses on improving trustworthiness and efficiency of large language models (LLMs) in output generation, particularly addressing hallucinations and increased inference demands. It aims to make these efforts transparent to the database community, emphasizing their importance for using LLMs in database tasks and adapting database techniques to LLMs. The synergy between LLMs and databases is explored, presenting new opportunities and challenges at their intersection.
categories: Exploring efforts to reduce plausible but faulty LLM outputs (hallucinations), Meeting the increased inference demands, Sharing essential concepts and strategies around LLMs, Reducing unfamiliarity of LLMs

title: Tool calling has become increasingly popular for Large Language Models (LLMs). However, for large tool...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of a framework named ToolDreamer for Large Language Models (LLMs). The challenge lies in the context window limit of LLMs when dealing with extensive tool sets. To circumvent this, an external retriever is utilized to provide the most relevant tools for a query. However, the existing retrieval models suffer from suboptimal retrieval due to misalignment between user queries and tool descriptions (TD). The proposed solution is to condition retriever models to fetch tools based on hypothetical TD generated using an LLM, improving the alignment between queries and tools within the language space of TD's. ToolDreamer improves the performance of sparse and dense retrievers with or without training, demonstrating its flexibility. The objective is to offload a portion of reasoning burden to the retriever, allowing the LLM to handle a large collection of tools effectively without overwhelming its context window.

categories: PDoctor is a proposed automated approach to test LLM agents and understand their erroneous planning. It formulates the detection of erroneous planning as a constraint satisfiability problem., A domain-specific language (DSL) for user queries is defined by PDoctor, and it synthesizes varying inputs with the assistance of the Z3 constraint solver., These synthesized inputs are natural language paragraphs that specify the requirements for completing a series of tasks., PDoctor then derives constraints from these requirements to form a testing oracle.

title: In spite of the great potential of large language models (LLMs) across various tasks, their...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel phase-aware quantization method to improve the efficiency of large language models (LLMs) on resource-constrained devices. The proposed approach, Progressive Mixed-Precision Decoding (PMPD), selectively allocates precision during different phases of LLM inference to enhance context extraction and memory bandwidth utilization. PMPD achieves significant speedup in matrix-vector multiplications over fp16 models when targeting Nvidia GPUs, and a throughput gain over both fp16 models and uniform quantization approaches on an LLM-optimized NPU, while preserving output quality.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) and causal learning each hold strong potential for clinical decision making...
categories: The study uses 15 prominent Large Language Models (LLMs), 6 traditional Machine Learning (ML) models, and 3 causal discovery (CD) algorithms. The LLMs, ML models, and CD algorithms are used to evaluate the performance of LLMs in clinical prognostics through the REACT-LLM benchmark.

title: This paper introduces Tele-LLM-Hub, a user friendly low-code solution for rapid prototyping and deployment of...
abstract: The abstract presents Tele-LLM-Hub, a user-friendly low-code solution for prototyping and deploying context-aware multi-agent Large Language Model systems designed for 5G and beyond networks. As telecom wireless networks grow in complexity, the need arises for intelligent applications to have a domain-specific understanding of network state. The paper proposes TeleMCP, the Telecom Model Context Protocol, to facilitate structured and context-rich communication between agents in telecom environments. Tele-LLM-Hub implements TeleMCP via a low-code interface that supports agent creation, workflow composition, and interaction with software stacks like srsRAN. Notable components include a direct chat interface, a repository of pre-built systems, an Agent Maker using finetuning with the RANSTRUCT framework, and an MA-Maker for constructing MA workflows. The main objective of Tele-LLM-Hub is to promote the democratization of context-aware multi-agent system design and spur innovation in next-generation wireless networks.

title: We investigate the existence and persistence of a specific type of gender bias in some...
abstract: The study investigates gender bias in popular LLMs, introducing RealWorldQuestioning dataset from business and health domains (education, jobs, personal finance, health). The research focuses on entropy bias, a discrepancy in the information generated by LLMs. Tested with four LLMs, responses were evaluated both qualitatively and quantitatively using ChatGPT-4o. Findings show no significant bias at category level but substantial differences at individual question level for men and women, often canceling out. A simple debiasing approach is suggested to merge gendered responses, which demonstrates improvement in information content in 78% of cases while maintaining balance in the rest.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable general capabilities, but enhancing skills such as reasoning...
abstract: The study introduces Universal Reasoner (UniR), a lightweight, composable, and plug-and-play reasoning module designed to enhance specialized reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs) without compromising their generalization. UniR decomposes the reward into a standalone reasoning module that is trained independently using predefined rewards, effectively translating trajectory-level signals into token-level guidance. At inference time, UniR can be combined with any frozen LLM by adding its output logits to those of the LLM backbone. The additive structure allows for modular composition, enabling complex reasoning via combination of multiple UniR modules trained for different tasks. Experimental results on mathematical reasoning and machine translation tasks show that UniR significantly outperforms existing baseline fine-tuning methods using the Llama3.2 model.
categories: UniR involves decomposing the reward into a standalone reasoning module, Training this reasoning module independently using predefined rewards, Combining the trained UniR with any frozen LLM at inference time by adding its output logits to those of the LLM backbone, Summing the logits of multiple UniR modules trained for different tasks to perform complex reasoning

title: The rapid proliferation of Large Language Models (LLMs) has significantly contributed to the development of...
abstract: The abstract introduces a novel technique, ObfusQAte, and a comprehensive framework, ObfusQA, for evaluating the robustness of Large Language Models (LLMs) by presenting them with obfuscated versions of questions. The study reveals that LLMs struggle to handle these nuanced variations, either failing or generating incorrect responses. The framework consists of multiple tiers of obfuscation levels and examines LLM capabilities across three dimensions: Named-Entity Indirection, Distractor Indirection, and Contextual Overload. To facilitate further research in this direction, ObfusQAte is made publicly available.

title: In a malicious tool attack, an attacker uploads a malicious tool to a distribution platform;...
abstract: The abstract discusses a study focusing on malicious tool code implementations in the context of LLM-agent settings. The research aims to bridge the gap in understanding how attackers can compromise security and privacy by embedding malicious behaviors into tools' code. A taxonomy of malicious tool behaviors is proposed, based on the confidentiality-integrity-availability triad, tailored for LLM-agent environments.
categories: The study presents MalTool, a coding-LLM-based framework that synthesizes tools with specified malicious behaviors. It can generate both standalone malicious tools and those embedded within otherwise benign implementations., MalTool utilizes an automated verifier to ensure functional correctness and structural diversity. The verifier validates whether the generated tools exhibit the intended malicious behaviors and differ sufficiently from previous instances, refining generations until success.

title: This study introduces "CosmoAgent," an innovative artificial intelligence system that utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs)...
abstract: The study presents 'CosmoAgent', an AI system that employs Large Language Models (LLMs) to simulate interactions between human and extraterrestrial civilizations. The research introduces a mathematical model for quantifying civilization development levels and uses a state transition matrix approach to evaluate their trajectories. This study offers insights into future decision-making at critical points of growth and saturation for civilizations. Recognizing the diversity of potential living conditions across the universe, the paper acknowledges the possibility of unique cosmologies, ethical codes, and worldviews among different civilizations.
categories: Quantitative analysis using LLMs to simulate inter-civilizational dynamics., Mathematical model for quantifying civilization development levels., State transition matrix approach to evaluate civilization trajectories., Proposing LLM agents with diverse ethical paradigms and simulating interactions between entities with distinct moral principles.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance on natural language tasks, but their decision-making...
abstract: The proposed method, ProtoSurE, is a novel prototype-based surrogate framework that provides faithful and human-understandable explanations for large language models (LLMs). It addresses the challenges in existing explanation methods by training an interpretable-by-design surrogate model that aligns with the target LLM while using sentence-level prototypes as human-understandable concepts. ProtoSurE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art explanation methods across diverse LLMs and datasets, demonstrating strong data efficiency requiring few training examples for good performance. This makes it practical for real-world applications.

title: Since state-of-the-art LLMs often underperform in languages other than English or Chinese, improving the capability...
abstract: This paper describes methods to adapt an existing English-based Language Model (LLM) for Korean in a low-budget scenario. The study details the entire end-to-end process including data collection, preprocessing, training the model, creating downstream benchmarks, and evaluations. Results indicate the proposed method effectively and cost-efficiently adds new language capabilities to existing LLMs, with new bilingual models, Thunder-LLM and Thunder-LLM-Ins, achieving superior Korean performance compared to state-of-the-art models while utilizing minimal data and computational resources. The authors share their comprehensive experience and make the code publicly available.

abstract: The study analyzes the design space for LLM chaining by surveying 107 papers across the crowdsourcing and chaining literature. It constructs a design space that covers designers' objectives and tactics used to build workflows. The study also identifies strategies that mediate how workflows use tactics to achieve objectives. To validate the applicability of techniques from crowdsourcing to chaining, three case studies are presented: creating a taxonomy, shortening text, and writing a short story. From these findings, takeaways for effective chain design are identified and implications for future research and development are raised.

title: Large language model (LLM) inference often suffers from high latency, particularly in resource-constrained environments such...
abstract: StorInfer is a novel storage-assisted LLM inference system designed to address high latency issues, particularly in resource-constrained environments. It achieves this by precomputing and storing predictable query-response pairs offline. When a user query matches a stored pair, StorInfer returns the stored response directly, thus reducing latency and compute costs. The system uses an LLM-driven generator to adaptively produce diverse and deduplicated queries based on a given knowledge base, employing techniques like adaptive query masking and adaptive sampling for semantic diversity. The generated 150K unique precomputed pairs take up to 830 MB of storage space. This system was evaluated across multiple QA datasets demonstrating its practicality and scalability in scenarios with predictable query distributions, highlighting StorInfer as a promising direction for efficient, low-latency LLM deployment.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) services exhibit impressive capability on unlearned tasks leveraging only a few...
abstract: This paper introduces a novel method to estimate the performance of Large Language Model (LLM) services across different tasks and contexts using only a few unlabeled samples, similar to in-context learning (ICL). The proposed method utilizes the negative log-likelihood and perplexity derived from LLM service invocation as effective features. Four distinct meta-models are used to estimate the performance of LLM services based on these features. The findings suggest that this method can be 'plug-and-play' and is applicable to various LLM services and tasks.
categories: Utilization of negative log-likelihood and perplexity derived from LLM service invocation as effective features, Application of four distinct meta-models to estimate the performance of LLM services based on these features

The authors propose using persona-based LLM prompting, a low-cost method for emulating diverse writing styles, to test this hypothesis.

It aims to leverage both internal knowledge of LLMs and external spatial information. EquiLLM consists of four components: geometry-aware prompting, an equivariant encoder, an LLM, and an equivariant adaptor.

title: Recent advancements in multi-model AI systems have leveraged LLM routers to reduce computational cost while...
abstract: This work focuses on the security implications of LLM rerouting attacks in multi-model AI systems. These attacks manipulate routing decisions by prepending specially crafted triggers to user queries, potentially causing increased computational cost, degraded response quality, and safety guardrails bypass. The study systematizes these threats based on the adversary's objectives (cost escalation, quality hijacking, and safety bypass) and knowledge. The measurement study reveals that existing routing systems are vulnerable to rerouting attacks, particularly in the cost escalation scenario. Through interpretability techniques, existing rerouting attacks are characterized as exploiting router decision boundaries through confounder gadgets that prepend queries to force misrouting.
categories: The study introduces RerouteGuard, a flexible and scalable guardrail framework for LLM rerouting., RerouteGuard filters adversarial rerouting prompts via dynamic embedding-based detection and adaptive thresholding.

In the LLM4RL class, an LLM assists the training of an RL model that performs a task not inherently related to natural language., RL training framework components: The LLM4RL class is further broken down based on the component of the RL training framework that the LLM assists or replaces. These include reward shaping, goal generation, and policy function., Common planning framework: In the third class, RL+LLM, an LLM and an RL agent are embedded in a common planning framework without either contributing to training or fine-tuning of the other.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate code, but can they generate fast code? In this...
abstract: The paper analyzes the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate fast code using a dataset of real-world tasks. The results show that while LLM-generated code generally performs better than the baseline, human-authored solutions outperform LLM fixes significantly. The study also finds that LLMs often match or are similar to the developer optimization idea in about two-thirds of cases, but these original ideas only occasionally yield substantial performance gains.

categories: 400 ophthalmology questions and paired answers were created by ophthalmologists to represent commonly asked patient questions, divided into fine-tuning (368; 92%), and testing (40; 8%). We find-tuned 5 different LLMs, including LLAMA2-7b, LLAMA2-7b-Chat, LLAMA2-13b, and LLAMA2-13b-Chat. For the testing dataset, additional 8 glaucoma QnA pairs were included. 200 responses to the testing dataset were generated by 5 fine-tuned LLMs for evaluation. A customized clinical evaluation rubric was used to guide GPT-4 evaluation, grounded on clinical accuracy, relevance, patient safety, and ease of understanding.

The research reveals challenges such as stochasticity, sensitivity to structural and semantic perturbations, and susceptibility to DAG misspecification in continuous domains. Despite these limitations, the study also open-sources the benchmarking framework for researchers to utilize their own DAGs and off-the-shelf LLMs for evaluation in various domains.

abstract: The abstract discusses the hallucination problem in Large Language Models (LLMs), which impacts their reliability and trustworthiness. The research analyzes internal mechanisms of LLMs across various Natural Language Generation tasks, using over 700 datasets.

abstract: This study investigates the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) to democratize real estate insights by generating house price estimates through optimized In-Context Learning (ICL) strategies. The research finds that LLMs can offer a more accessible, interactive, and interpretable alternative compared to traditional machine learning models. However, they struggle with overconfidence in price intervals and limited spatial reasoning.

Taiwan LLM outperforms existing models predominantly trained on Simplified Chinese or English in understanding and generating Traditional Chinese text.

abstract: The paper investigates whether large language models (LLMs) can become faster at reasoning through recurrent exposure on relevant tasks. abstract: The abstract discusses the high cost of training Large Language Models (LLMs) due to computational, hardware, energy, and engineering demands. It highlights an often-overlooked expense: human labor behind the models' training data. The paper aims to assign a monetary value to this labor and argues that compensation for training data producers should be the most expensive part of producing an LLM. After studying 64 LLMs released between 2016 and 2024, it's estimated that the costs of these models' training datasets are significantly larger than the costs to train the models themselves. The paper suggests research directions for fairer practices in the future due to the massive gap between the value of training data and the lack of compensation for its creation.

title: Inference-time alignment enables large language models (LLMs) to generate outputs aligned with end-user preferences without...
abstract: PITA is a new framework designed to align LLM outputs with user preferences during inference. It learns a small preference-based guidance policy that modifies token probabilities without LLM fine-tuning, reducing computational cost and bypassing the pre-trained reward model dependency. The authors evaluate PITA across diverse tasks such as mathematical reasoning and sentiment classification, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: Controlling undesirable Large Language Model (LLM) behaviors, such as the generation of unsafe content or...
categories: Introduction of a lightweight, trainable controller network during inference., The controller network observes specific intermediate LLM activations and predicts both a global scaling factor and layer-specific weights., The predicted global scaling factor and layer-specific weights dynamically modulate the intensity of a steering patch derived from a pre-computed 'refusal direction' vector., The steering patch is applied across the LLM's layers during generation., Training of the controller network on activations from harmful and benign prompts to learn nuanced, layer-aware interventions.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are commonly used as evaluators in various applications, but the reliability...
categories: To mitigate this bias, the study introduces contrastive decoding. This method led to an average relative improvement of up to 11.3% in Spearman correlation with human judgments across different score ranges.

title: Using the full potential of LLMs in higher education is hindered by challenges with access...
abstract: The paper discusses the challenges in utilizing Language Learning Models (LLMs) in higher education due to access issues. It introduces FLEXI, an open LLM infrastructure project at FernUniversität in Hagen, aimed at experimenting with teaching and research using locally maintained open LLMs. The objective of FLEXI is to generate evidence supporting (or contradicting) the use of locally maintained open LLMs in higher education.
categories: Description of the current state of establishing an open LLM infrastructure at FernUniversität in Hagen under the project name FLEXI., Experimentation within teaching and research using FLEXI for generating needed evidence.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have opened new opportunities for transforming natural language user intents into...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for transforming natural language user intents into executable actions for embodied AI agents, particularly in the context of human-robot interaction (HRI). The paper highlights security and privacy concerns such as self-preferencing, where a single provider dominates the market. It proposes an LLM oracle to decentralize LLMs and introduces a new aggregation method for robotic task planning. The proposed architecture includes a decentralized multi-robot infrastructure based on Hyperledger Fabric. The research aims to improve upon current aggregation methods unsuitable for robotic task planning due to their focus on semantic similarity rather than the temporal order of tasks.
categories: Proposal of an LLM oracle with a new aggregation method for robotic task planning., Proposal of a decentralized multi-robot infrastructure based on Hyperledger Fabric that can host the proposed oracle., Expression of natural language intent to the system, decomposition into subtasks, coordination of different robots from different vendors, and fine-grained access control management on data.

title: As on-device LLMs(e.g., Apple on-device Intelligence) are widely adopted to reduce network dependency, improve privacy,...
abstract: The AttestLLM framework is introduced as a solution for ensuring that only authorized LLMs can execute on target platforms. It achieves this through an algorithm/software/hardware co-design approach, embedding robust watermarking signatures onto the activation distributions of LLM building blocks. The framework optimizes the attestation protocol within the Trusted Execution Environment (TEE), ensuring efficient verification without compromising inference throughput. The paper provides extensive proof-of-concept evaluations on LLMs from Llama, Qwen, and Phi families for on-device use cases. AttestLLM is shown to demonstrate attestation reliability, fidelity, and efficiency. It enforces model legitimacy and resilience against model replacement and forgery attacks.

title: Large language model unlearning aims to remove harmful information that LLMs have learnt to prevent...
abstract: The abstract discusses the concept of large language model (LLM) unlearning, which aims to remove harmful information from LLMs to prevent misuse. The focus is on the impact of unlearning on LLM performance metrics using specific datasets. Two methods, LLMU and RMU, have been proposed for LLM unlearning, showing promising results in unlearning benchmarks. The study demonstrates that unlearning significantly impacts general model capabilities, with a more significant performance degradation observed for the LLMU method. Additionally, they show that simple techniques like 5-shot prompting or rephrasing questions can increase accuracy on unlearning benchmarks by over tenfold. The researchers also find that training on unrelated data nearly recovers pre-unlearning performance, indicating a failure of these methods to truly unlearn.
categories: LLMU and RMU - two proposed methods for Large Language Model (LLM) unlearning, WMDP dataset used for evaluation

title: Science journalism reports current scientific discoveries to non-specialists, aiming to enable public comprehension of the...
abstract: The abstract presents a new approach for science journalism using three LLMs that mimic real-world writing-reading-feedback-revision workflow. The proposed method generates articles that are more accessible than those produced by existing methods, including advanced models like GPT-4.

title: Computer science has historically presented barriers for non-native English speaking (NNES) students, often due to...
abstract: The study deploys a Large Language Model (LLM) tutor in an accelerated introductory computing course for NNES and NES students to evaluate its effectiveness specifically for NNES students. NNES students signed up for the LLM tutor at a similar rate as NES students, but used it at a lower rate with a small effect. NNES students asked significantly more questions in languages other than English compared to NES students, often incorporating English programming keywords. Both NNES and NES students appreciated the LLM tutor's accessibility, conversational style, and guardrails for guiding users to answers. NNES students found the LLM tutor approachable as they did not need to communicate in perfect English. NNES students rated help-seeking preferences of online resources higher than NES students. Many NNES students were unfamiliar with computing terminology in their native languages. The results suggest that LLM tutors can be a valuable resource for NNES students, providing tailored support that enhances their learning experience and overcomes language barriers.
categories: Deployment of an LLM tutor in an accelerated introductory computing course., Evaluation of the effectiveness of the LLM tutor specifically for NNES students., Analysis of student sign-up rates, usage rates, question patterns, and feedback.

title: Automated program repair has emerged as a powerful technique to mitigate the impact of software...
categories: RepairAgent is an autonomous agent based on a large language model (LLM)., Unlike existing deep learning-based approaches, RepairAgent treats the LLM as an agent capable of autonomously planning and executing actions to fix bugs by invoking suitable tools., The agent freely interleaves gathering information about the bug, gathering repair ingredients, and validating fixes., RepairAgent decides which tools to invoke based on the gathered information and feedback from previous fix attempts., Key contributions that enable RepairAgent include a set of tools useful for program repair, a dynamically updated prompt format that allows the LLM to interact with these tools, and a finite state machine that guides the agent in invoking the tools.

title: The rise of Large Language Models has created a general excitement about the great potential...

title: Attributing authorship in the era of large language models (LLMs) is increasingly challenging as machine-generated...
abstract: The study investigates attributing authorship in the era of large language models using two methods: Style Embeddings and an LLM judge (GPT-4o). The research reveals that Style Embeddings perform better on GPT-4o continuations, while the LLM judge excels in fiction and academic prose. Both methods show varying strengths based on the domain of the text. The study provides code and data for reproducibility, offering a benchmark for assessing attribution quality in AI-generated content.

title: Recent advancements in LLMs indicate potential for novel applications, as evidenced by the reasoning capabilities...
Recent progress in LLMs discussion suggests that multi-agent discussion improves the reasoning abilities of LLMs....
abstract: The abstract discusses the impact of multi-agent discussion on the reasoning abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). The research aims to reevaluate this claim through systematic experiments, introducing a novel group discussion framework to enhance the set of conversation mechanisms. Interestingly, it is found that a single-agent LLM with strong prompts can almost match the performance of the best existing discussion approach on various reasoning tasks and different base LLMs. The study also reveals that multi-agent discussion outperforms a single agent only in scenarios without demonstration in the prompt.
categories: Systematic experiments are conducted to reevaluate the impact of multi-agent discussion on LLMs., A novel group discussion framework is proposed to enrich the set of conversation mechanisms., Performance comparison between single-agent LLM with strong prompts and the best existing discussion approach is made on a wide range of reasoning tasks.

title: Large language models (LLMs) make it possible to generate synthetic behavioural data at scale, offering...
abstract: The study investigates the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to replicate personality-based variations in susceptibility to misinformation, focusing on news discernment. The research reveals that certain trait-misinformation associations are reliably replicated, particularly those involving Agreeableness and Conscientiousness, while others diverge, indicating systematic biases in how LLMs internalize and express personality.
categories:
1. Conditioning LLM agents on Big-Five personality profiles
2. Evaluating the LLM agents' responses to headline accuracy based on published datasets of human participants with known personality profiles
3. Comparing the LLM agents' responses to the original human patterns

title: Traditionally, AI development for two-player zero-sum games has relied on two primary techniques: decision trees...
abstract: The study proposes RL-LLM-DT, a novel AI development approach for two-player zero-sum games. This method automates the decision tree generation process by using reinforcement learning (RL) to discover counter-strategies and Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate improved decision trees. Experiments conducted in a curling game demonstrate the effectiveness of this integrated approach, with the AI based on RL-LLM-DT ranking first among 34 curling AIs on the Jidi platform, showcasing the enhanced robustness and adaptability of decision trees compared to traditional methods.

title: Transformers, driven by attention mechanisms, form the foundation of large language models (LLMs). As these...
abstract: The abstract discusses FlashInfer, an efficient and customizable attention engine for large language model (LLM) serving. It optimizes memory access and reduces redundancy through KV-cache storage heterogeneity solutions like block-sparse format and composable formats. FlashInfer offers a flexible attention template for Just-In-Time compilation and a load-balanced scheduling algorithm that adapts to user requests. Integrated into SGLang, vLLM, and MLC-Engine, it significantly boosts kernel performance across diverse inference scenarios. Key improvements include 29-69% inter-token-latency reduction compared to compiler backends for LLM serving benchmark, 28-30% latency reduction for long-context inference, and 13-17% speedup for parallel generation. keywords

title: Large language model (LLM)-based automatic speech recognition (ASR) achieves strong performance but often incurs high...
categories: The methods employed in this work include developing a new multi-stage LLM-ASR training strategy, EFIN (Encoder First Integration). This approach involves pretraining the speech encoder before integrating it with the LLM. Additionally, a scaling law is derived to approximate ASR error rates as a computation function, offering practical guidance for LLM-ASR scaling.

categories: Classical approaches in trial-patient matching are trial-specific., LLMs (Large Language Models) can consolidate distributed knowledge for better general solutions in clinical trial recruitment., The emergence of LLM-based approaches in clinical trial recruitment.

title: Simulation powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) has become a promising method for exploring complex...
We present Isabellm, an LLM-powered theorem prover for Isabelle/HOL that performs fully automatic proof synthesis....
This study presents a cross-national quantitative analysis of how university students in the United States...
abstract: The study reveals that LLMs improve access to information, writing skills, and academic performance among both U.S. and Bangladeshi students. However, concerns about overreliance, ethical risks, and critical thinking are acknowledged. The research finds that motivational beliefs and technical competencies influence the engagement with LLMs. Significant correlations were found between LLM use and perceived literacy benefits, as well as optimism. U.S. students and STEM majors were found to use LLMs more frequently. The findings support the development of ethical, inclusive, and pedagogically sound frameworks for integrating LLMs in higher education.

title: With the rapid growth of Large Language Models (LLMs) across various domains, numerous new LLMs...
We introduce a novel writing method called Probing Chain-of-Thought (ProCoT), which potentially prevents students from...
Many real world tasks where Large Language Models (LLMs) can be used require spatial reasoning,...
abstract: The abstract introduces DistRAG, a novel approach designed to enhance Large Language Models (LLMs) in tasks requiring spatial reasoning such as Point of Interest recommendation and itinerary planning. The issue addressed is that LLMs lack reliable spatial capabilities, particularly regarding distances. DistRAG addresses this by encoding geodesic distances between cities and towns in a graph and retrieving relevant context subgraphs for questions. This enables LLMs to answer distance-based reasoning questions they couldn't before. The method offers a flexible first step towards providing a rudimentary 'world model' to complement the linguistic knowledge held in LLMs.

title: We examine how large language models (LLMs) interpret historical stock returns and compare their forecasts...
abstract: The abstract discusses that large language models tend to over-extrapolate short-term trends in stock returns, similar to human behavior. Their forecasts are optimistic but better calibrated than survey evidence when it comes to confidence intervals. However, they underestimate outlier events, leading to skewed forecast distributions. The research suggests that while LLMs show common behavioral biases in forecasting expected returns, they might be more effective at gauging risks compared to humans.

title: We show, contrary to the optimism about LLM's problem-solving abilities, fueled by the recent gold...
Multi-agent systems (MAS) powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) have been demonstrated to push the...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development and implementation of MasRouter, a solution for Multi-Agent System Routing (MASR). This unified routing framework aims to optimize multi-agent systems powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) by balancing effectiveness and efficiency. It addresses the challenge of dynamic LLM selection and collaborative decision-making in MAS.
categories: MasRouter employs a cascaded controller network to determine collaboration modes, allocate roles, and route LLMs within the system. This progressive construction approach allows for an optimal balance between effectiveness and efficiency.

title: Existing AI Music composition tools are limited in generation duration, musical quality, and controllability. We...
categories: CoComposer is a multi-agent system that consists of five collaborating agents, each with a task based on the traditional music composition workflow. The agents work together to compose music using their respective tasks in a coordinated manner.

title: The field of computer architecture, which bridges high-level software abstractions and low-level hardware implementations, remains...
categories: QuArch benchmark is designed for the development and evaluation of LLM knowledge and reasoning capabilities specifically in computer architecture., It provides a comprehensive collection of 2,671 expert-validated question-answer (QA) pairs covering various aspects of computer architecture such as processor design, memory systems, and interconnection networks.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT have become increasingly integrated into critical activities of...
abstract: The study examines the usage of Large Language Models (LLMs) among 1500 US citizens. It finds that 42% of participants use LLMs, with a gender gap in adoption favoring males. Age factors also play a role. Technology-related education eliminates this gender gap. Expert users tend to list professional tasks as typical application scenarios, indicating discrepancies in effective usage at work. The findings emphasize the need for AI education to promote equitable access and benefits from LLMs in our technology-driven society.
categories: Survey of 1500 representative US citizens, Data analysis to identify usage patterns, gender gap, age factors, and professional application scenarios

title: Large language models, with their strong reasoning ability and rich knowledge, have brought revolution to...
abstract: In this paper, the authors propose TEAch Me Sign (TEAM-Sign), a method that treats sign language as another natural language. By fine-tuning an LLM and employing a stepwise prompting strategy, TEAM-Sign allows LLMs to learn the correspondence between text and sign language. Experimental results show its effectiveness in aligning the different distribution and grammatical rules between sign and spoken language.

title: As AI models progress beyond simple chatbots into more complex workflows, we draw ever closer...
abstract: The study presents an algorithmic framework called average bias-boundedness (A-BB) to address the issue of unknown or adversarially discovered biases in LLM judges, which are used as sources of rewards and feedback in autonomous AI systems. The proposed framework guarantees reductions of harm/impact due to any measurable bias. In evaluation on Arena-Hard-Auto with four LLM judges, they achieved (tau=0.5, delta=0.01) bias-bounded guarantees while retaining 61-99% correlation with original rankings across formatting and schematic bias settings. Most judge-bias combinations exceeded 80%. The code for reproducing their findings is available at https://github.com/penfever/bias-bounded-evaluation.

title: The growing adoption of large language models (LLMs) has led to a new paradigm in...
abstract: This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of mobile Large Language Model (LLM) agents. The study covers three representative categories: System-level AI Agents, Third-party Universal Agents, and Emerging Agent Frameworks. The authors identify 11 distinct attack surfaces in mobile LLM agents across their entire operational lifecycle. AgentScan, a semi-automated security analysis framework, is introduced to evaluate agents across all 11 attack scenarios. The research finds that every agent tested is vulnerable to targeted attacks, with the most severe cases exhibiting vulnerabilities across eight distinct attack vectors. These attacks can result in behavioral deviations, privacy leakage, or full execution hijacking.
categories: Analysis of the general workflow of mobile agents and identification of security threats across three core capability dimensions: language-based reasoning, GUI-based interaction, and system-level execution., Systematic evaluation of mobile LLM agents using AgentScan across all 11 attack scenarios, Discussion of defensive design principles and practical recommendations for building secure mobile LLM agents

title: As they become more capable, large language models (LLMs) have continued to rapidly increase in...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel, end-to-end, lossless model compression method named Huff-LLM. This method aims to address the challenge of running large language models (LLMs) on small, edge devices. The key feature is its ability to compress and store LLM weights in a compressed format across various storage locations - cloud, disk, main memory, on-chip memory/buffers. This allows for loading larger models into main memory, reducing bandwidth needed to load weights on chip, and making more efficient use of on-chip weight buffers. The compression method also results in latency and energy efficiency improvements during inference. The abstract suggests that while standard techniques focus on lossy compression methods like quantization or pruning, Huff-LLM provides a lossless alternative which maintains the original model behavior.
categories: Huff-LLM: A proposed end-to-end, lossless model compression method that can store and compress LLM weights across various storage locations., Compression of LLM weights for efficient use of memory on edge devices, Reduction in bandwidth required to load weights on chip, Efficient utilization of on-chip weight buffers, Latency and energy efficiency improvements during inference with the compressed model

title: Although the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs) in enterprise settings brings new opportunities and...
abstract: The abstract discusses the introduction of Large Language Models (LLMs) in enterprise settings, highlighting their potential benefits but also the challenges they pose, particularly in terms of generating inappropriate, biased, or misleading content that may violate regulations and lead to legal issues. To mitigate this risk, a tool named 'LLMGuard' is presented as a solution.
categories: LLMGuard monitors user interactions with an LLM application., It flags content against specific behaviours or conversation topics., The tool employs an ensemble of detectors to perform this task robustly.

title: Time series forecasting is central to data analysis and web technologies. The recent success of...
categories: Following an LLM-as-Enhancer paradigm to fully utilize the advantage of LLM in text understanding., Proposing FiCoTS, an LLM-enhanced fine-to-coarse framework for multimodal time series forecasting., The framework facilitates progressive cross-modality interaction by three levels:, - In the token-level modality alignment module, a dynamic heterogeneous graph is constructed to filter noise and align time series patches with text tokens., - In the feature-level modality interaction module, a global cross-attention mechanism is introduced to enable each time series variable to connect with relevant textual contexts., - In the decision-level modality fusion module, a gated network is designed to adaptively fuse the results of the two modalities for robust predictions.

title: Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) often require external tools, such as machine learning classifiers or...
Math reasoning has been one crucial ability of large language models (LLMs), where significant advancements...
abstract: The abstract discusses the lag in math reasoning performance of multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) compared to traditional language models. The authors propose a tuning-free approach called IP-Merging to enhance MLLMs' math reasoning ability by identifying and aligning crucial reasoning-associated parameters with those from off-the-shelf math language models, without compromising their other capabilities. The study suggests that existing model-merging approaches overlook the alignment between MLLMs and Math LLMs, which results in a large gap between their parameter spaces. The authors highlight two key factors contributing to this issue: the identification of reasoning-associated layers and the mitigation of the gaps in parameter space. The proposed IP-Merging method involves three steps: identifying reasoning-associated parameters, projecting them into an MLLM subspace to maintain alignment, and merging parameters within this subspace. This method aims to improve math reasoning ability directly from Math LLMs without tuning.

title: To render each generated token in real-time for users, the Large Language Model (LLM) server...
abstract: The abstract discusses a problem in Large Language Model (LLM) chatbots under unstable network conditions, where one packet loss can cause stalls in token streaming and affect the user experience. The paper proposes a novel transmission scheme named Eloquent, which aims to reduce these stalls by ensuring that each packet contains new tokens and can be independently rendered upon receipt. Eloquent is shown through simulation to significantly reduce the stall ratio compared to the commonly used retransmission method and a baseline packet duplication scheme.
categories: The paper proposes Eloquent, a transmission scheme that puts newly generated tokens as well as currently unacknowledged tokens in the next outgoing packet., Each packet is designed to contain some new tokens and can be independently rendered when received.

title: Probing techniques have shown promise in revealing how LLMs encode human-interpretable concepts, particularly when applied...
abstract: The study investigates the relationship between LLM (Language Model's) performance on probing tasks and response uncertainty. It reveals a strong correlation between improved probe performance and reduced response uncertainty, and vice versa. The analysis also indicates that high LLM response variance is associated with a larger set of important features, which poses a greater challenge for probe models and often results in diminished performance. Concrete examples are provided where LLM representations align with human knowledge across diverse domains, offering additional evidence of interpretable reasoning in LLMs. methods

title: As LLMs grow more powerful, their most profound achievement may be recognising when to say...
abstract: This study explores the self-knowledge of Large Language Models (LLMs), focusing on their ability to set feasibility boundaries and understand their own capabilities. The research reveals that even advanced models like GPT-4o and Mistral Large are uncertain about their abilities more than 80% of the time, indicating a lack of trustworthiness in responses. The analysis of confidence balance in LLMs shows that models exhibit oscillation between overconfidence and conservatism in feasibility boundaries depending on task categories. The study identifies temporal awareness and contextual understanding as significant self-knowledge weaknesses, leading to considerable confusion within the self-knowledge of LLMs.
categories: Allowing LLMs the flexibility to set their own feasibility boundaries, Analyzing the consistency of these limits

title: Large Language Models (LLMs), exemplified by ChatGPT, have significantly reshaped text generation, particularly in the...
abstract: The abstract discusses the infrequent acknowledgment of Large Language Model (LLM) use in scientific communication, despite their significant impact on text generation. It introduces four advanced LLM-generated text detectors and their suboptimal performance. The study also proposes a need for specialized detectors dedicated to LLM-assisted writing detection to foster more authentic recognition of LLM involvement in scientific communication and address the current challenges in acknowledgment practices.

title: Cost-efficient path planning across multiple terrains is a crucial task in robot navigation, requiring the...
categories: The paper proposes LLM-Advisor, a prompt-based, planner-agnostic framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) as non-decisive post-processing advisors for cost refinement. The LLM-Advisor does not modify the underlying planner. To mitigate hallucination from LLMs, the authors introduce two strategies. The paper also introduces two datasets, MultiTerraPath and RUGD_v2, for systematic evaluation of cost-efficient path planning.

title: There is general agreement that fostering trust and cooperation within the AI development ecosystem is...
abstract: The research investigates the complex interactions between AI developers, regulators, and users within an evolutionary game-theoretic framework, using LLMs to model their strategic choices under different regulatory scenarios. It identifies that strategic AI agents often adopt 'pessimistic' stances, and notes that full trust from users can be beneficial for effective regulation, while conditional trust may negatively impact the overall system. The paper concludes that a virtuous feedback loop between users' trust and regulators' reputation is crucial in encouraging developers to create safe AI. However, the level of trust may vary depending on the specific LLM used for testing.

title: The field of large language models (LLMs) has grown rapidly in recent years, driven by...
abstract: This study builds on previous research to explore personality modification in large language models (LLMs), utilizing the approach of 'activation engineering'. The goal is to develop a method for identifying and adjusting activation directions related to personality traits, potentially enabling dynamic LLM personality fine-tuning. This work aims to advance understanding of LLM interpretability while examining the ethical implications of such developments.

abstract: The abstract discusses DP2Unlearning, a novel language model (LLM) unlearning framework that provides formal forgetting guarantees at a lower cost than retraining from scratch. This is achieved by training LLMs on textual data protected using ε-differential privacy (DP), which later enables efficient unlearning with the associated disclosure guarantees. The experiments demonstrate that DP2Unlearning achieves similar model performance post-unlearning to exact unlearning, but at approximately half the unlearning cost. It also outperforms approximate unlearning methods in preserving the utility of the model and effectively forgetting targeted information. ], methods**: [ Training LLMs on textual data protected using ε-differential privacy (DP) Efficient unlearning through modifications of the trained model itself Evaluation of the model performance post-unlearning and comparison with exact unlearning and approximate unlearning methods

title: As Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents increasingly engage with human society, how well do we...
abstract: The study investigates prosocial behaviors in Large Language Model (LLM) agents, revealing that simply assigning a human-like identity doesn't guarantee human-like behaviors. The research finds variations in LLMs' decision-making across model architectures and prompts, without a clear pattern. The findings highlight the need for 'Prosocial AI' as a promising and urgent research direction in philanthropic studies, given the increasing integration of machine intelligence in society.

title: Code generation is one of the most active areas of application of Large Language Models...
abstract: The abstract discusses the application of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation and highlights their role in lowering barriers to writing code and accelerating the development process. It emphasizes that while LLMs can generate code, the quality of the programs depends significantly on the quality of the given prompts. The authors mention a user's background and familiarity with software development as factors influencing functionality and quality of generated code.
categories: Proposing an evaluation pipeline for LLM code generation aimed at measuring sensitivity to prompt augmentations, The method is agnostic to specific programming tasks and LLMs, making it widely applicable

title: AI agents have become surprisingly proficient at software engineering over the past year, largely due...
abstract: The paper explores the potential for AI agents to automate AI research itself, focusing on the post-training phase that transforms base Language Models (LLMs) into useful assistants. The authors introduce PostTrainBench, a tool to evaluate how well LLM agents can perform post-training autonomously under compute constraints. Frontier agents are asked to optimize the performance of a base LLM on specific benchmarks without providing predefined strategies or limits.
categories: PostTrainBench is used to benchmark LLM agents' post-training abilities under bounded compute constraints (10 hours on one H100 GPU)., Frontier agents like Claude Code with Opus 4.6 are asked to optimize the performance of a base LLM (e.g., Qwen3-4B on AIME) without any predefined strategies., Agents are given autonomy to find necessary information online, run experiments, and curate data for optimization.

title: Personalized tool utilization is essential for aligning large language models (LLMs) with user preference in...
abstract: In this work, the researchers propose ETAPP, a comprehensive benchmark and dataset for evaluating personalized tool invocation in LLMs. The benchmark includes a sandbox environment and 800 testing cases covering diverse user profiles. A key-point-based evaluation method is also introduced to improve accuracy and mitigate biases in the LLM-as-a-judge system. The study evaluates top-performing LLMs, investigates various tool-invoking strategies' effects on personalization performance, and assesses the impact of fine-tuning. The effectiveness of their preference-setting and key-point-based evaluation method is validated.

title: We study methods for efficiently aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences given budgeted...
abstract: The study focuses on efficiently aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences using budgeted online feedback. A formulation of the LLM alignment problem is presented within the framework of contextual dueling bandits, encompassing recent paradigms like online RLHF and online DPO. The formulation seeks sample-efficient algorithms that incorporate online active exploration, leading to a unified algorithm based on Thompson sampling. The new algorithm is applied in two distinct LLM alignment scenarios. The practical agent that implements this algorithm, named SEA (Sample-Efficient Alignment), is empirically validated through extensive experiments across different model scales and preference learning algorithms. The results show that SEA achieves highly sample-efficient alignment with oracle's preferences, outperforming recent active exploration methods for LLMs. The implementation of SEA is released, along with an efficient codebase for online alignment of LLMs to facilitate future research in this field.
categories: Formulation of LLM alignment problem within contextual dueling bandits, Incorporation of online active exploration, Unified algorithm based on Thompson sampling, Application of the algorithm in two distinct LLM alignment scenarios

title: As AI agents increasingly act on behalf of human stakeholders in economic settings, understanding their...
abstract: This research investigates AI agents' behavior in complex market environments, particularly focusing on Large Language Models (LLMs) in credence goods markets with information asymmetries and service providers seeking economic gain. Simulations using GPT-5.1 agents show that cooperation largely fails in one-shot settings, except under liability rules or with efficiency-loving preferences among experts. Repeated interactions increase consumer participation via competitive price reduction but do not eliminate expert fraud without explicit other-regarding preferences. Consumers focus on prices rather than strategic incentives, making them susceptible to exploitation. LLM markets exhibit higher consumer participation and lower prices compared to human experiments, but with more market concentration and polarized fraud patterns. Institutional design for AI agent markets may require distinct approaches, with social preference alignment emerging as a key determinant of market efficiency.

abstract: The study investigates the performance evaluation of Large Language Models (LLMs) across multiple European languages using translated versions of five benchmarks. 40 LLMs are assessed across 21 European languages. This research introduces a multilingual evaluation approach for LLMs, which includes newly created datasets: EU20-MMLU, EU20-HellaSwag, EU20-ARC, EU20-TruthfulQA, and EU20-GSM8K.
categories: The study employs translated versions of five widely-used benchmarks to assess the capabilities of LLMs., The effectiveness of translated benchmarks is examined., The impact of different translation services is assessed.

title: The post-training of LLMs, which typically consists of the supervised fine-tuning (SFT) stage and the...
abstract: The paper discusses the post-training process of Large Language Models (LLMs), which includes supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and preference learning stages such as RLHF or DPO. The widely used approach is to perform SFT followed by RLHF/DPO sequentially, but this method has a trade-off where the LLM gradually forgets the first stage's training during the second stage. The paper argues that this sequential paradigm is suboptimal due to its limitations despite its simplicity and modularity. A new joint post-training framework is proposed, which offers theoretical convergence guarantees, empirically outperforms sequential post-training with up to 23% overall performance improvement across multiple LLM evaluation benchmarks, and has minimal computational overhead. The authors propose a solution to the suboptimal sequential post-training approach of popular open-source LLMs by introducing a joint post-training framework.
categories: Sequential post-training: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by RLHF/DPO, Proposed joint post-training framework: The authors introduce a new method that addresses the suboptimalities of sequential post-training and offers theoretical convergence guarantees., Evaluation benchmarks: Multiple LLM evaluation benchmarks are used to test the performance of the proposed joint post-training framework.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to complex reasoning tasks that require executing several...
abstract: This research focuses on improving credit assignment in Large Language Models (LLMs) during Reinforcement Learning (RL) training. The authors question the effectiveness of value networks used in Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). They introduce VinePPO, a method that leverages the flexibility of language environments to compute unbiased Monte Carlo-based estimates. VinePPO outperforms PPO and other baselines across MATH and GSM8K datasets, demonstrating the importance of accurate credit assignment in RL training for LLMs.

title: This paper presents a novel methodology for generating synthetic Preference Optimization (PO) datasets using multi-model...
abstract: The paper introduces a novel methodology for creating synthetic Preference Optimization (PO) datasets using multi-model workflows. The aim is to automate and improve the dataset generation process. This involves two modules: response evaluation and response generation. In response evaluation, Large Language Models (LLMs) are used to evaluate and rank responses, a task usually done by human annotators. The study assesses the response evaluation module using three distinct prompting strategies. GPT-4o-as-a-Judge was found to be the most consistent across all datasets. For response generation, various configurations of the LLM Feedback Loop were compared, with the best configuration being the LLM Feedback Loop using Llama as the generator and Gemma as the reviewer, achieving a notable 71.8% and 73.8% win rate over single-model Llama and Gemma respectively. The final PO datasets were generated using this pipeline.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive zero-shot abilities in solving a wide range of...
abstract: This paper aims to improve the temporal awareness of large language models (LLMs) for sequential recommendation tasks. A prompting framework inspired by human cognitive processes is designed, which includes three strategies to exploit temporal information within historical interactions. Additionally, divergent thinking is emulated by aggregating LLM ranking results derived from these strategies. Evaluations on MovieLens-1M and Amazon Review datasets show significant enhancement of zero-shot capabilities for LLMs in sequential recommendation tasks.

abstract: This study investigates whether open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate commit messages that are comparable to those produced by the Omniscient Message Generator (OMG).

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) represent substantial intellectual and economic investments, yet their effectiveness can inadvertently...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of protecting Large Language Models (LLMs) from knowledge distillation (KD)-based imitation. The inadvertent effectiveness of LLMs can facilitate this imitation, allowing competitors to distill proprietary capabilities by observing publicly accessible outputs. This paper proposes a solution called Defensive Output Generation (DOGe), which modifies the output behavior of an LLM to be accurate and useful for legitimate users, yet misleading for distillation, thereby undermining imitation attempts. DOGe achieves this through fine-tuning only the final linear layer of the teacher LLM with an adversarial loss. The proposed strategy is effective and efficient, maintaining the performance of the teacher model while significantly degrading the performance of student models distilled from defensively generated outputs.
categories: Fine-tuning only the final linear layer of the teacher LLM with an adversarial loss., Modifying output behavior to be accurate and useful for legitimate users, yet misleading for distillation., Anticipating and disrupting distillation attempts during inference time.

title: Recent advancements in the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) show that employing group...

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as promising recommendation systems, offering novel ways to model...
abstract: This work introduces a novel approach to utilize Large Language Models (LLMs) for recommendation systems. The proposed method projects user and item embeddings learned from collaborative filtering into the LLM token space using separate lightweight projector modules. The finetuned LLM then generates recommendations by considering both these projected embeddings and textual tokens, aiming to leverage structured user-item interaction data for improved performance. Preliminary results indicate that this design enhances recommendation performance over text-only LLM baselines and provides a practical approach to merging traditional recommendation systems with modern LLMs.
categories: Learning user and item embeddings from collaborative filtering data, Projecting user and item embeddings into the LLM token space via separate lightweight projector modules, Fine-tuning an LLM to generate recommendations conditioned on both projected embeddings and textual tokens

title: Jailbreaking techniques trick Large Language Models (LLMs) into producing restricted output, posing a potential threat....
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel attack strategy called Emoji Attack, which exploits token segmentation bias in Large Language Models (LLMs) used as judges to evaluate the harmfulness of generated text. The strategy involves systematically inserting emojis into text before it is evaluated by a judge LLM, inducing embedding distortions that lower the likelihood of detecting unsafe content. This allows harmful content to be misclassified as safe, bypassing existing safeguards. Emojis are particularly effective due to their semantic ambiguity.

categories: The proposed Autonomous LLM-based Multi-Agent Software Engineering framework, ALMAS, aligns its agents with agile roles and can be used in a modular fashion to seamlessly integrate with human developers and their development environment. This suggests that the methods involve agent creation based on agile roles and flexible integration with existing development environments., The paper mentions the automation of code implementation, code testing, and code maintenance using LLM agents, indicating that these are some of the methods employed within ALMAS.

title: Speculative decoding is an inference-acceleration method for large language models (LLMs) where a small language...
abstract: The abstract discusses Recursive Speculative Decoding (RSD), a novel method for large language models (LLMs). RSD is a tree-based approach that aims to improve inference-acceleration by generating diverse draft tokens without replacement. It builds the tree using either Gumbel-Top-$k$ trick or Stochastic Beam Search, both designed to reduce computational cost of LLM. Empirical studies with Llama 2 and OPT models show that RSD outperforms baseline methods for fixed draft sequence length and often for fixed computational budgets at LLM, but existing works haven't been tested under fixed target computational budgets.

title: We study LLM judgments of misinformation expressed with uncertainty. Our experiments study the response of...
abstract: This study investigates the behavior of three widely used Language Model LMs (GPT-4o, LlaMA3, DeepSeek-v2) when faced with misinformation statements transformed into uncertain statements according to an uncertainty typology. The research found that after transformation, LLMs change their fact-checking classification from false to not-false in 25% of the cases, which cannot be explained by predictors typically sensitive to humans such as modality, linguistic cues, or argumentation strategy. The exception is doxastic transformations which use linguistic cue phrases like 'It is believed ...'. A further analysis was conducted to understand this phenomenon better by prompting the LLM to make another judgment about the transformed misinformation statements that is not related to truth value.
categories: Experiments were designed to study the response of three widely used LLMs (GPT-4o, LlaMA3, DeepSeek-v2) to misinformation propositions that have been verified false and then transformed into uncertain statements according to an uncertainty typology., The transformed misinformation statements were analyzed for changes in fact-checking classification., To gain further insight, the LLMs were prompted to make another judgment about the transformed misinformation statements that is not related to truth value, specifically estimating the frequency with which people make the uncertain statement.

abstract: The abstract introduces a novel approach that combines Variational Quantization with Conditional Flow Matching to convert audio into ultra-low bitrate discrete tokens for seamless integration with text tokens in large language models (LLMs). The goal is to achieve true multimodal capabilities, including audio comprehension and generation. The tokenizer outperforms a traditional VQ-VAE across various datasets with diverse acoustic events. The researchers fine-tuned a pretrained text-based LLM using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to assess its effectiveness.
categories: Variational Quantization and Conditional Flow Matching for converting audio into discrete tokens, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for fine-tuning a pretrained text-based LLM

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate summaries from clinical notes. However, their...
categories: The study introduces DistillNote, an evaluation framework for Large Language Model (LLM) summaries., DistillNote applies generated summaries downstream in a complex clinical prediction task to quantify retained prediction signal., Over 192,000 LLM summaries were generated from MIMIC-IV clinical notes with increasing compression rates: standard, section-wise, and distilled section-wise., Heart failure diagnosis was chosen as the prediction task., LLMs were fine-tuned on both the original notes and their summaries., Diagnostic performance was compared using the AUROC metric.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel in diverse applications including generation of code snippets, but often...
abstract: The abstract discusses a proposal for a cost-efficient Large Language Model (LLM) Multi-Agent based system to address the challenge of generating code for complex Machine Learning (ML) tasks. The new system leverages combination of experts using profiling, efficient retrieval of past observations, LLM cascades, and ask-the-expert calls.

title: This paper demonstrates RAGE, an interactive tool for explaining Large Language Models (LLMs) augmented with...
categories: The methods used by RAGE include the ability for the tool to query external sources and pull relevant information into its input context. It also includes pruning methods to navigate the vast space of possible explanations, allowing users to view the provenance of the produced answers.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in natural language tasks, yet understanding their...
abstract: The study introduces XplainLLM, a dataset and explanation framework designed to enhance the transparency and reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs). The dataset comprises 24,204 instances that interpret LLM's reasoning behavior using knowledge graphs and graph attention networks. It includes explanations for LLMs like Llama-3 and RoBERTa. XplainLLM features a framework for generating grounded explanations and debugger-scores for multidimensional quality analysis. The explanations include why-choose and why-not-choose components, reason-elements, and debugger-scores that shed light on the LLM's reasoning behavior. Evaluations suggest XplainLLM can reduce hallucinations and improve grounded explanation generation in LLMs, serving as a resource for researchers and practitioners to build trust and verify the reliability of LLM outputs.
categories: Knowledge graphs (KGs), Graph attention networks (GAT), Explanation framework, Generator for grounded explanations, Debugger-scores for multidimensional quality analysis

title: We present DESAMO, an on-device smart home system for elder-friendly use powered by Audio LLM,...
categories: DESAMO is an on-device smart home system for elder-friendly use powered by Audio LLM, which enables natural and private interactions., Conventional voice assistants rely on ASR-based pipelines or ASR-LLM cascades but struggle with unclear speech common among elderly users and cannot handle non-speech audio., DESAMO leverages an Audio LLM to process raw audio input directly, allowing for a robust understanding of user intent and critical events such as falls or calls for help.

title: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have expanded the horizons of natural language understanding...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel method called 'reflection-tuning' to improve Large Language Models (LLMs) by addressing the issue of low-quality data in training sets affecting instruction tuning. This approach uses an oracle LLM to recycle original training data, improving instructions and responses. Experiments on various benchmarks show that LLMs trained with recycled data outperform those trained with existing datasets.

abstract: The study introduces Robotouille, a challenging benchmark environment for testing Large Language Model (LLM) agents' ability to handle long-horizon asynchronous scenarios. The results show that ReAct (gpt4-o) struggles with asynchronous planning, achieving significantly lower performance (11%) compared to synchronous tasks (47%). This suggests a need for improvement in LLM agents, particularly in their handling of long-horizon feedback and self-auditing during task execution.

abstract: The paper discusses the issue of copyright infringement due to the untraceable nature of Large Language Models (LLMs) generated data. A method for injecting watermarks into LLM-generated datasets is proposed to track their usage and detect potential misuse.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated excellent capabilities in generating structured diagrams from natural language...
abstract: The abstract introduces MermaidSeqBench, a human-verified and LLM-synthetically-extended benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) capabilities in generating Mermaid sequence diagrams from textual prompts. This benchmark aims to fill the gap in systematic evaluations of LLMs' performance in this task due to the lack of existing benchmarks.
categories: The benchmark consists of a core set of 132 samples, starting from a small set of manually crafted and verified flows., These were expanded via a hybrid methodology combining human annotation, in-context LLM prompting, and rule-based variation generation., An LLM-as-a-judge model is used to assess Mermaid sequence diagram generation across fine-grained metrics such as syntax correctness, activation handling, error handling, and practical usability.

title: Automating code review with Large Language Models (LLMs) shows immense promise, yet practical adoption is...
abstract: The abstract presents 'Specification-Grounded Code Review (SGCR)', a framework to automate code review using Large Language Models (LLMs). The goal is to improve reliability and context-awareness, addressing practical adoption challenges. SGCR utilizes a dual-pathway architecture to ensure deterministic compliance with predefined rules derived from human-authored specifications while heuristically discovering and verifying additional issues. SGCR was tested in a live industrial environment at HiThink Research, demonstrating a 42% developer adoption rate and a 90.9% relative improvement over a baseline LLM (22%).
categories: SGCR framework with dual-pathway architecture:, - Explicit path: deterministic compliance with predefined rules derived from specifications., - Implicit path: heuristically discovering and verifying issues beyond those rules.

title: High-quality relevance judgements over large query sets are essential for evaluating Information Retrieval (IR) systems,...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel framework called Relevance Context Learning (RCL) to improve Information Retrieval (IR) systems by leveraging human relevance judgements to explicitly model topic-specific relevance criteria for Large Language Models (LLMs).
categories: RCL introduces two LLMs: Instructor and Assessor., The Instructor LLM is prompted to analyze sets of judged query-document pairs and generate explicit narratives that describe what constitutes relevance for a given topic., These relevance narratives are then used as structured prompts to guide the Assessor LLM in producing relevance judgements., A hybrid pooling strategy is proposed for realistic data collection, where a shallow depth-k pool from participating systems is judged by human assessors, while the remaining documents are labeled by LLMs., ],, gaps: [, Most existing approaches for automatic relevance assessment rely on zero-shot prompting or In-Context Learning (ICL) with a small number of labeled examples., Standard ICL treats examples as independent instances and fails to explicitly capture the underlying relevance criteria of a topic, restricting its ability to generalize to unseen query-document pairs., ],, evaluation: [, Experimental results demonstrate that RCL substantially outperforms zero-shot prompting and consistently improves over standard ICL., The findings indicate that transforming relevance examples into explicit, context-aware relevance narratives is a more effective way of exploiting human judgements for LLM-based IR dataset construction.
