title: Activation steering, or representation engineering, offers a lightweight approach to align large language models (LLMs)...
abstract: The abstract presents a new approach called ODESteer for alignment of large language models (LLMs) using a unified ordinary differential equations (ODEs)-based theoretical framework. The method aims to overcome the limitations of existing activation steering techniques, particularly the lack of a unified theoretical foundation and over-reliance on one-step steering. The proposed ODESteer identifies steering directions by defining the barrier function as the log-density ratio between positive and negative activations, and employs it to construct an ODE for multi-step and adaptive steering. Compared to state-of-the-art activation steering methods, ODESteer shows empirical advancement on diverse LLM alignment benchmarks with notable improvements over TruthfulQA, UltraFeedback, and RealToxicityPrompts.
categories: ODESteer: an ODE-based steering method guided by barrier functions derived from the proposed unified ODE theoretical framework, Defining the barrier function as the log-density ratio between positive and negative activations, Constructing an ODE for multi-step and adaptive steering, Utilizing the constructed ODE to manipulate internal activations of LLMs at inference time

title: Rapid advancements in large language models (LLMs) have the potential to assist in scientific progress....
abstract: The abstract introduces the Automated LLM Speedrunning Benchmark for evaluating AI agents' ability to reproduce scientific research results in an active research area, specifically focusing on improving Large Language Models (LLMs) training. The benchmark is based on the NanoGPT speedrun competition that aims to train a GPT-2 model in the shortest time possible. Each speedrun task provides the agent with previous records' training scripts and hints ranging from pseudocode to detailed descriptions of new record improvements. The benchmark is designed to be accessible and realistic for frontier problems, as it includes diverse code-level changes such as high-level algorithmic advancements and hardware-aware optimizations.
categories: Introducing the Automated LLM Speedrunning Benchmark: a competition that provides AI agents with previous records' training scripts and hints to reproduce results., Incorporating diverse code-level changes for training, including high-level algorithmic advancements and hardware-aware optimizations., Evaluating recent reasoning LLMs combined with SoTA scaffolds (state-of-the-art tools or frameworks) in reproducing known innovations from the benchmark.

title: Text clustering serves as a fundamental technique for organizing and interpreting unstructured textual data, particularly...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel framework for text clustering using Large Language Models (LLMs). The approach reframes text clustering as a classification task, eliminating the need for fine-tuning embedding models or complex clustering algorithms. The LLM generates candidate labels based on the dataset and merges semantically similar labels, then assigns the most appropriate label to each text sample.
categories: The framework consists of two main steps: first, the LLM generates a set of candidate labels; second, it assigns the most appropriate label to each text sample., The LLM leverages its in-context learning capabilities for this task.

title: LLMs are increasingly supporting decision-making across high-stakes domains, requiring critical reflection on the socio-technical factors...
The past few years has witnessed specialized large language model (LLM) inference systems, such as...
abstract: The abstract presents a review of techniques used in specialized large language model (LLM) inference systems to achieve high performance while preserving high inference quality over high-volume and high-velocity workloads. It covers topics like kernel design, batching, scheduling, paged memory, eviction and offloading techniques, quantization, cache persistence, and the combination of these techniques to form single-replica and multi-replica inference systems, including disaggregated and serverless systems. The review emphasizes that these techniques are crucial for overcoming challenges introduced by autoregressive generation and achieving the goals of the system. Lastly, it highlights remaining challenges.

title: We study program-learning methods that are efficient in both samples and computation. Classical learning theory...
abstract: The abstract presents a method called LLM-PV that allows for efficient program learning by avoiding exhaustive enumeration. The proposed approach utilizes a pretrained Language Model (LLM) to induce a proposal distribution over candidate programs, scores each proposal on a held-out validation set, and selects the best one without using gradient updates or adapting the sampling distribution. Across several algorithmic tasks such as parity variants, pattern matching, and primality testing, LLM-PV often recovers the exact underlying rule from a small labeled set and generalizes beyond training sequence lengths. Traditional ML baselines, SGD-trained transformers, and standard adaptation methods struggle to generalize reliably in the same settings.

title: The surge of LLM studies makes synthesizing their findings challenging. Analysis of experimental results from...
abstract: The research presents a semi-automated approach for analyzing experimental results from LLM studies, specifically focusing on arXiv papers. This approach automatically identifies relevant papers, extracts experimental results and related attributes, organizes them into a structured dataset named LLMEvalDB. The study then conducts an automated literature analysis of frontier LLMs using this dataset. Key findings include the benefits of in-context examples for coding & multimodal tasks but limited gains in math reasoning tasks compared to zero-shot Chain-of-Thought (CoT). The automatically updatable dataset enables continuous tracking of target models, extracting evaluation studies as new data becomes available.

title: Incorporating LLM into cybersecurity operations, a typical real-world high-stakes task, is critical but non-trivial in...
abstract: The study focuses on incorporating LLM into the vulnerability remediation process in cybersecurity operations. The goal is to promote an efficient LLM-supported collaborative vulnerability remediation process. Study 1 identifies deficiencies in user satisfaction within the existing process, leading to the design of an LLM-supported collaborative vulnerability remediation process (Study 2). Study 3 investigates LLM's double-edge roles through analysis and interviews. First-hand, real-world evidence suggests that facilitating collaborations among stakeholders, reshaping LLMs' roles according to task complexity, and addressing the short-term side effects of improved user engagement are crucial when incorporating LLMs into practical processes.
categories: Study 1: Deconstructing deficiencies in user satisfaction within the existing vulnerability remediation process, Study 2: Design, implementation, and empirical validation of an LLM-supported collaborative vulnerability remediation process through a field study, Study 3: Analysis of remediation reports and follow-up interviews to investigate LLM's double-edge roles

title: While Large Language Models (LLMs) possess significant capabilities in open-world agent tasks, they also face...

title: Meeting summarization has become a critical task since digital encounters have become a common practice....
abstract: The abstract discusses a multi-Large Language Model (LLM) correction approach for meeting summarization. This approach uses a two-phase process that mimics human review: mistake identification and summary refinement. The authors introduce QMSum Mistake, a dataset of 200 automatically generated meeting summaries annotated by humans on nine error types. These errors include structural, omission, and irrelevance errors. Experiments show that these errors can be identified with high accuracy by an LLM. Identified mistakes are transformed into actionable feedback to improve the quality of a given summary measured by relevance, informativeness, conciseness, and coherence. This post-hoc refinement improves summary quality effectively using multiple LLMs for output quality validation.

title: The rapid evolution of Large Language Model (LLM) inference systems has yielded significant efficiency improvements....
categories: Comprehensive examination of recent Large Language Model (LLM) inference systems to identify recurring anti-patterns across three key dimensions: Baseline Fairness, Evaluation Setup, and Metric Design., Provision of a comprehensive checklist derived from the analysis, establishing a framework for recognizing and avoiding these anti-patterns in favor of robust LLM inference evaluation.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved widespread adoption across numerous applications. However, many LLMs are...
abstract: The abstract discusses SecurityLingua, an approach to defend large language models (LLMs) against jailbreak attacks. It involves training a prompt compressor to discern the 'true intention' of input prompts, with a focus on detecting malicious intentions in adversarial prompts. The intention is passed via the system prompt to the target LLM to help it identify the true intention of the request. SecurityLingua ensures a consistent user experience by leaving the original input prompt intact while revealing potential malicious intentions and stimulating the built-in safety guardrails of the LLM. The proposed method incurs negligible overhead and extra token cost compared to existing defense methods, making it a practical solution for LLM defense.
categories: Training a prompt compressor to discern the 'true intention' of input prompts, Focusing on detecting malicious intentions in adversarial prompts, Passing the intention via the system prompt to the target LLM, Leaving the original input prompt intact while revealing potential malicious intentions

title: Mainstream LLM research has primarily focused on enhancing their generative capabilities. However, even the most...
Device-side Large Language Models (LLMs) have witnessed explosive growth, offering higher privacy and availability compared...
abstract: The paper presents FlexServe, a fast and secure Language Model (LLM) serving system designed for mobile devices. It aims to address the challenges posed by device-side LLMs, particularly the significant overhead incurred when protecting inference using ARM TrustZone due to its inflexible memory isolation and NPU. To achieve this, FlexServe introduces a Flexible Resource Isolation mechanism that allows efficient switching between unprotected and protected modes for memory pages and the NPU. The framework includes LLM-Aware Memory Management and a Secure Inference Pipeline to accelerate inference. A Multi-Model Scheduler is proposed to optimize multi-model workflows. The results demonstrate that FlexServe achieves an average speedup of $10.05	imes$ in Time to First Token (TTFT) compared to a strawman, and an average speedup of $2.44	imes$ TTFT compared to an optimized strawman with pipeline and secure NPU enabled. For multi-model agent workflows, the end-to-end speedup is up to $24.30	imes$ and $4.05	imes$ compared to the strawman and optimized strawman, respectively.
categories: Flexible Resource Isolation mechanism to construct Flexible Secure Memory (Flex-Mem) and Flexible Secure NPU (Flex-NPU), LLM-Aware Memory Management, Secure Inference Pipeline, Multi-Model Scheduler

title: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed communication, yet their role in secure...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel cryptographic embedding framework designed for covert Public Key or Symmetric Key encrypted communication over public chat channels using Large Language Models (LLMs). The framework aims to address the lack of secure messaging in surveillance-heavy environments and the potential legislation against encrypted communication. The unique properties of this framework include its LLM agnosticism, pre- or post-quantum agnosticism, and ability to generate humanlike chat texts, making it an alternative where traditional encryption is detectable and restricted.
categories: The method involves embedding the encrypted communication within humanlike produced texts using LLMs. Participants can use different local LLM models independently., The framework is pre- or post-quantum agnostic, ensuring compatibility with future advancements in quantum computing.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have spurred development in multiple industries. However, the growing number of...
Large Language Models (LLMs) have gained popularity in time series forecasting, but their potential for...
abstract: This study investigates the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) in time series anomaly detection. The research reveals several unexpected findings about LLMs' behavior in time series analysis, such as their understanding of time series data as images rather than text, lack of enhanced performance with explicit reasoning prompts, no evident relation to repetition biases or arithmetic abilities, and significant variations in behavior across different models. The investigation suggests that while LLMs can identify trivial time series anomalies, there is no evidence they can understand more subtle real-world anomalies.
categories: Investigation of LLM capabilities in time series anomaly detection, Formulation of key hypotheses based on conjectures from time series forecasting research, Design and conduct of principled experiments to test each hypothesis, Evaluation of LLMs' behaviors and performance in time series analysis

title: Large Language Model (LLM) libraries have emerged as the foundational infrastructure powering today's AI revolution,...
abstract: The abstract discusses an empirical investigation into bug characteristics and testing practices in modern Large Language Model (LLM) libraries, specifically focusing on HuggingFace Transformers and vLLM. The study categorizes bug symptoms into 5 types and root causes into 14 distinct categories, with API misuse emerging as the predominant root cause. The research examines 7,748 test functions to identify 7 distinct test oracle categories employed in current testing approaches.
categories: Manual analysis of 313 bug-fixing commits across HuggingFace Transformers and vLLM., Establishment of comprehensive taxonomies for categorizing bug symptoms and root causes.

title: As healthcare increasingly turns to AI for scalable and trustworthy clinical decision support, ensuring reliability...
abstract: The text discusses the challenge of ensuring reliability in AI models used for clinical decision support. It mentions that individual large language models are susceptible to hallucinations and inconsistency, while naive ensembles often fail to deliver stable and credible recommendations. To address this issue, a new approach is proposed based on a framework called LLM Chemistry, which improves the reliability in medication recommendation from brief clinical vignettes by leveraging multi-LLM collaboration guided by Chemistry-inspired interaction modeling. Preliminary results suggest that the Chemistry-based Multi-LLM collaboration strategy is effective, stable, and calibrated, and may offer a promising path towards reliable and trustworthy AI assistants in clinical practice.

title: Countless decisions shape our daily lives, and it is paramount to understand the how and...
abstract: The paper introduces STRUX, a new LLM decision-making framework that enhances the process by providing structured explanations for choices. It distills lengthy information into a concise table of key facts, determines pivotal facts, categorizes them as either favorable or adverse, and fine-tunes an LLM to optimize decision-making. Evaluated on forecasting stock investment decisions, STRUX demonstrated superior performance against strong baselines. It enhances decision transparency by allowing users to understand the impact of different factors, marking a meaningful step towards practical decision-making with LLMs.

categories: Proposal of a Model Context Protocol (MCP)-based Internet of Experts (IoX) framework for equipping LLMs with wireless environment-aware reasoning capabilities., Incorporation of a set of lightweight expert models, each trained to solve specific deterministic tasks in wireless communications, such as detecting wireless attributes like line-of-sight propagation, Doppler effects, or fading conditions., Utilization of MCP for the LLM to selectively query and interpret expert outputs at inference time without modifying its own parameters.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly persuasive, demonstrating the ability to personalize arguments in...
abstract: The study investigates the persuasive capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in a debate setting, particularly their ability to influence human opinions using personal data and various strategies such as personalized arguments, appeal to fabricated statistics, and a mixed strategy. The research found that LLMs, when utilizing the mixed strategy, were more effective than static human-written arguments, with a 51% chance of persuading participants compared to 32%. This suggests a potential for LLMs to facilitate inexpensive and persuasive large-scale disinformation campaigns.

title: The proliferation of open-sourced Large Language Models (LLMs) and diverse downstream tasks necessitates efficient model...
abstract: The study focuses on the efficient selection of Large Language Models (LLMs) for diverse downstream tasks, given computational constraints. The research question is to model dynamic behaviors of LLMs during fine-tuning to enhance understanding of their generalization performance across various tasks. A novel theoretical framework is proposed to assess the generalization capabilities of LLMs, providing an accurate and efficient method for LLM selection. The proposed framework includes deriving a PAC-Bayesian Generalization Bound to reveal fine-tuning dynamics of LLMs and introducing LENSLLM, a Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK)-based Rectified Scaling Model that offers accurate performance predictions across various tasks while maintaining computational efficiency.
categories: Deriving a PAC-Bayesian Generalization Bound, Introducing LENSLLM, a Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK)-based Rectified Scaling Model

title: Large language model (LLM) providers often hide the architectural details and parameters of their proprietary...
categories: Investigating the softmax bottleneck in most modern LLMs to unlock capabilities such as obtaining cheap full-vocabulary outputs., Auditing for specific types of model updates., Identifying the source LLM given a single full LLM output., Efficiently discovering the LLM's hidden size., Estimating the embedding size of OpenAI's gpt-3.5-turbo through empirical investigations.

title: We present a mixed-methods study to explore how large language models (LLMs) can assist users...
abstract: The study investigates how large language models can aid in the exploration and analysis of knowledge graphs. Participants expressed a desire for an LLM to facilitate data retrieval, identify interesting relationships, create visualizations, and maintain semantic intent. However, concerns were raised about the LLM's ability to translate natural language questions into KG queries, the risk of false data 'hallucination', and the challenges of engineering a perfect prompt. The research contributes a preliminary roadmap for designing LLM-driven knowledge graph exploration systems and outlines future opportunities in this emerging design space.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being integrated into various applications and services running on...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) on resource-limited mobile devices due to high computation, memory, and energy demands. It mentions that current LLM frameworks for mobile use power-hungry components - CPU, GPU, and Memory - even when running primarily GPU LLM models. The authors observe that modern mobile devices' DVFS governors for CPU, GPU, and memory operate independently and ineffectively, leading to energy inefficiency in LLM inference. The study measures the energy-efficiency of a SOTA LLM framework on mobile phones, finding up to 40.4% longer prefilling and decoding latency with triplet mobile governors compared to optimal combinations. An in-depth measurement study is conducted to understand the interplay between mobile governors causing the inefficiency in LLM inference.
categories: Measuring the energy-efficiency of a SOTA LLM framework consisting of various LLM models on mobile phones., Comparing the energy consumption of triplet mobile governors with optimal combinations of CPU, GPU, and memory frequencies for sampled prefill and decode lengths., Conducting an in-depth measurement study to uncover how the intricate interplay among the mobile governors cause inefficiency in LLM inference.

title: Enhancing the capability of large language models (LLMs) in reasoning has gained significant attention in...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development of DOTS, an approach to enhance large language models (LLMs) in dynamic reasoning. The goal is to enable LLMs to adapt their reasoning strategies based on the specific characteristics of each question and the capabilities of the task-solving LLM. This is achieved through three key steps: defining atomic reasoning action modules, searching for the optimal action trajectory for each training question, and using collected optimal trajectories to train an LLM for reasoning action planning.
categories: Defining atomic reasoning action modules that can be composed into various reasoning action trajectories., Searching for the optimal action trajectory for each training question through iterative exploration and evaluation., Using the collected optimal trajectories to train an LLM to plan for the reasoning trajectories of unseen questions., Proposing two learning paradigms: fine-tuning an external LLM as a planner to guide the task-solving LLM, or directly fine-tuning the task-solving LLM with an internalized capability for reasoning actions planning.

title: Code generation with Large Language Models (LLMs) has helped to increase software developer productivity in...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation to increase software developer productivity, but highlights that their impact on tasks outside of coding, particularly infrastructure management, is minimal. The study investigates the potential of an LLM agent to construct infrastructure using the Infrastructure as Code (IaC) paradigm, with a focus on a feedback loop for error and warning returns to improve the generated IaC code. The research finds that the effectiveness of the feedback loop decreases exponentially with each iteration until it reaches a plateau where it becomes ineffective.
categories: Use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for code generation, Infrastructure as Code (IaC) paradigm, Implementation of a feedback loop to return errors and warnings on the generated IaC, Iterative testing of the LLM agent's ability to improve the code using the feedback loop

title: Recent work has investigated the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) as zero-shot models for...
abstract: The study investigates the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) as zero-shot models for individual-level characteristics tasks such as risk modeling and survey dataset augmentation. The research examines LLMs' performance variability across a wide range of tabular prediction tasks. It is found that when an LLM performs well on the base prediction task, its predicted probabilities become a stronger signal for individual-level accuracy.
categories: Conduct a large-scale empirical study of LLMs' zero-shot predictive capabilities across various tabular prediction tasks., Assess the LLMs' performance on each task and analyze the relationship between base prediction task performance and individual-level accuracy.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel in diverse tasks such as text generation, data analysis, and...
abstract: This study examines the issue of identity confusion among Large Language Models (LLMs), revealing that 25.93% of 27 analyzed LLMs exhibit this problem. The analysis suggests that identity confusion arises from hallucinations rather than replication or reuse, and it significantly erodes user trust, particularly in critical tasks like education and professional use. Users attributed these failures to design flaws, incorrect training data, and perceived plagiarism.

title: Zero-shot text rankers powered by recent LLMs achieve remarkable ranking performance by simply prompting. Existing...
abstract: Zero-shot text rankers powered by recent LLMs achieve remarkable ranking performance by simply prompting. The paper proposes to incorporate fine-grained relevance labels into the prompt for LLM rankers, enabling them to better differentiate among documents with different levels of relevance to a query and thus derive a more accurate ranking. Experiments on 8 BEIR data sets show that adding fine-grained relevance labels significantly improves the performance of LLM rankers.

title: Data-to-text (D2T) generation aims to generate human-readable text from semi-structured data, such as tables and...
abstract: This study investigates the impact of model size on the performance of fine-tuned Language Model (LLM) for Data-to-Text (D2T) tasks. The research evaluates the performance across five datasets and twelve LLMs, assessing readability, informativeness, and faithfulness, using six automatic metrics. It also explores LLM performance concerning source-reference divergence.
categories: Comparative analysis of fifteen (5) D2T datasets and twelve state-of-the-art LLMs from five different LLM families (T5, BART, OPT, BLOOM, and Llama 2). The study incorporates six widely recognized automatic metrics -- 	extsc{BLEU}, 	extsc{METEOR}, 	extsc{BERTScore}, 	extsc{MoverScore}, 	extsc{Parent}, and 	extsc{BARTScore}.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance, and organizations are racing to serve LLMs...
abstract: The paper presents MuxServe, a spatial-temporal multiplexing system designed for efficient serving of multiple large language models (LLMs). The system aims to address the challenges posed by varying popularity of LLMs in organizations, where these models are used as endpoints for chat, programming, and search applications. MuxServe's key insight is to colocate LLMs based on their popularity to multiplex memory resources. It also leverages the characteristics of prefill and decoding phases to separate and flexibly colocate computations for improved efficiency. The paper formalizes the multiplexing problem, proposes a novel placement algorithm, and adaptive batch scheduling strategy to identify optimal colocations and maximize utilization.
categories: Formal formulation of the multiplexing problem, Proposal of a novel placement algorithm, Adaptive batch scheduling strategy, Design of a unified resource manager for flexible and efficient multiplexing

abstract: The abstract discusses a novel approach, AI System Optimization via Multiple LLM Evaluators (AIME), for optimizing text-based AI systems, particularly in complex tasks like code generation. The proposed method utilizes multiple Language Model Models (LLMs) to generate independent evaluations on separate criteria and combines them. Empirical results show AIME outperforming single-LLM methods with higher error detection rates and success rates on LeetCodeHard and HumanEval datasets.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to annotate learning interactions, yet concerns about reliability...
categories: Large Language Models (LLMs) were used to annotate learning interactions. The study tested verification-oriented orchestration-prompting models for self-verification and cross-verification. Transcripts from 30 one-to-one math sessions were used. Three production LLMs were compared: GPT, Claude, and Gemini. The outputs were compared with a blinded, disagreement-focused human adjudication using Cohen's kappa., The study employed a flexible orchestration framework that instantiates control, self-, and cross-verification. A concise notation, verifier(annotator) (e.g., Gemini(GPT) or Claude(Claude)), was used to standardize reporting and make directional effects explicit for replication.

title: This paper investigates integrating large language models (LLMs) with advanced hardware, focusing on developing a...
abstract: The abstract discusses the integration of large language models (LLMs) with advanced hardware, with a focus on developing a versatile device for enhanced LLM interaction. It outlines the impact of virtual assistants and LLMs in reshaping human-technology interactions, emphasizing key advancements and heralding a new era of intelligent hardware. The current landscape is evaluated, revealing substantial progress in LLM technology but also highlighting a gap in hardware development, especially concerning scalability, efficiency, affordability, and multimodal capabilities.
categories: Proposing a device that addresses the identified needs, emphasizing scalability, multimodal data processing, enhanced user interaction, and privacy considerations.

title: Graphs are foundational across domains but remain hard to use without deep expertise. LLMs promise...
categories: The novel abstraction for complex multi-query analytics over such graphs involves planning over a Semantic Catalog that describes both the graph schema and the graph operations. The design includes a separation between a Semantic Plane for LLM planning and broader reasoning, and an Execution Plane for deterministic, database-grade query execution over the full dataset and tool implementations. This abstraction is used as the basis of the first LLM-enhanced graph analytics framework called GraphSeek.

title: This paper presents a novel approach to enhance autonomous robotic manipulation using the Large Language...
categories: Utilization of Large Language Model (LLM) for logical inference to convert high-level language commands into sequences of executable motion functions., Combination of LLM with YOLO-based environmental perception., Employment of teleoperation for action correction., Application of Dynamic Movement Primitives (DMP) for action correction., The integration of these methods is aimed to improve the practicality and generalizability of the LLM-based human-robot collaboration system.

title: We explore the use of Large Language Model (LLM-based) chatbots to power recommender systems. We...
abstract: The study investigates the use of Large Language Model (LLM)-based chatbots in powering recommender systems. It reveals that these chatbots perform poorly when faced with under-specified requests, often making incorrect assumptions, providing long responses, or refusing to answer. This is hypothesized to be due to LLM fine-tuning using annotators who may not capture multi-turn conversation utility or fully represent users interacting with a recommender system. The research first analyzes public LLM chat logs to confirm the prevalence of query under-specification. It then delves into synthetic recommendation problems, formulated as Partially Observed Decision Processes (PODP). Findings indicate that pre-trained LLMs can be sub-optimal for PODPs and propose better policies to clarify under-specified queries when necessary. The study proceeds by re-calibrating LLMs with learned control messages to approximate the improved policy. Empirical evidence shows that the proposed lightweight learning approach effectively uses logged conversation data to re-calibrate the response strategies of LLM-based chatbots for recommendation tasks.

title: Polite speech poses a fundamental alignment challenge for large language models (LLMs). Humans deploy a...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of polite speech in large language models (LLMs). It reveals that larger models (>=70B parameters) replicate human-like politeness strategies from the computational pragmatics literature. Surprisingly, human evaluators prefer LLM-generated responses in open-ended contexts. However, the analysis uncovers that models excessively rely on negative politeness strategies even in positive contexts, potentially causing misunderstandings. The study concludes with a question about pragmatic alignment in AI systems. Polite speech and its alignment challenge for large language models Comparison of human and LLM responses in both constrained and open-ended production tasks Findings suggest that larger models replicate key preferences from the computational pragmatics literature, human evaluators prefer LLM-generated responses, and models disproportionately rely on negative politeness strategies. Important questions about pragmatic alignment in AI systems
categories: Comparison of human and LLM responses, Investigation of context-sensitive repertoire of linguistic strategies, Constrained and open-ended production tasks, Linguistic analyses to reveal preferences and strategies used by models

title: The scaling of transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) has significantly expanded their context lengths, enabling...
abstract: The proposed PecSched system outperforms existing scheduling strategies for Large Language Models (LLMs) by reducing the 99th percentile queueing delay of short-input requests by up to 92% and improving their throughput by up to 595%. Notably, this improvement is achieved without significantly affecting the Job Completion Time (JCT) of long-input requests. This suggests that PecSched addresses the inefficiencies caused by the skewed distribution of input lengths, such as head-of-line blocking, resource underutilization, and starvation of long-input requests, which are common challenges with existing scheduling approaches.

title: Large Language Model (LLM) agents show considerable promise for automating complex tasks using contextual reasoning;...
abstract: The abstract discusses the potential of Large Language Model (LLM) agents in automating complex tasks but highlights concerns about privacy leakage and system exploitation due to multiple agent interactions and prompt injection vulnerabilities. It argues for the application of established information security principles, such as defense-in-depth, least privilege, complete mediation, and psychological acceptability, during the deployment of LLM agents at scale. The introduction of AgentSandbox, a conceptual framework that embeds these security principles throughout an agent's life-cycle is proposed. The paper evaluates AgentSandbox with state-of-the-art LLMs across three dimensions: benign utility, attack utility, and attack success rate. It is asserted that AgentSandbox maintains high utility while substantially mitigating privacy risks.
categories: Proposing the application of established information security principles in deploying LLM agents, Introducing AgentSandbox, a conceptual framework embedding these security principles, Evaluating AgentSandbox with state-of-the-art LLMs across three dimensions: benign utility, attack utility, and attack success rate

title: Spatial-temporal sequence forecasting (STSF) is a long-standing research problem with widespread real-world applications. Neural architecture...
abstract: This paper proposes a novel neural architecture search (NAS) method for spatial-temporal sequence forecasting (STSF). The proposed method utilizes large language models (LLMs) to automate the network design process. Instead of directly generating architectures, the paper introduces a multi-level enhancement mechanism. On the step-level, LLM is inspired to serve as an instructor for architecture search based on its internal knowledge. On the instance-level, a one-step tuning framework and memory bank are used to evaluate and improve LLM's search ability. On the task-level, a two-stage architecture search is proposed to balance exploration and optimization stages. The method demonstrates competitive effectiveness with superior efficiency compared to existing NAS methods for STSF.

title: Test set contamination, wherein test data from a benchmark ends up in a newer model's...
abstract: The abstract introduces LiveBench, a new benchmark for Large Language Models (LLMs). It aims to mitigate test set contamination and pitfalls of LLM judging and human crowdsourcing. The benchmark updates frequently with questions from recent information sources such as math competitions, arXiv papers, news articles, and datasets. It scores answers automatically according to objective ground-truth values and contains a wide variety of challenging tasks spanning math, coding, reasoning, language, instruction following, and data analysis.
categories: LiveBench consists of questions based on recently-released resources like math competitions, arXiv papers, news articles, and datasets., It includes harder, contamination-limited versions of tasks from previous benchmarks such as Big-Bench Hard, AMPS, and IFEval.

title: This paper introduces a new methodology for using LLM-based systems for accurate and efficient semantic...
categories: The paper presents a new methodology for using LLM-based systems for accurate and efficient semantic tagging of UN Security Council resolutions. The main goal is to leverage LLM performance variability to build ensemble systems for data cleaning and semantic tagging tasks. This involves running multiple GPT models, selecting the best output based on the introduced evaluation metrics (CPR and TWF), and combining these outputs to create a reliable LLM system for performing semantic tagging on challenging texts.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are playing an increasingly important role in physics research by assisting...
As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as agents, their integration into interactive environments...
abstract: This paper introduces Agent-SafetyBench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the safety of large language model (LLM) agents. The benchmark includes 349 interaction environments, 2,000 test cases, and covers 8 categories of safety risks across 10 common failure modes. Evaluation of 16 popular LLM agents reveals a significant concern: none of the agents achieve a safety score above 60%. The study highlights two fundamental safety defects in current LLM agents: lack of robustness and lack of risk awareness, suggesting that relying on defense prompts alone may be insufficient. To promote further research in agent safety evaluation and improvement, Agent-SafetyBench has been released at https://github.com/thu-coai/Agent-SafetyBench.

title: We present that hierarchical LLM reasoning via scaling thought templates can effectively optimize the reasoning...
Closed large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 have set state-of-the-art results across a number...

title: Broad adoption of Large Language Models (LLM) demands rapid expansions of cloud LLM inference clusters,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the increasing carbon emissions due to the expansion of Large Language Model (LLM) inference clusters on cloud servers, particularly focusing on CPU emissions. The paper proposes a solution to extend the amortization of CPU embodied over an increased lifespan by introducing an aging-aware CPU core management technique. This technique delays CPU aging effects, allowing for safer increases in CPU life, and improves performance by exploiting underutilization patterns in cloud LLM inference. The technique involves halting aging in unused cores and even-outing aging in active cores via selective deep idling and aging-aware inference task allocation. The research demonstrates superior performance of this technique over existing methods, estimating a 37.67% reduction in yearly embodied carbon emissions through maintaining p99 performance. Additionally, the method reduces CPU underutilization by 77% and has less than a 10% impact on the inference service quality.
categories: Aging-aware CPU core management technique, Halting aging in unused cores, Even-outing aging in active cores via selective deep idling, Aging-aware inference task allocation

title: Scientific publishing lays the foundation of science by disseminating research findings, fostering collaboration, encouraging reproducibility,...
categories: The methods used for this study include a systematic analysis across multiple scientific journals over a specific time period (January 2020 - February 2024) on the arXiv, bioRxiv, and Nature portfolio journals. The analysis is based on a population-level statistical framework to measure the prevalence of LLM modified content. This statistical estimation operates at the corpus level, making it more robust than inference on individual instances.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities as autonomous agents through tool use, planning,...
abstract: The abstract discusses the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in multi-agent systems for problem-solving collaboratively. It highlights that while these systems are widely adopted, their safety and security remain under-explored, especially in multi-agent settings. The introduction of TAMAS, a benchmark for evaluating the robustness and safety of multi-agent LLM systems, is presented. This benchmark consists of five scenarios with 300 adversarial instances across six attack types and 211 tools, along with 100 harmless tasks. The study assesses system performance across ten backbone LLMs and three agent interaction configurations from Autogen and CrewAI frameworks.
categories: Introducing TAMAS benchmark for evaluating robustness and safety of multi-agent LLM systems., Including five distinct scenarios, 300 adversarial instances, six attack types, 211 tools, and 100 harmless tasks., Assessing system performance across ten backbone LLMs and three agent interaction configurations from Autogen and CrewAI frameworks., Introducing Effective Robustness Score (ERS) to assess the tradeoff between safety and task effectiveness of these frameworks.

title: End users face a choice between privacy and efficiency in current Large Language Model (LLM)...
abstract: The abstract proposes a novel Large Language Model (LLM) service paradigm that balances privacy and efficiency by distributing computation between edge devices and the cloud. The core innovation, PrivateLoRA, reduces communication overhead by exploiting the low rank of residual activations, resulting in significant data locality maintenance and resource efficiency. Under standard 5G networks, PrivateLoRA delivers impressive performance gains over device-only solutions for 7B models and substantial GPU-performance approximations for 33B models. The approach aims to democratize access to state-of-the-art generative AI for edge devices, paving the way for more tailored LLM experiences for the general public.
categories: Distributing privacy-sensitive computation on edge devices and shared computation in the cloud., Transmitting only activations between the central cloud and edge devices to ensure data locality., Exploiting the low rank of residual activations to address communication overhead and achieve over 95% communication reduction.

title: Despite their general capabilities, LLMs still struggle on biomedical NER tasks, which are difficult due...
abstract: The abstract discusses an approach to improve Large Language Models (LLMs) performance on Biomedical Named Entity Recognition (NER) tasks, particularly in limited data settings. The proposed method involves knowledge augmentation by incorporating definitions of relevant concepts on-the-fly.
categories: Development of a new knowledge augmentation approach, Performing a comprehensive exploration of prompting strategies for providing a test bed, Evaluation of the performance of both open source and closed LLMs, Conducting experiments to demonstrate the usefulness of definition augmentation, Investigating the impact of careful prompting strategies on LLM performance

title: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have shown impressive progress in mathematical reasoning tasks....
abstract: The abstract discusses the limitations of current large language models in mathematical problem solving, specifically focusing on their inability to solve Olympiad-level problems. The research shows that LLMs often provide correct answers due to pattern recognition or heuristic shortcuts instead of genuine mathematical reasoning. It highlights the need for developing evaluation benchmarks that prioritize the soundness of reasoning over the mere correctness of final answers.

title: The rapid progress in machine learning (ML) has brought forth many large language models (LLMs)...
abstract: The paper presents MetaLLM, a framework that optimizes the selection of large language models (LLMs) for classification and multi-choice question-answering tasks. By treating the selection problem as a multi-armed bandit, MetaLLM balances prediction accuracy and cost efficiency under uncertainty. Experiments on OpenAI, Together AI, and open-source LLMs demonstrate its effectiveness in real-world scenarios, paving the way for future extensions.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as proxies for human judgment in computational social...
Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in decision-making scenarios that involve risk assessment, yet...
abstract: This study investigates the alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) with human economic rationality in decision-making scenarios that involve risk assessment. The research focuses on examining if LLM-generated responses reflect appropriate levels of risk aversion or risk-seeking behavior based on individual personas. The findings reveal that while LLMs make reasonable decisions in simplified, personalized risk contexts, their performance declines in more complex economic decision-making tasks.
categories: Assessment of LLM responses for appropriate levels of risk aversion or risk-seeking behavior based on individual personas., Analysis of LLMs in simplified, personalized risk contexts and more complex economic decision-making tasks.

title: Extending large language models (LLMs) to the speech domain has recently gained significant attention. A...
abstract: The abstract discusses the development of AZeroS (Auden Zero-instruction-tuned Speech-LLM), a speech language model that achieves strong generalization by training with Self-Generated Instruction-Free Tuning (SIFT). SIFT generates supervision signals using a frozen LLM and textual representations of speech as input, eliminating the need for task-specific question-answer pairs. AZeroS is trained on speech-text pairs derived from publicly available corpora containing around 25,000 hours of speech with ASR transcripts and 3,000 hours of speech with paralinguistic labels. The model updates only two lightweight projection modules while keeping both the LLM and audio encoders frozen. AZeroS achieves state-of-the-art performance on various semantic and paralinguistic benchmarks.
categories: Training a speech language model using Self-Generated Instruction-Free Tuning (SIFT)., SIFT generates supervision signals from a frozen LLM using textual representations of speech as input., AZeroS is trained on speech-text pairs derived from publicly available corpora., The model updates only two lightweight projection modules (23.8 million parameters each)., Both the LLM and audio encoders remain frozen during training.

title: With the rapid development of evaluation datasets to assess LLMs understanding across a wide range...
abstract: The paper discusses the challenges in identifying a suitable language understanding benchmark for Low-Resource Languages (LRL) given the rapid development of evaluation datasets for Language Learning Models (LLMs). The authors introduce 	extbf{	extit{ProverbEval}}, an LLM evaluation benchmark specifically designed for LRL focusing on culture-specific scenarios. They benchmark various LLMs and explore factors affecting the benchmarking process, observing significant performance variations. Factors include the order of answer choices in multiple-choice tasks and native language proverbs improving tasks like proverb generation. Monolingual evaluations outperformed cross-lingual counterparts in generation tasks. The work highlights the importance of considering the order of choices, choice of prompt language, task variability, and generation tasks when creating LLM evaluation benchmarks.
categories: Introduce a new LLM evaluation benchmark named 	extbf{	extit{ProverbEval}} for LRL., Benchmark various LLMs using the introduced benchmark., Explore factors affecting the benchmarking process, including the order of answer choices in multiple-choice tasks and choice of prompt language.

title: Sea surface temperature (SST) prediction is a critical task in ocean science, supporting various applications,...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel framework for Sea Surface Temperature (SST) prediction called Ocean Knowledge Graph-enhanced Large Language Model (OKG-LLM). This framework aims to advance SST prediction accuracy by integrating domain knowledge and numerical data, an aspect that existing data-driven methods often overlook. The work presents the first systematic effort to construct an Ocean Knowledge Graph (OKG) specifically designed for SST prediction. The OKG is developed to represent diverse ocean knowledge, with a graph embedding network used to learn the comprehensive semantic and structural knowledge within it. The learned knowledge is then aligned and fused with fine-grained numerical SST data. A pre-trained LLM is leveraged to model SST patterns for accurate prediction.
categories: Construction of an Ocean Knowledge Graph (OKG) specifically designed for Sea Surface Temperature (SST) prediction., Development of a graph embedding network to learn the comprehensive semantic and structural knowledge within the OKG., Alignment and fusion of the learned knowledge with fine-grained numerical SST data., Leveraging a pre-trained Large Language Model (LLM) to model SST patterns for accurate prediction.

title: Improving customer service quality and response time are critical factors for maintaining customer loyalty and...
abstract: The paper proposes a multi-agent system to handle customer requests using LLM based agents integrated with fuzzy logic. This system aims to address the risk of hallucination in LLMs, which is a major challenge in maintaining customer loyalty and increasing market share. The proposed system could potentially improve customer service quality by providing faster responses while mitigating risks associated with LLMs.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are rapidly becoming integral to a wide range of tools, tasks,...
In recent years, large language models (LLMs) have become incredibly popular, with ChatGPT for example...
abstract: The abstract discusses the challenge of 'hallucinations' in large language models (LLMs), such as ChatGPT, which outputs misinformation in a confident manner despite its impressive language understanding and logical prowess. The authors question the use of the term 'hallucination' and propose a psychological taxonomy based on cognitive biases and other psychological phenomena for a more precise understanding.
categories: Proposing a psychological taxonomy based on cognitive biases and other psychological phenomena

title: Large language models (LLMs) have driven significant progress across a wide range of real-world applications....
abstract: This research investigates the challenges faced by deep learning (DL) frameworks in supporting Large Language Models (LLMs). The study analyzes issue reports from three major DL frameworks and eight associated LLM toolkits, constructing a taxonomy of LLM-centric framework bugs, user requirements, and user questions. The research further reveals key technical challenges and mismatches between LLM user needs and developer priorities. The study found that the large parameter scale and extended execution cycles of LLMs impose exacting demands on DL frameworks, particularly in terms of scalability, stability, and efficiency.
categories: Manual review of issue reports from three major DL frameworks (MindSpore, PyTorch, and TensorFlow) and eight associated LLM toolkits such as Megatron., Interviews with 11 LLM users and eight DL framework developers to refine and enrich the taxonomy.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) have gained significant attention in on-device applications due to their remarkable...
abstract: This work presents 'P3Defer', a Chain-of-Thought (CoT)-enhanced policy learning framework designed for privacy-preserved deferral decision-making in the cascading of on-device and server LLMs. The aim is to improve cascade efficiency while minimizing privacy risks. The authors perform extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness and superiority of their proposed approach over existing methods.

title: Large language models (LLMs) achieve impressive results over various tasks, and ever-expanding public repositories contain...
abstract: The abstract discusses an efficient, training-free approach to representing large language models (LLMs) as linear operators within the prompts' semantic task space. This method provides a highly interpretable representation of the models' application and demonstrates competitive or state-of-the-art results in success prediction and model selection tasks.
categories: The approach utilizes closed-form computation of geometrical properties, It represents LLMs as linear operators within the prompts' semantic task space

title: Recent studies have revealed that when LLMs are appropriately prompted and configured, they demonstrate mixed...
abstract: This paper presents PELLI, a comprehensive code quality assessment framework for LLMs. It aims to ensure high-quality code changes and harmonious integration between LLMs and human developers. The study evaluates three primary nonfunctional requirements (maintainability, performance, and reliability) across five popular LLMs in three application domains following Python coding standards. The outcomes reveal that GPT-4T and Gemini slightly outperform other LLMs based on the evaluated nonfunctional requirements. The paper also highlights the importance of prompt design in influencing overall code quality and variations in scores across different metrics and prompts within each application domain.
categories: PELLI is an iterative analysis-based process that generates quantitative metrics for analyzing maintainability, performance, and reliability., The study selects five popular LLMs for evaluation., Three application domains are chosen while following Python coding standards.

title: Post-training compression reduces the computational and memory costs of large language models (LLMs), enabling resource-efficient...
abstract: Post-training compression of large language models (LLMs) is evaluated for its impact on agentic abilities, including workflow generation, tool use/function call, long-context understanding, and real-world application. The study introduces the Agent Compression Benchmark (ACBench), which includes 12 tasks across 4 capabilities, various compression methods, and 15 models.
categories: ACBench consists of:, - 12 tasks spanning 4 agentic capabilities, - Quantization methods (GPTQ, AWQ), - Pruning methods (Wanda, SparseGPT), - 15 models including small, standard, and distilled reasoning LLMs

categories: A novel 12,000-item Q&A benchmark covering 11 ethics and 9 safety dimensions in medical contexts was created to quantitatively evaluate the risks., State-of-the-art Chinese medical LLMs were assessed using this dataset, including Qwen 2.5-32B and DeepSeek., The performance of these models was evaluated, showing moderate baseline performance (accuracy 42.7% for Qwen 2.5-32B) that improved significantly after fine-tuning on the data (up to 50.8% accuracy)., Systemic governance shortfalls were identified that currently hinder safe LLM deployment, such as the lack of fine-grained ethical audit protocols, slow adaptation by hospital IRBs, and insufficient evaluation tools., A practical governance framework for healthcare institutions was proposed to proactively manage LLM risks, including embedding LLM auditing teams, enacting data ethics guidelines, and implementing safety simulation pipelines.

title: Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming integral to daily life, showcasing their vast potential across...
abstract: This work presents GREEN-CODE, a framework designed for energy-efficient code generation in Large Language Models (LLMs). The goal is to reduce the resource intensity of LLM inference during continuous processes, particularly in software development tasks such as code completion, modification, bug fixing, and code translation. The proposed approach uses dynamic early exit during LLM inference and trains a Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent that learns to balance the trade-offs between accuracy, latency, and energy consumption.
categories: Dynamic early exit during LLM inference., Training of a Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent.

title: In environments with sparse or delayed rewards, reinforcement learning (RL) incurs high sample complexity due...
categories: The proposed method constructs a memory graph that encodes subgoals and trajectories from both Large Language Models (LLM) guidance and the agent's own successful rollouts., From this graph, a utility function is derived that evaluates how closely the agent's trajectories align with prior successful strategies., This utility shapes the advantage function, providing the critic with additional guidance without altering the reward., The method primarily relies on offline input and only occasional online queries, avoiding dependence on continuous LLM supervision.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly being integrated into computational social science research, yet their...
abstract: The article discusses the integration of large language models (LLMs) into computational social science research, highlighting unique challenges due to their blackboxed training and stochastic elements. It advocates for explicit assessment of uncertainty in such applications, a requirement long established in both quantitative methodology in social sciences and machine learning. The authors propose a unified framework for evaluating LLM uncertainty across two dimensions: task type (T), distinguishing between classification, short-form, and long-form generation; and validation type (V), capturing the availability of reference data or evaluative criteria. Drawn from both computer science and social science literature, existing uncertainty quantification methods are mapped to this T-V typology, offering practical recommendations for researchers. The framework serves as a methodological safeguard and a guide for integrating LLMs into rigorous social science research.

title: Large language models (LLMs) should undergo rigorous audits to identify potential risks, such as copyright...
categories: Proposal of a toolkit named G-effect to quantify the impacts of unlearning objectives on model performance from a gradient perspective., The G-effect provides detailed insights into the unlearning impacts across instances, updating steps, and LLM layers.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success across various natural language processing (NLP) tasks....
abstract: This paper analyzes the parsing capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in natural language processing tasks, identifying shortcomings in syntactic parsing. The study suggests that LLMs struggle to fully leverage grammar rules in existing treebanks for generating valid syntactic structures. A self-correction method is proposed using grammar rules from these treebanks to guide LLMs in correcting errors without additional training. Experimental results demonstrate improved performance in both in-domain and cross-domain settings on English and Chinese datasets.

title: Large language models (LLMs) are known to produce varying responses depending on prompt phrasing, indicating...
categories: Systematic investigation of how deliberate prompt framing skews model judgments across four high-stakes evaluation tasks. This was achieved by designing symmetric prompts using predicate-positive and predicate-negative constructions, and observing the resulting discrepancies in model outputs., Observation of 14 LLM judges' susceptibility to framing, with model families showing distinct tendencies toward agreement or rejection.

title: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) offer unique opportunities for scientific tasks, yet their...
abstract: The abstract discusses a novel approach to introduce domain-specific physical knowledge into Large Language Models (LLMs) for effective reasoning over complex numerical data in scientific tasks. The method employs a teacher-student knowledge distillation framework, where a large LLM generates synthetic question-answer supervision to transfer physical reasoning to a smaller LLM. The student is conditioned on latent physical features and trained via a lightweight adapter and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The models, tested with 1B, 8B, and 32B parameters, show substantial improvement over strong baselines like Gemini 3 Pro across multiple downstream tasks. The models can predict complex properties, manipulate latent structures through physical manipulation, and interpret latent structures through natural language. While the experiments focus on astrophysics, the framework is domain-agnostic and applicable to various scientific fields.
categories: Teacher-student knowledge distillation, Synthetic question-answer supervision generation by a large LLM (teacher), Conditioning the student on latent physical features, Training the student via a lightweight adapter and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), Downstream tasks without task-specific fine-tuning

The study compared rubric-based grading decisions made by an LLM and TAs with varying levels of expertise. Additionally, the TAs' perceptions of feedback generated by an LLM were examined.

title: Recent advances show that large language models (LLMs) can act as autonomous agents capable of...

title: Many people feel compelled to interpret, describe, and respond to Large Language Models (LLMs) as...
abstract: The abstract discusses the debate about attributing mental lives to Large Language Models (LLMs), with inflationists arguing such ascriptions are warranted and deflationists claiming they are misplaced due to anthropomorphic projections that may lead to misplaced trust or confusion about LLMs' moral status. The paper assesses two common deflationary arguments: the 'robustness strategy' and the 'etiological strategy'. Both strategies offer challenges to inflationism but do not provide a knock-down case against ascriptions of mentality to LLMs simpliciter. A modest form of inflationism is proposed, permitting ascriptions of mental states and capacities (e.g., knowledge, beliefs, desires) under certain conditions, with caution advised when attributing metaphysically demanding phenomena like phenomenal consciousness.
categories: The paper evaluates two common deflationary arguments against LLM mentality: the robustness strategy and the etiological strategy.

title: Large language models (LLMs) have exhibited impressive capabilities in comprehending complex instructions. However, their blind...
abstract: The paper discusses the limitations and challenges of semantic censorship approaches used to filter out problematic responses generated by large language models (LLMs). The authors argue that due to LLMs' programmatic capabilities, semantic censorship can be an undecidable problem, leading to inherent challenges in censorship. They also suggest that knowledgeable attackers can reconstruct impermissible outputs from a collection of permissible ones.
categories: Analysis and discussion on the theoretical limitations of semantic censorship approaches used with LLMs., Demonstration of semantic censorship as an undecidable problem due to LLMs' programmatic capabilities., Argument that the challenges extend beyond semantic censorship, as attackers can reconstruct impermissible outputs from permissible ones.

title: We review discourses about the philosophy of science in qualitative research and evidence from cognitive...
abstract: The abstract presents a framework for discussing the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) in supporting the qualitative analysis process in research. The framework is based on two key questions: 'is the LLM proposing or refuting a qualitative model?' and 'is the human researcher checking the LLM's decision-making directly?'. It suggests that using LLMs to surface counter-examples for human review could be a promising space for adopting these tools into the qualitative research process, as it overlaps with researchers from various philosophical assumptions, enabling cross-paradigm collaboration.
categories: Review of discourses about the philosophy of science in qualitative research, Examination of evidence from cognitive linguistics, Formulation of a framework for discussing LLMs' role in qualitative analysis
